content.text:
Here's the detailed plan. Nothing executed yet — review first.

---

# Plan: Move VM to system-level services with `agnostic-user`

## 1. Goals
- No personal-name user in the VM's active architecture.
- Long-running services that don't need interactive shells run as dedicated **system users** (nologin, no `/home`), matching how `caddy` already runs.
- One login user — **`agnostic-user`** — exists solely to back the browser shells (`ttyd`, `code-server`) and any interactive `gcloud compute ssh`-forwarded Claude Code sessions.
- All code and config that used to live under `/home/condecopedro/` moves to `/opt/iapetus/` (repos) and `/var/lib/<svc>/` (runtime state).
- The *laptop* (macOS) dotfiles workflow is unchanged.
- Existing VM is migrated in place with a rollback path; new VMs bootstrap directly into this layout.

## 2. Target VM state

### Users
| User | Type | Shell | Home | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `agnostic-user` | login | `/bin/zsh` | `/home/agnostic-user` | backs `ttyd`, `code-server`, owns `/opt/iapetus/`, owns interactive dev environment, runs `dotfiles-sync` |
| `alive-svc` | system | `/usr/sbin/nologin` | — | runs `alive-server.service` |
| `openclaw-svc` | system | `/usr/sbin/nologin` | — | runs `openclaw-gateway.service` |
| `caddy` | system | *(existing)* | *(existing)* | unchanged |
| `condecopedro` | *deleted* after migration verified | | | |
| `kerima.weller` | *deleted* immediately (phantom) | | | |

`agnostic-user` gets `/etc/sudoers.d/agnostic-user` with `NOPASSWD: ALL` so browser shells can still `sudo`.

### Filesystem layout
```
/opt/iapetus/                                    agnostic-user:agnostic-user 755
├── attlas/                                      (git clone of TC-AVNP/attlas)
│   ├── base-setup/alive-server/
│   │   └── attlas-server                        (built binary, world-readable)
│   └── diary/public/                            (Hugo output, served by caddy)
└── dotfiels/                                    (git clone of TC-AVNP/dotfiels)
    ├── claude/memory/                           (memory source of truth)
    ├── zsh/.zshrc
    └── systemd/…

/var/lib/alive-server/                           alive-svc:alive-svc 700 (via StateDirectory)
├── .attlas-server-config.json                   (fetched from Secret Manager, 600)
└── .attlas-session-secret                       (auto-generated, 600)

/var/lib/openclaw/                               openclaw-svc:openclaw-svc 700 (via StateDirectory)
└── (whatever .openclaw/ currently contains)

/home/agnostic-user/                             agnostic-user:agnostic-user 750
├── .zshrc            →  /opt/iapetus/dotfiels/zsh/.zshrc
├── .tmux.conf        →  /opt/iapetus/dotfiels/tmux/tmux.conf
├── .claude/
│   ├── settings.json →  /opt/iapetus/dotfiels/claude/settings.json
│   └── projects/
│       └── -opt-iapetus-attlas/
│           └── memory  →  /opt/iapetus/dotfiels/claude/memory
└── (code-server state dir, ssh keys, etc — interactive dev state)
```

### Systemd units (all in `/etc/systemd/system/`)
| Unit | `User=` | `WorkingDirectory` / `ExecStart` base path | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `alive-server.service` | `alive-svc` | `/opt/iapetus/attlas/base-setup/alive-server` | adds `StateDirectory=alive-server`, `Environment=HOME=/var/lib/alive-server` so the binary's hardcoded `~/.attlas-server-config.json` resolves there |
| `openclaw-gateway.service` | `openclaw-svc` | `/usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw` | adds `StateDirectory=openclaw`, `Environment=HOME=/var/lib/openclaw` |
| `ttyd.service` | `agnostic-user` | `/usr/local/bin/ttyd ... /usr/bin/zsh` | user change only |
| `code-server.service` | `agnostic-user` | `/usr/bin/code-server` | user change only |
| `dotfiles-sync.service` | `agnostic-user` | `/opt/iapetus/dotfiels/systemd/dotfiles-sync.sh` | **NEW as system unit** (was user unit). Paired with `dotfiles-sync.timer` |
| `caddy.service` | `caddy` | unchanged | unchanged |

Key point: `alive-server` and `openclaw-gateway` keep their existing binary/config reading logic — I set `HOME` via `Environment=` so they find their config in `/var/lib/<svc>/` instead of rewriting the Go/Node code.

## 3. Repo changes

### `TC-AVNP/attlas`
- **`infra/singlevm-setup/startup.sh`** — rewrite the "create user" section to create `agnostic-user`, `alive-svc`, `openclaw-svc` (no more `condecopedro`). Create `/opt/iapetus/` and `chown agnostic-user:`. Clone attlas to `/opt/iapetus/attlas` as `agnostic-user` (via `sudo -u`). Write sudoers drop-in for `agnostic-user` only.
- **`infra/singlevm-setup/variables.tf`** — remove `vm_user`. Optionally add `agnostic_user_name` (default `"agnostic-user"`) if we want it parameterized.
- **`infra/singlevm-setup/vm.tf`** — update `templatefile` args to match.
- **`infra/singlevm-setup/README.md` + `CLAUDE.md`** — document the new users and `/opt/iapetus/` layout. Add the OS-Login / phantom-user warning (see §6).
- **`base-setup/setup.sh`** — heavily revised. In order:
  1. Install packages (same).
  2. Install Node 24 (same).
  3. Clone dotfiels to `/opt/iapetus/dotfiels` (as `agnostic-user`) instead of `~/dotfiles`.
  4. Run `sudo -u agnostic-user bash /opt/iapetus/dotfiels/install.sh` to set up `agnostic-user`'s home symlinks.
  5. Install Claude Code / Go (same).
  6. Build `alive-server` binary: `sudo -u agnostic-user bash -c 'cd /opt/iapetus/attlas/base-setup/alive-server && go build -o attlas-server .'`
  7. Create `/var/lib/alive-server/` (via a oneshot `systemd-tmpfiles`-style setup, or just `install -d -o alive-svc -g alive-svc -m 700`).
  8. Fetch `attlas-server-config` from Secret Manager and write to `/var/lib/alive-server/.attlas-server-config.json`, chown/chmod.
  9. Write the new `alive-server.service` unit file with `User=alive-svc`, `StateDirectory=alive-server`, `Environment=HOME=/var/lib/alive-server`, WorkingDirectory/ExecStart under `/opt/iapetus/attlas/...`.
  10. `systemctl daemon-reload && systemctl enable --now alive-server`.
  11. Cloudflare DNS update (same).
  12. Install caddy (same).
  13. Install system-level `dotfiles-sync.service`/`.timer` from `/opt/iapetus/dotfiels/systemd/system/` (new dir — see dotfiels section), enable timer.
  14. Prompt to run `services/install.sh`.
- **`services/install-terminal.sh`** — systemd unit `User=agnostic-user`.
- **`services/install-code-server.sh`** — `User=agnostic-user`.
- **`services/install-diary.sh`** — `DIARY_DIR="/opt/iapetus/attlas/diary"`. Build hugo as `agnostic-user` (or root — hugo doesn't care). Output path served by caddy unchanged.
- **`services/uninstall-diary.sh`** — `rm -rf /opt/iapetus/attlas/diary/public`.
- **`services/install-openclaw.sh`** — `User=openclaw-svc`, add `StateDirectory=openclaw`, `Environment=HOME=/var/lib/openclaw`. This one is **the biggest risk** — I need to inspect the current install script to see what state it writes where.
- **All `CLAUDE.md` / `README.md`** — update paths and referenced user.

### `TC-AVNP/dotfiels`
- **`systemd/` directory split**:
  - Move current files to `systemd/user/dotfiles-sync.{service,timer,sh}` — laptop / user-level install path (Linux dev laptops that want the sync).
  - Add `systemd/system/dotfiles-sync.{service,timer}` — the system-level variant with `User=agnostic-user`, `WorkingDirectory=/opt/iapetus/dotfiels`, `ExecStart=/opt/iapetus/dotfiels/systemd/user/dotfiles-sync.sh` (same script, different unit).
  - The `dotfiles-sync.sh` script itself is unchanged — it self-locates.
- **`install.sh`** — add a `SYSTEM_INSTALL` detection or env var:
  - If `$EUID -eq 0` AND Linux → refuse (should not run as root; system install is done by `base-setup/setup.sh`, not by `install.sh`).
  - If `$EUID -ne 0` AND Linux → behaves as today (user install).
  - macOS → unchanged.
  - `CLAUDE_MEMORY_PROJECT_PATHS` on the VM use case: the path passed in is `/opt/iapetus/attlas` (not `$HOME/...`). Since the script computes `encoded=$(echo "$proj_path" | sed 's|[/.]|-|g')`, it already handles absolute paths — no change needed.
  - To support the VM path, **add `/opt/iapetus/attlas` as a second entry** gated on "this directory exists" so the array works for both laptops (`$HOME/iapetus/attlas`) and the VM-agnostic-user install (`/opt/iapetus/attlas`). Or: detect the layout via `$HOME` and pick one.
- **`install-linux.sh`** — drop the user-systemd install from here (it was VM-specific and is now handled by `base-setup/setup.sh`). Keep only the apt-get packages and the agnostic-user home symlinks. If we want linux-laptop support, keep the user systemd install as a fallback — TBD; I'd vote "drop it, the VM does system-level install via base-setup, laptops don't need sync".

## 4. Migration of the **existing** VM (`simple-zombie`)

Broken into phases with explicit rollback points.

### Phase A — prepare (no downtime, fully reversible)
1. Push all repo changes to origin and verify CI/smoke-tests.
2. SSH to VM, backup condecopedro state to `/tmp/condecopedro-backup/`:
   - `.attlas-server-config.json`, `.attlas-session-secret`, `.attlas-services.json`, `.openclaw/`, `.claude/`, `.ssh/`, `attlas/.git/config` (contains the PAT URL).
3. Backup current systemd units to `/tmp/systemd-backup/`.
4. Create new users: `agnostic-user`, `alive-svc`, `openclaw-svc`.
5. Create `/opt/iapetus/` owned by `agnostic-user`.
6. Clone fresh repos (don't `mv` from condecopedro — start clean):
   - `sudo -u agnostic-user git clone ... /opt/iapetus/attlas`
   - `sudo -u agnostic-user git clone ... /opt/iapetus/dotfiels`
7. Build `attlas-server` binary in place as `agnostic-user`.
8. Set up `/var/lib/alive-server/` and `/var/lib/openclaw/` via `install -d` with correct owner/perms.
9. Copy condecopedro's secrets to the new state dirs and chown them appropriately.
10. Run `sudo -u agnostic-user bash /opt/iapetus/dotfiels/install.sh` to populate agnostic-user's home symlinks.

**Checkpoint A**: nothing in production has changed. Old services still running as condecopedro. New tree exists in parallel. Rollback = `rm -rf /opt/iapetus /var/lib/alive-server /var/lib/openclaw`, `userdel -r agnostic-user alive-svc openclaw-svc`.

### Phase B — cut over services (brief downtime)
11. Write new systemd unit files to `/etc/systemd/system/` (over top of existing).
12. `systemctl daemon-reload`.
13. `systemctl restart alive-server.service`. Verify with `systemctl status`, `journalctl -u alive-server -n 50`, and `curl localhost:<port>/...` if possible.
14. `systemctl restart openclaw-gateway.service`. Same verification.
15. `systemctl restart ttyd.service`. Hit `/terminal` in a browser and confirm shell opens as `agnostic-user` (`whoami`).
16. `systemctl restart code-server.service`. Hit `/code`, confirm.
17. Install + start `dotfiles-sync.timer` (system). Run `dotfiles-sync.service` manually once and check the log.
18. Verify Caddy routes still work end-to-end: `/`, `/diary/`, `/terminal`, `/code`, `/openclaw`.

**Checkpoint B**: new services running, old user still exists, old files untouched under `/home/condecopedro/`. Rollback = restore the backed-up systemd units from `/tmp/systemd-backup/`, `daemon-reload`, `restart` — falls back to condecopedro.

### Phase C — clean up (irreversible)
19. `userdel -r kerima.weller` (phantom).
20. Leave `condecopedro` in place for a soak period (configurable — suggested **48h minimum**). Don't delete.
21. After the soak period, after confirming everything on the dashboard still works: `userdel -r condecopedro` and `rm -rf /home/condecopedro`.
22. Remove `/etc/sudoers.d/condecopedro`.

**Checkpoint C**: machine state matches the "new VM" layout exactly.

## 5. Side tasks
- **Phantom-user prevention on `gcloud compute ssh`**: by default, gcloud creates an instance-scoped linux user matching your local username. Fix: **enable OS Login** on the instance (`gcloud compute instances add-metadata simple-zombie --zone=europe-west1-b --metadata enable-oslogin=TRUE`), which uses IAM instead. Or: when SSH'ing in for maintenance, use `gcloud compute ssh agnostic-user@simple-zombie --zone=...` explicitly. I'll add a doc note either way; enabling OS Login is a separate decision.
- **Laptop dotfiles test**: after the dotfiels commit, re-run `bash install.sh` on this Mac to confirm the `$HOME/iapetus/attlas` flow still works for the laptop.

## 6. Risks and unknowns I'll resolve before / during execution
1. **`openclaw-gateway` state**: I don't yet know what's in `.openclaw/`, what files the daemon expects, or whether it tolerates `HOME=/var/lib/openclaw`. **Mitigation**: inspect `install-openclaw.sh` and the openclaw package before Phase B. If it hardcodes a non-`$HOME` path (e.g. `/etc/openclaw/…`), the plan changes slightly. Worst case: openclaw stays as a personal-user service on the first pass and we handle it in a follow-up.
2. **`alive-server` HOME assumption**: I'm assuming the Go binary reads `$HOME/.attlas-server-config.json`. If it reads a hardcoded absolute path, the `Environment=HOME=…` trick doesn't help and we need either a symlink at the hardcoded path or a small code change. **Mitigation**: `strings` the binary before Phase B, or grep the alive-server source.
3. **`code-server` user state loss**: code-server stores extensions / open editors / settings in `$HOME/.local/share/code-server`. The new `agnostic-user` starts empty. Not a correctness issue, but you'll lose your in-browser editor state. I'll `cp -a` that dir across as part of Phase A if you want continuity — flag it in the plan.
4. **Dotfiles-sync auth**: the current dotfiels clone has the PAT embedded in `.git/config` (`https://<PAT>@github.com/...`). That still works for `git pull` as any user. The new clone in Phase A will embed the same PAT for `agnostic-user`. No change needed but worth noting that the PAT is on disk in the clone.
5. **`.zshrc` assumptions**: the dotfiles zshrc sets `PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"` and similar. This is user-agnostic — will work for agnostic-user. I'll skim it once before running.
6. **Services install state file**: `~/.attlas-services.json` tracks which services are installed. Unclear if alive-server or some other component reads it. **Mitigation**: grep the attlas repo and either move it to `/var/lib/alive-server/` too or leave it behind in condecopedro's home during soak.

## 7. Explicit non-goals for this change
- No attlas auto-pull (attlas repo stays manual `git pull`).
- No secret rotation.
- No changes to Caddy config, Cloudflare DNS, or the `attlas.uk` domain.
- No changes to the terraform state backend or IAM policies.
- No renaming of binaries, secrets, or domains.
- Laptop macOS workflow stays exactly as it is today.

## 8. Execution order
Once you approve:
1. Resolve risks #1 and #2 (inspect `install-openclaw.sh`, `alive-server` binary / source) → report findings and adjust the plan if needed → get your re-approval if any assumption breaks.
2. Make attlas repo changes in a branch, commit locally, show the diff.
3. Make dotfiels repo changes in a branch, commit locally, show the diff.
4. On your go, push both.
5. Execute Phase A on the VM.
6. Pause for your confirmation.
7. Execute Phase B on the VM.
8. Pause for your confirmation + soak period.
9. Execute Phase C.

---

**What I need from you before starting:**
- Confirm the user name: `agnostic-user` exactly (hyphen, not underscore)?
- Confirm you want Phase C (deleting `condecopedro`) to happen in this session, or as a follow-up after a real soak period.
- Confirm the code-server state migration: copy `.local/share/code-server` across, or start fresh.
- Confirm whether to enable OS Login on the instance as part of this (recommended but a separate-ish change).
- Any pushback on the `/opt/iapetus/` location? Alternative could be `/srv/iapetus/` — `/srv/` is the FHS-blessed place for "site-specific data served by this system". I'd actually weakly prefer `/srv/iapetus/` over `/opt/iapetus/` if you don't have a preference.
- Anything in the non-goals list you want pulled into scope.

I will not touch the VM until you explicitly approve.

message.content: why /opt/iapetus/ and va/ ?

content.text:
Good pushback. Honest answer: **`/var/lib/<svc>/` is correct and uncontroversial, but `/opt/iapetus/` is a pragmatic choice, not a great one.**

## `/var/lib/<svc>/` — this one is right

FHS (the Filesystem Hierarchy Standard that Linux distros follow) defines `/var/lib/` as "variable state information" — data that services write and persist across reboots, but that isn't config. Think: databases, spool files, auto-generated secrets, session data. This exactly matches what `alive-server` and `openclaw-gateway` need:
- `.attlas-server-config.json` (fetched from Secret Manager, not truly config since it rotates)
- `.attlas-session-secret` (auto-generated at first boot)
- openclaw's `.openclaw/` state

And crucially, **systemd has native support for it**: setting `StateDirectory=alive-server` in the service unit makes systemd auto-create `/var/lib/alive-server/`, chown it to the service user, restrict permissions, and even auto-delete it on uninstall. It's the idiomatic and supported path.

## `/opt/iapetus/` — this one is a compromise

FHS conventions for each candidate:

| Path | FHS definition | Fit for our repo |
|---|---|---|
| `/opt/` | "Add-on application software packages" — traditionally for vendor-shipped tarballs, self-contained | **Weak fit.** We're not installing a package; we're cloning a git repo that's simultaneously source code, build tree, and runtime. Not what `/opt/` was designed for. |
| `/srv/` | "Site-specific data served by this system" | **Partial fit.** `attlas/diary/public/` IS served by caddy, so it fits. But the alive-server source code, Go build tree, and dotfiles aren't "served data" — they're source. Using `/srv/` for the whole repo is a stretch. |
| `/usr/local/` | "Locally installed software by sysadmin" | **Wrong.** This is for built binaries (`/usr/local/bin/`). A git checkout here is unusual. |
| `/var/lib/` | Runtime state | **Wrong.** Source code isn't state. |
| `/home/agnostic-user/iapetus/` | User home | **Actually a strong fit.** See below. |

The awkwardness is that on a single-purpose project VM, the git repo is *both* development workspace *and* the source for the running services — a hybrid role FHS wasn't really designed for. `/opt/` is the closest match among the system-wide options, but it's honestly a stretch.

## The better option: `/home/agnostic-user/iapetus/`

Putting the repos inside `agnostic-user`'s home has real advantages I glossed over in the plan:

1. **Identical layout on laptop and VM.** `$HOME/iapetus/attlas` everywhere. The path the Claude memory symlinks encode to is computed from `$HOME` the same way on both — no special-casing `/opt/…` vs `$HOME/…` in `install.sh`.
2. **No permission contortions.** agnostic-user owns everything in their home. Services (alive-svc, openclaw-svc) still read via POSIX perms as long as the home is `755` or the relevant subdirs are `755`. This is how services like nginx serving `/home/user/public_html/` have worked for decades.
3. **One fewer top-level directory.** Nothing special under `/opt/` or `/srv/`.
4. **Backup is trivially "everything in /home".**
5. **`systemctl enable --now dotfiles-sync.timer` still works as a system unit** — it just needs `User=agnostic-user` and `WorkingDirectory=/home/agnostic-user/iapetus/dotfiels`. Nothing about system-level systemd units requires the thing they manage to live outside `/home`.

The only downside: if you ever `userdel -r agnostic-user`, you lose the repos. But on this VM, agnostic-user is fundamentally baked in (it backs ttyd and code-server — the reason the user exists at all). You're never going to delete it without reprovisioning the whole box anyway. That risk is theoretical.

**Runtime state still goes to `/var/lib/alive-server/` and `/var/lib/openclaw/`** — that's still the right place, because those are owned by *different* system users (`alive-svc`, `openclaw-svc`) that genuinely have no home and shouldn't touch `agnostic-user`'s files.

## What I'd propose instead

Revised layout:

```
/home/agnostic-user/                            agnostic-user:agnostic-user 755
├── iapetus/
│   ├── attlas/                                 (git clone)
│   └── dotfiels/                               (git clone)
├── .zshrc            →  iapetus/dotfiels/zsh/.zshrc
├── .tmux.conf        →  iapetus/dotfiels/tmux/tmux.conf
└── .claude/
    ├── settings.json →  iapetus/dotfiels/claude/settings.json
    └── projects/-home-agnostic-user-iapetus-attlas/memory
                       →  iapetus/dotfiels/claude/memory

/var/lib/alive-server/                          alive-svc:alive-svc 700 (StateDirectory)
/var/lib/openclaw/                              openclaw-svc:openclaw-svc 700 (StateDirectory)
```

Services read source/binaries from `/home/agnostic-user/iapetus/attlas/…` (world-readable or group-readable), write state into their own `/var/lib/<svc>/`. Clean separation, FHS-compliant for the state, idiomatic for the repos.

**Net benefit over the original plan:** the laptop and VM layouts become genuinely identical (modulo the username in the `$HOME` path), which means `install.sh` needs zero special cases for the VM.

Want me to update the plan document with this revision before execution, or are you good with a verbal "yes, use `/home/agnostic-user/iapetus/` instead of `/opt/iapetus/`"?

message.content: i like The better option: /home/agnostic-user/iapetus/

content.text:

Creating tasks for the refactor so progress is trackable.

content.text:
Tasks queued. **Before I start task #6**, I still need answers to the open questions from the end of the plan — I don't want to execute on assumptions after the earlier round of course-corrections. They are:

1. **User name spelling** — `agnostic-user` exactly? (hyphen, lowercase). Or would you prefer `agnostic`, `agnosticuser`, or something else?
2. **Phase C (deleting `condecopedro`)** — run it in this session right after Phase B verification, or defer it as a follow-up after you've soaked the new setup for 24–48h?
3. **code-server state migration** — `code-server` keeps open-editors, installed extensions, and settings in `~/.local/share/code-server/`. Want me to `cp -a` that from `condecopedro` → `agnostic-user` in Phase A so you don't lose your editor state, or start `code-server` fresh under the new user?
4. **OS Login on the instance** — enabling it (`gcloud compute instances add-metadata simple-zombie --metadata enable-oslogin=TRUE`) would prevent `gcloud compute ssh` from auto-creating phantom users keyed to your local username. Recommended, but it's a separate-ish change. Include it, or skip for now?
5. **Non-goals pulled into scope?** — anything from the "explicit non-goals" list (no attlas auto-pull, no secret rotation, no Caddy/Cloudflare/domain changes, no terraform backend changes) that you actually *do* want me to touch while I'm in there?

Once I have those, I'll start with task #6 (risk inspection of `alive-server` and `openclaw`) and report findings before making any repo changes.

message.content: 1. agnostic-user is correct  2. keep condeco pedro, but add a task to delete the vm and recreate it entirely to prove the whole setup is working perfectly. this can be the last step. 3. i have no work open we can lose it its fine, so start fresh.  4. include it but should this end up in terraform? 5. nothing else no.

content.text:
Got all five. Let me react to each and then restructure the task list.

1. **`agnostic-user`** — locked in.
2. **Delete and recreate the VM as the final validation.** This is actually a *big* improvement over my in-place Phase A/B/C plan — it replaces them entirely. Here's why that's better:
   - Zero risk of leftover state contaminating the new layout.
   - It's the ultimate test: if `terraform apply` on a destroyed VM lands in the target state with no manual steps, the new `startup.sh` + `base-setup/setup.sh` are proven correct for any future VM.
   - No rollback plan needed — if anything breaks, we revert the commits and `terraform apply` again.
   - `condecopedro` just disappears with the old VM. No in-place user surgery needed.
   
   So I'm **dropping tasks #9, #10, #11** (Phase A/B/C) and replacing them with two cleaner tasks: terraform destroy+apply, and end-to-end verification.

3. **code-server: start fresh** — noted, nothing to migrate.

4. **OS Login — yes, and yes it should be in terraform.** It belongs on the `google_compute_instance` resource as:
   ```hcl
   metadata = {
     enable-oslogin = "TRUE"
   }
   ```
   That keeps it declarative and reproducible, and avoids the `gcloud compute instances add-metadata` imperative drift.
   
   One subtlety worth flagging: OS Login doesn't make `gcloud compute ssh simple-zombie` log you in *as* `agnostic-user` directly. It makes gcloud authenticate you via your IAM identity, which maps to a predictable username derived from your Google account (something like `condecopedro_gmail_com`). So the "phantom `kerima.weller` per local machine" problem goes away — different laptops with the same Google account now get the *same* VM username. To actually land in `agnostic-user`, you either `sudo -iu agnostic-user` after login, or `gcloud compute ssh agnostic-user@simple-zombie --zone=...` (which works when your IAM user has sudo rights). Either way is fine; the win is "no more phantom users".
   
5. **Nothing else in scope.** Noted.

**Also flagging for confirmation before I act on it:** when I do the terraform recreate, I'll use `terraform taint google_compute_instance.vm` → `terraform apply`, which destroys and recreates **only the VM** while keeping `google_compute_address.vm_static_ip` intact. That preserves the external IP and avoids a Cloudflare DNS change + propagation delay. The alternative (`terraform destroy` + `terraform apply`) releases the static IP and you get a new one — slower, noisier, no benefit. Assuming you want the taint-only approach.

Updating the task list now.

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# OpenClaw — AI agent daemon with Telegram, Brave search, Anthropic
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
OPENCLAW_HOME="$HOME/.openclaw"

# 1. Install OpenClaw
if ! command -v openclaw &>/dev/null; then
  sudo npm install -g openclaw@latest
fi
echo "openclaw: $(openclaw --version 2>&1 | head -1 || echo 'installed')"

# 2. Fetch secrets from GCP Secret Manager
echo "Fetching OpenClaw secrets from Secret Manager..."
SECRETS_FILE=$(mktemp)
gcloud secrets versions access latest --secret=openclaw-config --quiet > "$SECRETS_FILE"

# 3. Build config from template + secrets
echo "Building OpenClaw config..."
mkdir -p "$OPENCLAW_HOME/identity" "$OPENCLAW_HOME/credentials" "$OPENCLAW_HOME/agents/main/agent" "$OPENCLAW_HOME/devices" "$OPENCLAW_HOME/workspace"

python3 - "$SECRETS_FILE" "$SCRIPT_DIR" "$OPENCLAW_HOME" <<'PYEOF'
import json, os, sys

with open(sys.argv[1]) as f:
    secrets = json.load(f)
script_dir = sys.argv[2]
oc_home = sys.argv[3]
# Read template and substitute secrets
with open(os.path.join(script_dir, "openclaw", "config-template.json")) as f:
    config = json.load(f)

config["tools"]["web"]["search"]["apiKey"] = secrets["brave_search_api_key"]
config["channels"]["telegram"]["botToken"] = secrets["telegram_bot_token"]
config["gateway"]["auth"]["token"] = secrets["gateway_auth_token"]
config["agents"]["defaults"]["workspace"] = os.path.join(oc_home, "workspace")

# Resolve external origin for Control UI (Caddy reverse proxy)
config["gateway"]["controlUi"]["allowedOrigins"] = ["https://attlas.uk"]

with open(os.path.join(oc_home, "openclaw.json"), "w") as f:
    json.dump(config, f, indent=2)

# Write identity files
with open(os.path.join(oc_home, "identity", "device.json"), "w") as f:
    json.dump({
        "version": 1,
        "deviceId": secrets["device_id"],
        "publicKeyPem": secrets["device_public_key"],
        "privateKeyPem": secrets["device_private_key"],
        "createdAtMs": 1774199430254
    }, f, indent=2)

with open(os.path.join(oc_home, "identity", "device-auth.json"), "w") as f:
    json.dump({
        "version": 1,
        "deviceId": secrets["device_id"],
        "tokens": {
            "operator": {
                "token": secrets["operator_token"],
                "role": "operator",
                "scopes": ["operator.admin"],
                "updatedAtMs": 1774201373737
            }
        }
    }, f, indent=2)

# Write auth profiles (Anthropic API key)
with open(os.path.join(oc_home, "agents", "main", "agent", "auth-profiles.json"), "w") as f:
    json.dump({
        "version": 1,
        "profiles": {
            "anthropic:default": {
                "type": "api_key",
                "provider": "anthropic",
                "key": secrets["anthropic_api_key"]
            }
        },
        "lastGood": {"anthropic": "anthropic:default"}
    }, f, indent=2)

# Write telegram credentials
with open(os.path.join(oc_home, "credentials", "telegram-pairing.json"), "w") as f:
    json.dump({"version": 1, "requests": []}, f, indent=2)

with open(os.path.join(oc_home, "credentials", "telegram-default-allowFrom.json"), "w") as f:
    json.dump({"version": 1, "allowFrom": secrets["telegram_allow_from"]}, f, indent=2)

# Write paired devices
with open(os.path.join(oc_home, "devices", "paired.json"), "w") as f:
    did = secrets["device_id"]
    json.dump({
        did: {
            "deviceId": did,
            "publicKey": secrets["device_public_key"].split("\\n")[1] if "\\n" in secrets["device_public_key"] else "",
            "platform": "linux",
            "clientId": "gateway-client",
            "clientMode": "ui",
            "role": "operator",
            "roles": ["operator"],
            "scopes": ["operator.read", "operator.admin"],
            "approvedScopes": ["operator.read", "operator.admin"],
            "tokens": {
                "operator": {
                    "token": secrets["operator_token"],
                    "role": "operator",
                    "scopes": ["operator.admin"]
                }
            },
            "displayName": "openclaw-tui"
        }
    }, f, indent=2)

print("Config files written to " + oc_home)
PYEOF

rm -f "$SECRETS_FILE"

# 4. Set permissions
chmod 700 "$OPENCLAW_HOME"
find "$OPENCLAW_HOME/identity" "$OPENCLAW_HOME/credentials" "$OPENCLAW_HOME/agents" -type d -exec chmod 700 {} \;
find "$OPENCLAW_HOME/identity" "$OPENCLAW_HOME/credentials" "$OPENCLAW_HOME/agents" -type f -exec chmod 600 {} \;

# 5. Install and start daemon as system-level service
# Stop user-level service if it exists (migration from old setup)
systemctl --user stop openclaw-gateway 2>/dev/null || true
systemctl --user disable openclaw-gateway 2>/dev/null || true

OPENCLAW_VERSION=$(openclaw --version 2>&1 | grep -oP '[\d.]+' | head -1)
NODE_BIN=$(which node)
OPENCLAW_PKG=$(dirname "$(readlink -f "$(which openclaw)")")
OPENCLAW_JS="${OPENCLAW_PKG}/dist/index.js"

sudo tee /etc/systemd/system/openclaw-gateway.service > /dev/null <<UNIT_EOF
[Unit]
Description=OpenClaw Gateway (v${OPENCLAW_VERSION})
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=$(whoami)
ExecStart=${NODE_BIN} ${OPENCLAW_JS} gateway --port 18789
Restart=always
RestartSec=5
Environment=HOME=/home/$(whoami)
Environment=OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=18789

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
UNIT_EOF

sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl enable --now openclaw-gateway

# 6. Expose dashboard via Caddy
sudo cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/openclaw.caddy" /etc/caddy/conf.d/

echo "openclaw installed and configured"
echo "Check status: openclaw daemon status"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Cloud terminal (ttyd) — web-based terminal with zsh + dotfiles
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"

# Install ttyd
if ! command -v ttyd &>/dev/null; then
  TTYD_VERSION="1.7.7"
  sudo curl -fsSL "https://github.com/tsl0922/ttyd/releases/download/${TTYD_VERSION}/ttyd.x86_64" \
    -o /usr/local/bin/ttyd
  sudo chmod +x /usr/local/bin/ttyd
fi
echo "ttyd: $(ttyd --version 2>&1 | head -1)"

# Create systemd unit
sudo tee /etc/systemd/system/ttyd.service > /dev/null <<EOF
[Unit]
Description=ttyd - Web terminal
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=$(whoami)
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/ttyd --base-path /terminal --port 7681 --writable /usr/bin/zsh
Restart=always
RestartSec=5

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF

# Deploy Caddy route snippet
sudo cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/terminal.caddy" /etc/caddy/conf.d/

# Start
sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl enable --now ttyd

echo "terminal installed -> /terminal (port 7681)"

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Cloud VS Code (code-server) — VS Code in the browser
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"

# Install code-server
if ! command -v code-server &>/dev/null; then
  curl -fsSL https://code-server.dev/install.sh | sh
fi
echo "code-server: $(code-server --version 2>&1 | head -1)"

# Create systemd unit
sudo tee /etc/systemd/system/code-server.service > /dev/null <<EOF
[Unit]
Description=code-server - VS Code in browser
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=$(whoami)
ExecStart=/usr/bin/code-server --bind-addr 127.0.0.1:8080 --auth none --disable-telemetry
Restart=always
RestartSec=5

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF

# Ship default settings (dark theme, disable welcome)
CS_USER_DIR="$HOME/.local/share/code-server/User"
mkdir -p "$CS_USER_DIR"
cat > "$CS_USER_DIR/settings.json" <<'SETTINGS'
{
  "workbench.colorTheme": "Default Dark Modern",
  "workbench.startupEditor": "none"
}
SETTINGS

# Pre-install extensions
code-server --install-extension golang.go 2>/dev/null || true
code-server --install-extension dart-code.flutter 2>/dev/null || true

# Deploy Caddy route snippet
sudo cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/code.caddy" /etc/caddy/conf.d/

# Start
sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl enable --now code-server

echo "code-server installed -> /code (port 8080)"

content.text:
Critical findings from alive-server `main.go`:

**The "just set `Environment=HOME=`" trick is NOT enough.** The binary has 5 uses of `os.UserHomeDir()`:
1. `.attlas-session-secret` — fine, goes to `/var/lib/alive-server/`
2. `.attlas-server-config.json` — fine, goes to `/var/lib/alive-server/`
3. **`.claude.json`** (line 242-243) — reads the interactive user's Claude Code auth file to display "Claude logged in" in the dashboard. With `HOME=/var/lib/alive-server`, this points to the wrong location. Also, Claude's file is `600` owned by the interactive user, so `alive-svc` physically can't read it.
4. `.attlas-services.json` — service install state, can live with alive-svc. OK.
5. **`filepath.Join(home, "attlas")`** (line 774-775) — defines `attlasDir`, which is used to find `services/install-*.sh` (line 656), `services/uninstall-*.sh` (line 687), and `diary/public/` (line 798). With `HOME=/var/lib/alive-server`, this becomes `/var/lib/alive-server/attlas` — which doesn't exist. **Broken.**

**Fix: small code change to main.go** — make the two broken paths configurable via env vars:

```go
// Line 774-775 area
attlasDir = os.Getenv("ATTLAS_DIR")
if attlasDir == "" {
    home, _ := os.UserHomeDir()
    attlasDir = filepath.Join(home, "attlas")  // legacy fallback
}

// Line 242-243 area
claudeJSONPath := os.Getenv("CLAUDE_JSON_PATH")
if claudeJSONPath == "" {
    home, _ := os.UserHomeDir()
    claudeJSONPath = filepath.Join(home, ".claude.json")
}
```

Then the systemd unit sets:
```ini
Environment=HOME=/var/lib/alive-server
Environment=ATTLAS_DIR=/home/agnostic-user/iapetus/attlas
Environment=CLAUDE_JSON_PATH=/home/agnostic-user/.claude.json
```

The `.claude.json` read will still fail (file perms), but the existing code already handles that gracefully (`isClaudeLoggedIn()` returns `false` on read error). Dashboard shows "Claude not logged in" — cosmetic, non-blocking. Can be fixed later by making that file group-readable by an `alive-svc` group.

**Findings on the service install scripts:**

- **`install-terminal.sh`** and **`install-code-server.sh`**: both hardcode `User=$(whoami)` in their systemd unit heredocs, and code-server also writes to `$HOME/.local/share/code-server/User/settings.json` and runs `code-server --install-extension` which touches `$HOME/.local/share/code-server/extensions`. These scripts were designed to be run *as the target service user* from an interactive shell — not as root. They need refactoring to take the service user as a variable and `sudo -u` the user-specific bits.

- **`install-openclaw.sh`**: same story. `OPENCLAW_HOME="$HOME/.openclaw"` (relative to caller's `$HOME`), runs `sudo npm install -g openclaw@latest` (needs root), writes state to `$OPENCLAW_HOME`, writes a systemd unit with `User=$(whoami)` and `Environment=HOME=/home/$(whoami)`. The **good news**: it already knows to set `Environment=HOME=` in the unit, which confirms openclaw itself also uses `$HOME` for its state path (consistent with the `.openclaw/` dir). If we make this script configurable (`SERVICE_USER` / `SERVICE_HOME` env vars with backward-compat defaults), the refactor is mechanical.

Now pulling the live VM state to cross-check against the source scripts.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Base setup — run once after first SSH into a fresh VM.
# Installs packages, dotfiles, Node.js, Claude Code, Go, alive-server, and Caddy gateway.
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"

echo "=== Base setup starting ==="

# 1. Base packages
echo "Installing base packages..."
sudo apt-get update -qq
sudo apt-get install -y -qq zsh tmux python3 curl git build-essential jq

# 2. Node.js 24
if ! command -v node &>/dev/null || [[ "$(node -v)" != v24* ]]; then
  echo "Installing Node.js 24..."
  curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_24.x | sudo bash -
  sudo apt-get install -y -qq nodejs
fi
echo "Node.js: $(node -v)"

# 3. Clone dotfiles into the iapetus workspace
mkdir -p ~/iapetus
if [ ! -d ~/iapetus/dotfiels ]; then
  echo "Cloning dotfiels..."
  PAT=$(gcloud secrets versions access latest --secret=github-pat --quiet)
  git clone "https://${PAT}@github.com/TC-AVNP/dotfiels.git" ~/iapetus/dotfiels
fi

# 4. Run dotfiles installer
echo "Running dotfiles installer..."
cd ~/iapetus/dotfiels && bash install.sh

# 5. Set zsh as default shell
sudo chsh -s /usr/bin/zsh "$(whoami)"
echo "Default shell set to zsh"

# 6. Install Claude Code
if ! command -v claude &>/dev/null; then
  echo "Installing Claude Code..."
  sudo npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
fi

# 7. Install Go
if ! command -v go &>/dev/null; then
  echo "Installing Go..."
  curl -fsSL "https://go.dev/dl/go1.22.5.linux-amd64.tar.gz" | sudo tar -C /usr/local -xzf -
  echo 'export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin' | sudo tee /etc/profile.d/go.sh > /dev/null
  export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin
fi
echo "Go: $(go version)"

# 8. Build alive-server (Go binary)
echo "Building alive-server..."
cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/alive-server"
go build -o attlas-server .
echo "alive-server built"

# 9. Install Caddy
if ! command -v caddy &>/dev/null; then
  echo "Installing Caddy..."
  sudo apt-get install -y -qq debian-keyring debian-archive-keyring apt-transport-https
  curl -1sLf 'https://dl.cloudsmith.io/public/caddy/stable/gpg.key' | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /usr/share/keyrings/caddy-stable-archive-keyring.gpg
  curl -1sLf 'https://dl.cloudsmith.io/public/caddy/stable/debian.deb.txt' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/caddy-stable.list
  sudo apt-get update -qq
  sudo apt-get install -y -qq caddy
fi

# 10. Start alive-server (VM dashboard)
ALIVE_DIR="$SCRIPT_DIR/alive-server"
sudo tee /etc/systemd/system/alive-server.service > /dev/null <<UNIT_EOF
[Unit]
Description=Attlas VM Dashboard
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=$(whoami)
WorkingDirectory=${ALIVE_DIR}
ExecStart=${ALIVE_DIR}/attlas-server
Restart=always
RestartSec=5

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
UNIT_EOF

# Fetch OAuth config for the Go server
echo "Fetching server config from Secret Manager..."
gcloud secrets versions access latest --secret=attlas-server-config --quiet > ~/.attlas-server-config.json 2>/dev/null || true
chmod 600 ~/.attlas-server-config.json

sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl enable --now alive-server

# 11. Update Cloudflare DNS to point attlas.uk to this VM's IP
EXTERNAL_IP=$(curl -sf -H "Metadata-Flavor: Google" http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/instance/network-interfaces/0/access-configs/0/external-ip)
echo "External IP: ${EXTERNAL_IP}"
CF_TOKEN=$(gcloud secrets versions access latest --secret=cloudflare-dns-token --quiet 2>/dev/null || true)
if [ -n "$CF_TOKEN" ]; then
  CF_ZONE="813c7bfa1c9f2b1a02a60c97f3171fa6"
  # Get the A record ID
  RECORD_ID=$(curl -sf "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones/${CF_ZONE}/dns_records?type=A&name=attlas.uk" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${CF_TOKEN}" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['result'][0]['id'])")
  # Update the A record
  curl -sf -X PUT "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones/${CF_ZONE}/dns_records/${RECORD_ID}" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${CF_TOKEN}" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d "{\"type\":\"A\",\"name\":\"attlas.uk\",\"content\":\"${EXTERNAL_IP}\",\"proxied\":false}" > /dev/null
  echo "Cloudflare DNS updated: attlas.uk -> ${EXTERNAL_IP}"
else
  echo "WARNING: cloudflare-dns-token not found in Secret Manager, skipping DNS update"
fi

# 12. Deploy base Caddyfile
CADDY_DOMAIN="attlas.uk"

sudo cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/Caddyfile" /etc/caddy/Caddyfile
sudo mkdir -p /etc/caddy/conf.d
sudo mkdir -p /etc/systemd/system/caddy.service.d
echo "[Service]
Environment=CADDY_DOMAIN=${CADDY_DOMAIN}" | sudo tee /etc/systemd/system/caddy.service.d/override.conf > /dev/null

sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl enable --now caddy
sudo systemctl restart caddy

# 12. Verify dashboard is reachable
echo ""
echo "Verifying dashboard is reachable..."
sleep 5  # give Caddy time to obtain TLS cert
if curl -sf "http://localhost:3000/api/status" -o /dev/null; then
  echo "OK: alive-server responding on localhost:3000"
else
  echo "FAILED: alive-server not responding"
  echo "Check logs: sudo journalctl -u alive-server --no-pager -n 30"
fi

echo ""
echo "=== Base setup complete ==="
echo ""
echo "NOTE: Run 'claude' to log in to Claude Code (requires interactive auth)."
echo ""
if [ -t 0 ]; then
  read -p "Install services now? (y/n) " -n 1 -r
  echo
  if [[ $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
    bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/../services/install.sh"
  fi
else
  echo "Non-interactive mode — skipping services prompt."
  echo "Run ~/iapetus/attlas/services/install.sh to install services."
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Interactive service installer — presents a menu of available services.
# Each service has its own install script in this directory.
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"

# Discover available services (any file matching install-*.sh)
SERVICES=()
DESCRIPTIONS=()
for f in "$SCRIPT_DIR"/install-*.sh; do
  [ -f "$f" ] || continue
  name=$(basename "$f" | sed 's/install-//;s/\.sh//')
  desc=$(head -2 "$f" | grep '^#' | tail -1 | sed 's/^# *//')
  SERVICES+=("$name")
  DESCRIPTIONS+=("$desc")
done

if [ ${#SERVICES[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "No services found (no install-*.sh files in $SCRIPT_DIR)"
  exit 0
fi

echo "=== Available services ==="
echo ""
for i in "${!SERVICES[@]}"; do
  echo "  $((i+1)). ${SERVICES[$i]} — ${DESCRIPTIONS[$i]}"
done
echo ""
echo "  a. Install ALL"
echo "  q. Quit"
echo ""
read -p "Select services to install (e.g. 1 3, or 'a' for all): " -r SELECTION

if [[ "$SELECTION" == "q" ]]; then
  echo "No services installed."
  exit 0
fi

SELECTED=()
if [[ "$SELECTION" == "a" ]]; then
  SELECTED=("${SERVICES[@]}")
else
  for num in $SELECTION; do
    idx=$((num - 1))
    if [ $idx -ge 0 ] && [ $idx -lt ${#SERVICES[@]} ]; then
      SELECTED+=("${SERVICES[$idx]}")
    else
      echo "Invalid selection: $num"
    fi
  done
fi

if [ ${#SELECTED[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "No valid services selected."
  exit 0
fi

echo ""
echo "Installing: ${SELECTED[*]}"
echo ""

for svc in "${SELECTED[@]}"; do
  echo "--- Installing $svc ---"
  bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/install-$svc.sh"
  echo ""
done

# Reload Caddy to pick up any new route snippets
if command -v caddy &>/dev/null; then
  sudo systemctl reload caddy
  echo "Caddy reloaded."
fi

echo ""
echo "=== Done ==="

content.text:
## Inspection summary (the shape of the rewrite)

**Blocker-level findings that change the plan:**

1. **alive-server `main.go` needs two small code changes.** The `Environment=HOME=` trick isn't sufficient because of `attlasDir = filepath.Join(home, "attlas")` at line 775 — that's used to locate `services/install-*.sh`, `services/uninstall-*.sh`, and `diary/public/`. With `HOME=/var/lib/alive-server`, it resolves to nowhere. Needs an `ATTLAS_DIR` env var override. Similarly `.claude.json` (cosmetic "is Claude logged in" check) should take `CLAUDE_JSON_PATH`. Both changes are small and backward-compatible (fallback to `$HOME/attlas` and `$HOME/.claude.json`).

2. **All four `services/install-*.sh` scripts assume they run interactively as the service user.** They use `$(whoami)` for the systemd `User=` and `$HOME` for state paths. They need to accept `SERVICE_USER` (and for openclaw, `SERVICE_HOME`) as parameters, generate the systemd unit with the explicit user, and `sudo -u $SERVICE_USER` the user-specific bits (code-server extensions, settings.json).

3. **`base-setup/setup.sh` needs a full restructure.** Currently runs interactively as the target user. Needs to run as root (or via sudo from someone with passwordless sudo), create users, clone to `/home/agnostic-user/iapetus/`, write configs to `/var/lib/alive-server/`, build alive-server via `sudo -u agnostic-user`, generate the `alive-server.service` unit with new `User=alive-svc` / `Environment=ATTLAS_DIR=…` / `StateDirectory=alive-server`, etc.

4. **`startup.sh`** (templated by Terraform): needs to create all three users (`agnostic-user`, `alive-svc`, `openclaw-svc`) and clone attlas to `/home/agnostic-user/iapetus/attlas`.

5. **`vm.tf`**: add `metadata = { enable-oslogin = "TRUE" }`, drop `vm_user` templatefile arg. **`variables.tf`**: remove `vm_user`.

**Non-blocker findings:**

- Base Caddyfile and conf.d snippets have no user/path references — no changes needed.
- `services/install.sh` (the menu) is generic discovery — no changes needed.
- `openclaw-config` secret gets re-fetched from Secret Manager and the state is regenerated on install. Losing `/home/condecopedro/.openclaw/` (tasks SQLite, telegram pairing, etc.) is fine per your "start fresh" answer. You'll need to re-pair Telegram once after the new VM is up.
- All four required secrets (`attlas-server-config`, `cloudflare-dns-token`, `github-pat`, `openclaw-config`) exist in Secret Manager ✓. Binaries that are installed via package manager (`node`, `caddy`, `code-server`) or one-shot downloads (`hugo`, `ttyd`, `go`, `openclaw` via npm) will reinstall cleanly on a fresh VM.
- `.claude.json` read will fail (perms) after the rewrite — dashboard shows "Claude not logged in" as a cosmetic side effect. Fixable later with an `alive-svc` group or a dedicated status file; out of scope for this pass.

**No surprises** that invalidate the architecture. Proceeding to task #7 (attlas repo rewrite).

Starting with the `alive-server` code changes, since they're the only code change in the pass and I want them out of the way early.

func main() {
	sessionSecret = loadOrCreateSecret()
	oauthConfig = loadOAuthConfig()

	// Resolve paths
	execPath, _ := os.Executable()
	execDir := filepath.Dir(execPath)
	distDir = filepath.Join(execDir, "frontend", "dist")
	home, _ := os.UserHomeDir()
	attlasDir = filepath.Join(home, "attlas")

	if _, err := os.Stat(distDir); err != nil {
		wd, _ := os.Getwd()
		distDir = filepath.Join(wd, "frontend", "dist")
	}

	mux := http.NewServeMux()

	// Auth
	mux.HandleFunc("/api/auth/verify", handleAuthVerify)
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /oauth2/login", handleOAuth2Login)
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /oauth2/callback", handleOAuth2Callback)
	mux.HandleFunc("/logout", handleLogout)

	// API
	mux.HandleFunc("/api/status", handleStatus)
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /api/claude-login", handleClaudeLogin)
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /api/claude-login/code", handleClaudeCode)
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /api/install-service", handleInstallService)
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /api/uninstall-service", handleUninstallService)

	// Diary (Hugo static site)
	diaryDir := filepath.Join(attlasDir, "diary", "public")
	mux.Handle("/diary/", http.StripPrefix("/diary/", http.FileServer(http.Dir(diaryDir))))

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Minimal startup script — runs as root on every boot.
# Only purpose: ensure the attlas repo is cloned for the VM user.
set -euo pipefail
exec > >(tee -a /var/log/startup-script.log) 2>&1
echo "=== startup.sh running at $(date) ==="

VM_USER="${vm_user}"
ATTLAS_REPO="${attlas_repo}"
HOME_DIR="/home/$${VM_USER}"

# 1. Install git if missing
if ! command -v git &>/dev/null; then
  export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
  apt-get update -qq
  apt-get install -y -qq git curl
fi

# 2. Create user if missing
if ! id "$${VM_USER}" &>/dev/null; then
  useradd -m -s /bin/bash "$${VM_USER}"
  usermod -aG sudo "$${VM_USER}"
  echo "$${VM_USER} ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL" > /etc/sudoers.d/$${VM_USER}
  chmod 440 /etc/sudoers.d/$${VM_USER}
fi

# 3. Clone attlas repo into the iapetus workspace if not already cloned.
#    Layout on the VM mirrors local: everything lives under ~/iapetus/
#    (named after Atlas's father — parent directory of attlas).
WORKSPACE_DIR="$${HOME_DIR}/iapetus"
ATTLAS_DIR="$${WORKSPACE_DIR}/attlas"

sudo -u "$${VM_USER}" mkdir -p "$${WORKSPACE_DIR}"

if [ ! -d "$${ATTLAS_DIR}" ]; then
  echo "Cloning attlas repo..."
  # Fetch PAT from Secret Manager
  PAT=$(gcloud secrets versions access latest --secret=github-pat --quiet 2>/dev/null || true)
  if [ -z "$${PAT}" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: Could not fetch github-pat from Secret Manager. Skipping clone."
  else
    REPO_WITH_PAT=$(echo "$${ATTLAS_REPO}" | sed "s|https://|https://$${PAT}@|")
    sudo -u "$${VM_USER}" git clone "$${REPO_WITH_PAT}" "$${ATTLAS_DIR}"
    echo "attlas repo cloned to $${ATTLAS_DIR}"
  fi
else
  echo "attlas repo already exists at $${ATTLAS_DIR}"
fi

echo "=== startup.sh finished at $(date) ==="

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Startup script — runs as root on every boot (via metadata_startup_script).
# Creates the service accounts and clones the attlas repo. All idempotent.
#
# User model:
#   - agnostic-user   login user, NOPASSWD sudo, backs ttyd/code-server and
#                     owns /home/agnostic-user/iapetus/{attlas,dotfiels}.
#   - alive-svc       system user, nologin, runs alive-server.service with
#                     state under /var/lib/alive-server/.
#   - openclaw-svc    system user, nologin, runs openclaw-gateway.service
#                     with state under /var/lib/openclaw/.
#
# Anything that needs packages beyond git (node, caddy, go, hugo, etc.) or
# needs to build and wire up services is deferred to base-setup/setup.sh,
# which an operator runs once after the first boot.
set -euo pipefail
exec > >(tee -a /var/log/startup-script.log) 2>&1
echo "=== startup.sh running at $(date) ==="

ATTLAS_REPO="${attlas_repo}"
AGNOSTIC_USER="agnostic-user"
AGNOSTIC_HOME="/home/$${AGNOSTIC_USER}"
WORKSPACE_DIR="$${AGNOSTIC_HOME}/iapetus"
ATTLAS_DIR="$${WORKSPACE_DIR}/attlas"

# 1. Install git if missing (everything else is base-setup's job)
if ! command -v git &>/dev/null; then
  export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
  apt-get update -qq
  apt-get install -y -qq git curl
fi

# 2. Create the agnostic login user
if ! id "$${AGNOSTIC_USER}" &>/dev/null; then
  echo "Creating login user $${AGNOSTIC_USER}..."
  useradd -m -s /bin/bash "$${AGNOSTIC_USER}"
  usermod -aG sudo "$${AGNOSTIC_USER}"
  echo "$${AGNOSTIC_USER} ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL" > /etc/sudoers.d/$${AGNOSTIC_USER}
  chmod 440 /etc/sudoers.d/$${AGNOSTIC_USER}
fi

# Ensure agnostic-user's home is traversable by services that need to read
# repo files under /home/agnostic-user/iapetus/attlas/.
chmod 755 "$${AGNOSTIC_HOME}"

# 3. Create system users for long-running services (nologin, no home).
#    systemd's StateDirectory= will provision /var/lib/<svc>/ on first start.
for svc_user in alive-svc openclaw-svc; do
  if ! id "$${svc_user}" &>/dev/null; then
    echo "Creating system user $${svc_user}..."
    useradd --system --no-create-home --shell /usr/sbin/nologin "$${svc_user}"
  fi
done

# 4. Clone the attlas repo into the iapetus workspace (as agnostic-user).
sudo -u "$${AGNOSTIC_USER}" mkdir -p "$${WORKSPACE_DIR}"

if [ ! -d "$${ATTLAS_DIR}" ]; then
  echo "Cloning attlas repo..."
  PAT=$(gcloud secrets versions access latest --secret=github-pat --quiet 2>/dev/null || true)
  if [ -z "$${PAT}" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: Could not fetch github-pat from Secret Manager. Skipping clone."
  else
    REPO_WITH_PAT=$(echo "$${ATTLAS_REPO}" | sed "s|https://|https://$${PAT}@|")
    sudo -u "$${AGNOSTIC_USER}" git clone "$${REPO_WITH_PAT}" "$${ATTLAS_DIR}"
    echo "attlas repo cloned to $${ATTLAS_DIR}"
  fi
else
  echo "attlas repo already exists at $${ATTLAS_DIR}"
fi

echo "=== startup.sh finished at $(date) ==="
echo ""
echo "Next step: SSH in and run sudo bash $${ATTLAS_DIR}/base-setup/setup.sh"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Minimal startup script — runs as root on every boot.
# Only purpose: ensure the attlas repo is cloned for the VM user.
set -euo pipefail
exec > >(tee -a /var/log/startup-script.log) 2>&1
echo "=== startup.sh running at $(date) ==="

VM_USER="${vm_user}"
ATTLAS_REPO="${attlas_repo}"
HOME_DIR="/home/$${VM_USER}"

# 1. Install git if missing
if ! command -v git &>/dev/null; then
  export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
  apt-get update -qq
  apt-get install -y -qq git curl
fi

# 2. Create user if missing
if ! id "$${VM_USER}" &>/dev/null; then
  useradd -m -s /bin/bash "$${VM_USER}"
  usermod -aG sudo "$${VM_USER}"
  echo "$${VM_USER} ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL" > /etc/sudoers.d/$${VM_USER}
  chmod 440 /etc/sudoers.d/$${VM_USER}
fi

# 3. Clone attlas repo into the iapetus workspace if not already cloned.
#    Layout on the VM mirrors local: everything lives under ~/iapetus/
#    (named after Atlas's father — parent directory of attlas).
WORKSPACE_DIR="$${HOME_DIR}/iapetus"
ATTLAS_DIR="$${WORKSPACE_DIR}/attlas"

sudo -u "$${VM_USER}" mkdir -p "$${WORKSPACE_DIR}"

if [ ! -d "$${ATTLAS_DIR}" ]; then
  echo "Cloning attlas repo..."
  # Fetch PAT from Secret Manager
  PAT=$(gcloud secrets versions access latest --secret=github-pat --quiet 2>/dev/null || true)
  if [ -z "$${PAT}" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: Could not fetch github-pat from Secret Manager. Skipping clone."
  else
    REPO_WITH_PAT=$(echo "$${ATTLAS_REPO}" | sed "s|https://|https://$${PAT}@|")
    sudo -u "$${VM_USER}" git clone "$${REPO_WITH_PAT}" "$${ATTLAS_DIR}"
    echo "attlas repo cloned to $${ATTLAS_DIR}"
  fi
else
  echo "attlas repo already exists at $${ATTLAS_DIR}"
fi

echo "=== startup.sh finished at $(date) ==="

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Base setup — run ONCE after the first boot of a fresh VM.
# Must be invoked as root (e.g. `sudo bash setup.sh`).
#
# This script assumes startup.sh has already:
#   - Created the login user `agnostic-user` with NOPASSWD sudo
#   - Created the system users `alive-svc` and `openclaw-svc`
#   - Cloned attlas to /home/agnostic-user/iapetus/attlas
#
# Responsibilities of this script:
#   1. Install system packages (zsh, tmux, node, caddy, go, hugo, etc.)
#   2. Clone the dotfiels repo into /home/agnostic-user/iapetus/dotfiels
#      and run its install.sh as agnostic-user (wires home symlinks and
#      installs the system-level dotfiles-sync service).
#   3. Build the alive-server Go binary as agnostic-user.
#   4. Provision /var/lib/alive-server/ and write the OAuth config there.
#   5. Install alive-server.service running as `alive-svc` with the state
#      directory, HOME, ATTLAS_DIR and CLAUDE_JSON_PATH set correctly.
#   6. Install Caddy and drop the base Caddyfile.
#   7. Point Cloudflare DNS at the current VM IP.
#   8. Prompt to install browser services (ttyd, code-server, openclaw,
#      diary), all of which run as `agnostic-user` or `openclaw-svc`.
set -euo pipefail

if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: setup.sh must run as root. Use 'sudo bash setup.sh'." >&2
  exit 1
fi

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"

AGNOSTIC_USER="agnostic-user"
AGNOSTIC_HOME="/home/${AGNOSTIC_USER}"
WORKSPACE_DIR="${AGNOSTIC_HOME}/iapetus"
ATTLAS_DIR="${WORKSPACE_DIR}/attlas"
DOTFIELS_DIR="${WORKSPACE_DIR}/dotfiels"
ALIVE_STATE_DIR="/var/lib/alive-server"

echo "=== Base setup starting (running as root) ==="

# Guard: startup.sh must have run.
if ! id "${AGNOSTIC_USER}" &>/dev/null; then
  echo "ERROR: ${AGNOSTIC_USER} does not exist. Did startup.sh run?" >&2
  exit 1
fi
if [[ ! -d "${ATTLAS_DIR}" ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: ${ATTLAS_DIR} does not exist. Did startup.sh run?" >&2
  exit 1
fi

# 1. Base packages
echo "Installing base packages..."
export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
apt-get update -qq
apt-get install -y -qq zsh tmux python3 curl git build-essential jq

# 2. Node.js 24
if ! command -v node &>/dev/null || [[ "$(node -v)" != v24* ]]; then
  echo "Installing Node.js 24..."
  curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_24.x | bash -
  apt-get install -y -qq nodejs
fi
echo "Node.js: $(node -v)"

# 3. Clone dotfiels (as agnostic-user) into the iapetus workspace
if [[ ! -d "${DOTFIELS_DIR}" ]]; then
  echo "Cloning dotfiels..."
  PAT=$(gcloud secrets versions access latest --secret=github-pat --quiet)
  sudo -u "${AGNOSTIC_USER}" git clone \
    "https://${PAT}@github.com/TC-AVNP/dotfiels.git" "${DOTFIELS_DIR}"
fi

# 4. Run dotfiles installer AS agnostic-user (populates ${AGNOSTIC_HOME}
#    symlinks, installs the system-level dotfiles-sync.timer via sudo).
echo "Running dotfiles installer as ${AGNOSTIC_USER}..."
sudo -u "${AGNOSTIC_USER}" bash "${DOTFIELS_DIR}/install.sh"

# 5. Set zsh as default shell for agnostic-user
chsh -s /usr/bin/zsh "${AGNOSTIC_USER}"
echo "Default shell set to zsh for ${AGNOSTIC_USER}"

# 6. Install Claude Code (global npm, available to any user)
if ! command -v claude &>/dev/null; then
  echo "Installing Claude Code..."
  npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
fi

# 7. Install Go
if ! command -v go &>/dev/null; then
  echo "Installing Go..."
  curl -fsSL "https://go.dev/dl/go1.22.5.linux-amd64.tar.gz" | tar -C /usr/local -xzf -
  echo 'export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin' > /etc/profile.d/go.sh
fi
export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin
echo "Go: $(go version)"

# 8. Build alive-server (Go binary) as agnostic-user so the on-disk
#    permissions match the rest of the repo.
echo "Building alive-server..."
sudo -u "${AGNOSTIC_USER}" bash -c "export PATH=\$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin && cd '${ATTLAS_DIR}/base-setup/alive-server' && go build -o attlas-server ."
echo "alive-server built"

# 9. Provision /var/lib/alive-server/ and seed the OAuth config from
#    Secret Manager. The service itself will have StateDirectory= set so
#    systemd also enforces ownership, but we pre-populate here because
#    the config file must exist before the first service start.
echo "Provisioning ${ALIVE_STATE_DIR}..."
install -d -o alive-svc -g alive-svc -m 700 "${ALIVE_STATE_DIR}"
gcloud secrets versions access latest --secret=attlas-server-config --quiet \
  > "${ALIVE_STATE_DIR}/.attlas-server-config.json"
chown alive-svc:alive-svc "${ALIVE_STATE_DIR}/.attlas-server-config.json"
chmod 600 "${ALIVE_STATE_DIR}/.attlas-server-config.json"

# 10. Install alive-server.service
#     - Runs as the alive-svc system user
#     - HOME=${ALIVE_STATE_DIR} so os.UserHomeDir() resolves config/secret paths
#     - ATTLAS_DIR overrides the baked-in $HOME/attlas assumption
#     - CLAUDE_JSON_PATH points at the interactive user's file (read may fail,
#       handled gracefully by the "is Claude logged in" cosmetic check)
echo "Installing alive-server.service..."
cat > /etc/systemd/system/alive-server.service <<UNIT
[Unit]
Description=Attlas VM Dashboard
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=alive-svc
Group=alive-svc
WorkingDirectory=${ATTLAS_DIR}/base-setup/alive-server
ExecStart=${ATTLAS_DIR}/base-setup/alive-server/attlas-server
Restart=always
RestartSec=5
StateDirectory=alive-server
StateDirectoryMode=0700
Environment=HOME=${ALIVE_STATE_DIR}
Environment=ATTLAS_DIR=${ATTLAS_DIR}
Environment=CLAUDE_JSON_PATH=${AGNOSTIC_HOME}/.claude.json

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
UNIT

systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl enable --now alive-server

# 11. Install Caddy
if ! command -v caddy &>/dev/null; then
  echo "Installing Caddy..."
  apt-get install -y -qq debian-keyring debian-archive-keyring apt-transport-https
  curl -1sLf 'https://dl.cloudsmith.io/public/caddy/stable/gpg.key' | gpg --dearmor -o /usr/share/keyrings/caddy-stable-archive-keyring.gpg
  curl -1sLf 'https://dl.cloudsmith.io/public/caddy/stable/debian.deb.txt' | tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/caddy-stable.list
  apt-get update -qq
  apt-get install -y -qq caddy
fi

# 12. Update Cloudflare DNS to point attlas.uk at the current VM IP
EXTERNAL_IP=$(curl -sf -H "Metadata-Flavor: Google" http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/instance/network-interfaces/0/access-configs/0/external-ip)
echo "External IP: ${EXTERNAL_IP}"
CF_TOKEN=$(gcloud secrets versions access latest --secret=cloudflare-dns-token --quiet 2>/dev/null || true)
if [[ -n "$CF_TOKEN" ]]; then
  CF_ZONE="813c7bfa1c9f2b1a02a60c97f3171fa6"
  RECORD_ID=$(curl -sf "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones/${CF_ZONE}/dns_records?type=A&name=attlas.uk" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${CF_TOKEN}" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['result'][0]['id'])")
  curl -sf -X PUT "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones/${CF_ZONE}/dns_records/${RECORD_ID}" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${CF_TOKEN}" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d "{\"type\":\"A\",\"name\":\"attlas.uk\",\"content\":\"${EXTERNAL_IP}\",\"proxied\":false}" > /dev/null
  echo "Cloudflare DNS updated: attlas.uk -> ${EXTERNAL_IP}"
else
  echo "WARNING: cloudflare-dns-token not found in Secret Manager, skipping DNS update"
fi

# 13. Deploy base Caddyfile
CADDY_DOMAIN="attlas.uk"
cp "${SCRIPT_DIR}/Caddyfile" /etc/caddy/Caddyfile
mkdir -p /etc/caddy/conf.d
mkdir -p /etc/systemd/system/caddy.service.d
cat > /etc/systemd/system/caddy.service.d/override.conf <<OVERRIDE
[Service]
Environment=CADDY_DOMAIN=${CADDY_DOMAIN}
OVERRIDE

systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl enable --now caddy
systemctl restart caddy

# 14. Verify dashboard is reachable
echo ""
echo "Verifying dashboard is reachable..."
sleep 5
if curl -sf "http://localhost:3000/api/status" -o /dev/null; then
  echo "OK: alive-server responding on localhost:3000"
else
  echo "FAILED: alive-server not responding"
  echo "Check logs: journalctl -u alive-server --no-pager -n 30"
fi

echo ""
echo "=== Base setup complete ==="
echo ""
echo "NOTE: Run 'sudo -iu ${AGNOSTIC_USER}' then 'claude' to log in to Claude Code."
echo ""

# 15. Offer to install browser services.
if [ -t 0 ]; then
  read -p "Install services now? (y/n) " -n 1 -r
  echo
  if [[ $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
    bash "${SCRIPT_DIR}/../services/install.sh"
  fi
else
  echo "Non-interactive mode — skipping services prompt."
  echo "Run sudo bash ${ATTLAS_DIR}/services/install.sh to install services."
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Base setup — run once after first SSH into a fresh VM.
# Installs packages, dotfiles, Node.js, Claude Code, Go, alive-server, and Caddy gateway.
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"

echo "=== Base setup starting ==="

# 1. Base packages
echo "Installing base packages..."
sudo apt-get update -qq
sudo apt-get install -y -qq zsh tmux python3 curl git build-essential jq

# 2. Node.js 24
if ! command -v node &>/dev/null || [[ "$(node -v)" != v24* ]]; then
  echo "Installing Node.js 24..."
  curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_24.x | sudo bash -
  sudo apt-get install -y -qq nodejs
fi
echo "Node.js: $(node -v)"

# 3. Clone dotfiles into the iapetus workspace
mkdir -p ~/iapetus
if [ ! -d ~/iapetus/dotfiels ]; then
  echo "Cloning dotfiels..."
  PAT=$(gcloud secrets versions access latest --secret=github-pat --quiet)
  git clone "https://${PAT}@github.com/TC-AVNP/dotfiels.git" ~/iapetus/dotfiels
fi

# 4. Run dotfiles installer
echo "Running dotfiles installer..."
cd ~/iapetus/dotfiels && bash install.sh

# 5. Set zsh as default shell
sudo chsh -s /usr/bin/zsh "$(whoami)"
echo "Default shell set to zsh"

# 6. Install Claude Code
if ! command -v claude &>/dev/null; then
  echo "Installing Claude Code..."
  sudo npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
fi

# 7. Install Go
if ! command -v go &>/dev/null; then
  echo "Installing Go..."
  curl -fsSL "https://go.dev/dl/go1.22.5.linux-amd64.tar.gz" | sudo tar -C /usr/local -xzf -
  echo 'export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin' | sudo tee /etc/profile.d/go.sh > /dev/null
  export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin
fi
echo "Go: $(go version)"

# 8. Build alive-server (Go binary)
echo "Building alive-server..."
cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/alive-server"
go build -o attlas-server .
echo "alive-server built"

# 9. Install Caddy
if ! command -v caddy &>/dev/null; then
  echo "Installing Caddy..."
  sudo apt-get install -y -qq debian-keyring debian-archive-keyring apt-transport-https
  curl -1sLf 'https://dl.cloudsmith.io/public/caddy/stable/gpg.key' | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /usr/share/keyrings/caddy-stable-archive-keyring.gpg
  curl -1sLf 'https://dl.cloudsmith.io/public/caddy/stable/debian.deb.txt' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/caddy-stable.list
  sudo apt-get update -qq
  sudo apt-get install -y -qq caddy
fi

# 10. Start alive-server (VM dashboard)
ALIVE_DIR="$SCRIPT_DIR/alive-server"
sudo tee /etc/systemd/system/alive-server.service > /dev/null <<UNIT_EOF
[Unit]
Description=Attlas VM Dashboard
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=$(whoami)
WorkingDirectory=${ALIVE_DIR}
ExecStart=${ALIVE_DIR}/attlas-server
Restart=always
RestartSec=5

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
UNIT_EOF

# Fetch OAuth config for the Go server
echo "Fetching server config from Secret Manager..."
gcloud secrets versions access latest --secret=attlas-server-config --quiet > ~/.attlas-server-config.json 2>/dev/null || true
chmod 600 ~/.attlas-server-config.json

sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl enable --now alive-server

# 11. Update Cloudflare DNS to point attlas.uk to this VM's IP
EXTERNAL_IP=$(curl -sf -H "Metadata-Flavor: Google" http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/instance/network-interfaces/0/access-configs/0/external-ip)
echo "External IP: ${EXTERNAL_IP}"
CF_TOKEN=$(gcloud secrets versions access latest --secret=cloudflare-dns-token --quiet 2>/dev/null || true)
if [ -n "$CF_TOKEN" ]; then
  CF_ZONE="813c7bfa1c9f2b1a02a60c97f3171fa6"
  # Get the A record ID
  RECORD_ID=$(curl -sf "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones/${CF_ZONE}/dns_records?type=A&name=attlas.uk" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${CF_TOKEN}" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['result'][0]['id'])")
  # Update the A record
  curl -sf -X PUT "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones/${CF_ZONE}/dns_records/${RECORD_ID}" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${CF_TOKEN}" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d "{\"type\":\"A\",\"name\":\"attlas.uk\",\"content\":\"${EXTERNAL_IP}\",\"proxied\":false}" > /dev/null
  echo "Cloudflare DNS updated: attlas.uk -> ${EXTERNAL_IP}"
else
  echo "WARNING: cloudflare-dns-token not found in Secret Manager, skipping DNS update"
fi

# 12. Deploy base Caddyfile
CADDY_DOMAIN="attlas.uk"

sudo cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/Caddyfile" /etc/caddy/Caddyfile
sudo mkdir -p /etc/caddy/conf.d
sudo mkdir -p /etc/systemd/system/caddy.service.d
echo "[Service]
Environment=CADDY_DOMAIN=${CADDY_DOMAIN}" | sudo tee /etc/systemd/system/caddy.service.d/override.conf > /dev/null

sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl enable --now caddy
sudo systemctl restart caddy

# 12. Verify dashboard is reachable
echo ""
echo "Verifying dashboard is reachable..."
sleep 5  # give Caddy time to obtain TLS cert
if curl -sf "http://localhost:3000/api/status" -o /dev/null; then
  echo "OK: alive-server responding on localhost:3000"
else
  echo "FAILED: alive-server not responding"
  echo "Check logs: sudo journalctl -u alive-server --no-pager -n 30"
fi

echo ""
echo "=== Base setup complete ==="
echo ""
echo "NOTE: Run 'claude' to log in to Claude Code (requires interactive auth)."
echo ""
if [ -t 0 ]; then
  read -p "Install services now? (y/n) " -n 1 -r
  echo
  if [[ $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
    bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/../services/install.sh"
  fi
else
  echo "Non-interactive mode — skipping services prompt."
  echo "Run ~/iapetus/attlas/services/install.sh to install services."
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Cloud terminal (ttyd) — web-based terminal with zsh + dotfiles
#
# Must be invoked as root (typically via services/install.sh from
# base-setup/setup.sh). The ttyd service runs as SERVICE_USER (default
# agnostic-user) so browser shells land in the login user's account.
set -euo pipefail

if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: install-terminal.sh must run as root." >&2
  exit 1
fi

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
SERVICE_USER="${SERVICE_USER:-agnostic-user}"

# Install ttyd
if ! command -v ttyd &>/dev/null; then
  TTYD_VERSION="1.7.7"
  curl -fsSL "https://github.com/tsl0922/ttyd/releases/download/${TTYD_VERSION}/ttyd.x86_64" \
    -o /usr/local/bin/ttyd
  chmod +x /usr/local/bin/ttyd
fi
echo "ttyd: $(ttyd --version 2>&1 | head -1)"

# Create systemd unit
cat > /etc/systemd/system/ttyd.service <<UNIT
[Unit]
Description=ttyd - Web terminal
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=${SERVICE_USER}
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/ttyd --base-path /terminal --port 7681 --writable /usr/bin/zsh
Restart=always
RestartSec=5

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
UNIT

# Deploy Caddy route snippet
cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/terminal.caddy" /etc/caddy/conf.d/

# Start
systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl enable --now ttyd

echo "terminal installed -> /terminal (port 7681), running as ${SERVICE_USER}"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Cloud VS Code (code-server) — VS Code in the browser
#
# Must be invoked as root. code-server runs as SERVICE_USER (default
# agnostic-user). User-specific setup (settings, extensions) is performed
# via `sudo -u ${SERVICE_USER}` so the files land in the right home.
set -euo pipefail

if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: install-code-server.sh must run as root." >&2
  exit 1
fi

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
SERVICE_USER="${SERVICE_USER:-agnostic-user}"
SERVICE_HOME="$(getent passwd "${SERVICE_USER}" | cut -d: -f6)"

if [[ -z "${SERVICE_HOME}" || ! -d "${SERVICE_HOME}" ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: home directory for ${SERVICE_USER} not found." >&2
  exit 1
fi

# Install code-server
if ! command -v code-server &>/dev/null; then
  curl -fsSL https://code-server.dev/install.sh | sh
fi
echo "code-server: $(code-server --version 2>&1 | head -1)"

# Create systemd unit
cat > /etc/systemd/system/code-server.service <<UNIT
[Unit]
Description=code-server - VS Code in browser
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=${SERVICE_USER}
ExecStart=/usr/bin/code-server --bind-addr 127.0.0.1:8080 --auth none --disable-telemetry
Restart=always
RestartSec=5

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
UNIT

# Ship default settings (dark theme, disable welcome) as SERVICE_USER
CS_USER_DIR="${SERVICE_HOME}/.local/share/code-server/User"
sudo -u "${SERVICE_USER}" mkdir -p "${CS_USER_DIR}"
sudo -u "${SERVICE_USER}" tee "${CS_USER_DIR}/settings.json" > /dev/null <<'SETTINGS'
{
  "workbench.colorTheme": "Default Dark Modern",
  "workbench.startupEditor": "none"
}
SETTINGS

# Pre-install extensions as SERVICE_USER
sudo -u "${SERVICE_USER}" code-server --install-extension golang.go 2>/dev/null || true
sudo -u "${SERVICE_USER}" code-server --install-extension dart-code.flutter 2>/dev/null || true

# Deploy Caddy route snippet
cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/code.caddy" /etc/caddy/conf.d/

# Start
systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl enable --now code-server

echo "code-server installed -> /code (port 8080), running as ${SERVICE_USER}"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Diary — Hugo-powered project diary served at /diary/
#
# Must be invoked as root. The diary build output lives inside the attlas
# repo under agnostic-user's iapetus workspace; Hugo is invoked as
# SERVICE_USER so the `public/` dir is owned by that user.
set -euo pipefail

if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: install-diary.sh must run as root." >&2
  exit 1
fi

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
SERVICE_USER="${SERVICE_USER:-agnostic-user}"
SERVICE_HOME="$(getent passwd "${SERVICE_USER}" | cut -d: -f6)"
DIARY_DIR="${SERVICE_HOME}/iapetus/attlas/diary"

# Install Hugo
if ! command -v hugo &>/dev/null; then
  echo "Installing Hugo..."
  HUGO_VERSION="0.147.6"
  curl -fsSL "https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/releases/download/v${HUGO_VERSION}/hugo_${HUGO_VERSION}_linux-amd64.tar.gz" \
    -o /tmp/hugo.tar.gz
  tar -C /usr/local/bin -xzf /tmp/hugo.tar.gz hugo
  rm -f /tmp/hugo.tar.gz
fi
echo "hugo: $(hugo version 2>&1 | head -1)"

# Build the site as SERVICE_USER so the output is owned correctly
echo "Building diary site..."
sudo -u "${SERVICE_USER}" bash -c "cd '${DIARY_DIR}' && hugo --baseURL /diary/ --destination public"
echo "Diary built to ${DIARY_DIR}/public"

# Deploy Caddy route snippet
cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/diary.caddy" /etc/caddy/conf.d/

echo "diary installed -> /diary/"
