file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Helper that runs 'claude login' with a PTY and navigates the setup wizard.
Communicates with the dashboard via files in /tmp/.
"""

import pty
import os
import select
import time
import sys
import re
import struct
import fcntl
import termios
import traceback

URL_FILE = "/tmp/claude-login-url"
CODE_FILE = "/tmp/claude-login-code"
RESULT_FILE = "/tmp/claude-login-result"
LOG_FILE = "/tmp/claude-login-helper.log"

log_f = open(LOG_FILE, "w")

def log(msg):
    ts = time.strftime("%H:%M:%S")
    line = f"[{ts}] {msg}"
    log_f.write(line + "\n")
    log_f.flush()

def read_all(fd, timeout=5):
    buf = b""
    deadline = time.time() + timeout
    while time.time() < deadline:
        r, _, _ = select.select([fd], [], [], 0.3)
        if r:
            try:
                buf += os.read(fd, 8192)
            except OSError:
                break
        elif buf:
            break
    return buf

def clean(data):
    return re.sub(rb"\x1b\[[0-9;?]*[a-zA-Z]|\x1b[()][AB012]|\x1b[=>]|\x1b\][^\x07]*\x07", b"", data).decode(errors="replace")

def main():
    # Clean up
    for f in [URL_FILE, CODE_FILE, RESULT_FILE]:
        try: os.remove(f)
        except FileNotFoundError: pass

    master_fd, slave_fd = pty.openpty()
    winsize = struct.pack("HHHH", 30, 120, 0, 0)
    fcntl.ioctl(slave_fd, termios.TIOCSWINSZ, winsize)

    env = os.environ.copy()
    env["BROWSER"] = "echo"
    env["TERM"] = "xterm-256color"
    env["COLUMNS"] = "120"
    env["LINES"] = "30"

    pid = os.fork()
    if pid == 0:
        os.close(master_fd)
        os.setsid()
        fcntl.ioctl(slave_fd, termios.TIOCSCTTY, 0)
        os.dup2(slave_fd, 0)
        os.dup2(slave_fd, 1)
        os.dup2(slave_fd, 2)
        if slave_fd > 2: os.close(slave_fd)
        os.execvpe("claude", ["claude", "login"], env)

    # Parent
    os.close(slave_fd)
    log(f"Started claude login (PID {pid})")

    # Continuously read PTY and navigate the wizard
    # Goal: reach the "paste code" screen
    # Along the way, press Enter on theme picker, login method, etc.
    buf = b""
    entered = set()
    url = None
    triggers = ["theme", "text style", "login method", "select login", "subscription", "claude account"]

    log("Navigating wizard to 'paste code' screen...")

    deadline = time.time() + 90
    while time.time() < deadline:
        data = read_all(master_fd, 3)
        if data:
            buf += data
            log(f"  [{len(buf)} bytes total] last read: {len(data)} bytes")

        text = clean(buf).lower()

        # Check if we reached the code input screen
        if "paste code" in text or ("paste" in text and "code" in text):
            log("  REACHED 'paste code' screen!")
            break

        # Press Enter on any wizard screen we recognize
        matched = False
        for trigger in triggers:
            if trigger in text and trigger not in entered:
                log(f"  found '{trigger}' — waiting 2s then pressing Enter")
                time.sleep(2)
                os.write(master_fd, b"\r")
                entered.add(trigger)
                log(f"  Enter pressed for '{trigger}'")
                time.sleep(3)
                # Read new data and APPEND (don't reset)
                new_data = read_all(master_fd, 5)
                buf += new_data
                log(f"  after Enter: +{len(new_data)} bytes, total {len(buf)}")
                matched = True
                break

        if not matched and not data:
            log(f"  no data, waiting...")
            time.sleep(2)

    # Extract URL from accumulated output
    # The URL may be wrapped across lines in the terminal output
    # Strip all ANSI codes and whitespace, then find the URL
    raw = buf.decode(errors="replace")
    # Remove ANSI escape sequences
    stripped = re.sub(r"\x1b\[[0-9;?]*[a-zA-Z]|\x1b[()][AB012]|\x1b[=>]|\x1b\][^\x07]*\x07", "", raw)
    # Remove carriage returns AND newlines (URL wraps across lines at terminal width)
    stripped = stripped.replace("\r", "").replace("\n", "")

    # Find URL. After stripping ANSI + newlines, the URL runs into the next text.
    # Known trailing text is "Pastecodehereifprompted>" or "Browserdidn'topen..."
    # Simply cut at "Paste" or at the first uppercase letter after "state="
    url_match = re.search(r"(https://claude\.com/[^\s]*|https://console\.anthropic\.com/[^\s]*)", stripped)
    if url_match:
        url = url_match.group(1)
        # Cut at "Paste" if it got concatenated
        for cut_word in ["Paste", "Browser", "Press", "Sign"]:
            idx = url.find(cut_word)
            if idx > 0:
                url = url[:idx]
                break
    log(f"Extracted URL ({len(url) if url else 0} chars): ends with ...{url[-30:] if url else ''}")

    if not url:
        log(f"ERROR: No URL found. Clean text: {clean(buf)[-500:]}")
        with open(RESULT_FILE, "w") as f:
            f.write("ERROR: Could not find auth URL")
        os.kill(pid, 9)
        return

    log(f"URL: {url[:80]}...")
    with open(URL_FILE, "w") as f:
        f.write(url)

    # Wait for code
    log("Waiting for code file...")
    code = None
    for i in range(300):
        if os.path.exists(CODE_FILE):
            with open(CODE_FILE) as f:
                code = f.read().strip()
            if code:
                os.remove(CODE_FILE)
                break
        if i % 30 == 0:
            log(f"  waiting... ({i}s)")
            try: os.kill(pid, 0)
            except ProcessLookupError:
                log("ERROR: Process died")
                with open(RESULT_FILE, "w") as f:
                    f.write("ERROR: claude login exited")
                return
        time.sleep(1)

    if not code:
        log("ERROR: Timed out")
        with open(RESULT_FILE, "w") as f:
            f.write("ERROR: Timed out waiting for code")
        os.kill(pid, 9)
        return

    # Type code
    log(f"Typing code ({len(code)} chars)...")
    for ch in code:
        os.write(master_fd, ch.encode())
        time.sleep(0.01)
    time.sleep(0.5)
    os.write(master_fd, b"\r")
    log("Code submitted, handling post-login prompts...")

    # Handle remaining prompts (security notice, trust folder)
    time.sleep(5)
    post_buf = read_all(master_fd, 10)
    post_text = clean(post_buf).lower()
    log(f"Post-login: {len(post_buf)} bytes")

    if "enter" in post_text or "continue" in post_text:
        log("  pressing Enter (security notice)")
        time.sleep(1)
        os.write(master_fd, b"\r")
        time.sleep(5)
        post_buf = read_all(master_fd, 10)
        post_text = clean(post_buf).lower()

    if "trust" in post_text:
        log("  pressing Enter (trust folder)")
        time.sleep(1)
        os.write(master_fd, b"\r")
        time.sleep(3)

    # Exit
    os.write(master_fd, b"\x03")
    time.sleep(2)

    # Clean up
    try: os.kill(pid, 9)
    except ProcessLookupError: pass
    try: os.close(master_fd)
    except OSError: pass
    try: os.waitpid(pid, 0)
    except ChildProcessError: pass

    # Check auth
    import subprocess
    result = subprocess.run(["claude", "auth", "status"], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5)
    log(f"Auth status: {result.stdout.strip()}")

    if '"loggedIn": true' in result.stdout:
        with open(RESULT_FILE, "w") as f:
            f.write("SUCCESS")
        log("AUTH SUCCEEDED!")
    else:
        with open(RESULT_FILE, "w") as f:
            f.write("FAILED: check log")
        log("AUTH FAILED")

try:
    main()
except Exception as e:
    log(f"FATAL: {e}")
    log(traceback.format_exc())
    with open(RESULT_FILE, "w") as f:
        f.write(f"ERROR: {e}")
finally:
    log_f.close()

content.text:
You're right to call this out. I did **not** actually test the dashboard's view of the service state — I only tested the systemd layer. My verification checked `systemctl is-active`, port listeners, and process users, then reported "verified end-to-end" without ever loading the dashboard. That was incomplete and I should have caught it.

**Here's what actually happened**, tracing through `alive-server/main.go`:

Look at `loadInstalledServices()` around line 271:
```go
func loadInstalledServices() map[string]bool {
    data, err := os.ReadFile(serviceStateFile())
    if err != nil {
        // First run: detect currently installed services by binary
        state := make(map[string]bool)
        for _, svc := range knownServices {
            if _, err := exec.LookPath(svc.Command); err == nil {
                state[svc.ID] = true
            }
        }
        // ... writes state to file
```

Sequence of events on the new VM:
1. `base-setup/setup.sh` built and started `alive-server.service`
2. alive-server's **first-ever run** happened here. At this point, none of the four browser services (ttyd, code-server, openclaw, hugo/diary) were installed yet. `loadInstalledServices()` ran, found zero matching binaries, and persisted `{}` to `/var/lib/alive-server/.attlas-services.json`.
3. *Then* `services/install.sh` ran and installed all four. But the install scripts never updated `.attlas-services.json` — that file is owned `0600 alive-svc:alive-svc` and the installers don't talk to the API, they just write systemd units directly.
4. Subsequent dashboard reads hit the cached `{}` and report "not installed" for everything.

I can see the exact symptom in my own earlier verification output and missed it:
```
-rw-------  1 alive-svc alive-svc    2 Apr 10 13:30 .attlas-services.json
```
**2 bytes** — that's literally `{}`. I should have questioned why that file was empty if the services were installed.

**Immediate fix** (30 seconds):

echo "--- clearing stale state file + restart ---"
sudo rm /var/lib/alive-server/.attlas-services.json
sudo systemctl restart alive-server
sleep 2
echo "--- state file after restart (should have detected binaries) ---"
sudo cat /var/lib/alive-server/.attlas-services.json 2>&1
echo ""
echo "--- /api/status snapshot ---"
curl -s http://localhost:3000/api/status | python3 -m json.tool 2>&1 | head -40
' 2>&1 | tail -60

content.text:
Fixed and confirmed via API. The state file is now:
```json
{"code-server":true,"diary":true,"openclaw":true,"terminal":true}
```

And `/api/status` returns `"installed": true, "running": true` for all four. **Refresh the dashboard** and the four "Install" buttons should switch to whatever the installed-state UI looks like.

**On the testing gap — owning it.**

My task #14 verification was: SSH in, run a script that checks `systemctl is-active`, port listeners, file ownership, dotfile symlinks, and a single `curl https://attlas.uk/` to see the Caddy 302. **I never opened the dashboard or hit `/api/status`.** That's the layer where this bug lives — at the dashboard's "view of the system", not the system itself. The systemd units were genuinely correct; the dashboard's cached opinion of them was wrong.

I should have either (a) loaded the dashboard in a browser and described what I saw, or (b) at minimum hit `curl localhost:3000/api/status` and parsed the JSON. I did neither and that's a real verification gap. Sorry — should have been more thorough, especially after all the moving parts in this refactor.

**Why the bug exists in the first place** is a design choice in alive-server worth fixing properly:

`loadInstalledServices()` (main.go ~line 271) caches its detection in `.attlas-services.json` on first read. The cache is never invalidated when you install or uninstall a service later. The install scripts (`install-*.sh`) write systemd units directly without going through alive-server's API, so alive-server has no idea anything changed. The cache is a premature optimization — `exec.LookPath` is microseconds, there's no reason to persist its result.

**Three places we could fix this for good** (smallest blast radius first):

1. **Add `rm /var/lib/alive-server/.attlas-services.json && systemctl restart alive-server` at the end of `services/install.sh`.** Tiny shell change. Forces re-detection on the next API hit. Doesn't fix the underlying caching problem but eliminates the symptom for the install path.

2. **In `services/install.sh`, after installing services, hit `curl -X POST localhost:3000/api/install-service` for each one.** Goes through alive-server's own install endpoint, which presumably updates the state correctly. Cleaner, but the install endpoint also re-runs the install script — risk of double-install.

3. **Drop the cache entirely from alive-server.** Remove `.attlas-services.json`, make `loadInstalledServices()` always do the `LookPath` walk on every call. Cheapest at runtime, simplest code, makes the dashboard reflect reality rather than a snapshot. **My recommendation.** ~10 lines deleted from main.go.

I'd vote for **#3** as a small follow-up commit. Want me to do it now, or leave it?

---

Now to come back to your **Claude-login-via-dashboard** question, which is what I was mid-investigation on when this came up. Important new finding from reading the code:

**The dashboard already has a Claude login flow.** That "Login to Claude Code" button you see in the screenshot calls `POST /api/claude-login`, which (per `main.go` line 566 and the `claude-login-helper.py` next to the binary):

1. Spawns `claude login` in a PTY as a subprocess
2. Scrapes the OAuth URL from the wizard's terminal output
3. Returns the URL to the dashboard, which presumably shows it to you to click
4. You paste the resulting code back in the dashboard
5. The helper types the code into the PTY, the wizard finishes, and `claude` writes its `.claude.json`

**The problem in the new architecture:** all of this runs **as `alive-svc`** (because alive-server is the parent process). So:
- The PTY subprocess is `alive-svc`'s
- `claude login`'s `os.UserHomeDir()` returns `/var/lib/alive-server/`
- It writes `/var/lib/alive-server/.claude.json` — owned by `alive-svc`, mode 600
- When you open `/terminal` (running as `agnostic-user`) and type `claude`, the CLI looks at `/home/agnostic-user/.claude.json` — **which doesn't exist**
- You're not logged in there

So even if the dashboard login flow technically succeeds, the credentials land in the wrong user's home and `agnostic-user` can't see them.

**Three ways to make dashboard-login → terminal-login work:**

**(A) Run the `claude login` PTY as `agnostic-user`, not `alive-svc`.**
The Python helper currently does `os.execvpe("claude", ["claude", "login"], env)`. We change it to `subprocess.Popen(["sudo", "-u", "agnostic-user", "-H", "claude", "login"], ...)` or fork/exec with `setuid` to agnostic-user before exec. This requires alive-svc to have `sudoers` permission to run `claude` as `agnostic-user` non-interactively (a single line in `/etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc`). The credentials land in `/home/agnostic-user/.claude.json` directly. The dashboard's "is logged in" check then ALSO needs to read that file — fixed by giving it a group ACL or making the file group-readable.

**(B) Write to `alive-svc`'s home, then symlink.**
`/home/agnostic-user/.claude.json` becomes a symlink to `/var/lib/alive-server/.claude.json`. The dashboard naturally reads the file it owns; the CLI in `/terminal` follows the symlink and reads the same content. The catch: `claude` CLI rewrites the file via atomic rename (write to temp, rename over the path), which **destroys** the symlink — first time the user runs `claude` from `/terminal` and it updates auth state, the symlink is gone and the dashboard can't see it anymore. Brittle.

**(C) Shared file via group + ACL.**
Create a `claude-auth` group, add both `agnostic-user` and `alive-svc` to it, store the file at `/home/agnostic-user/.claude.json` mode `660 agnostic-user:claude-auth`, and set a default ACL on the parent so the group is preserved across atomic rewrites. Most Unix-y, but ACLs are subtle and the default-ACL inheritance trick is fragile.

**My recommendation: option (A).** The flow is most natural — the Claude account belongs to the *user* (agnostic-user), not the *dashboard service*, so the credentials should sit in the user's home. alive-svc only needs to *trigger* and *check* the login, not own it. The sudoers rule is one line and tightly scoped:

```
alive-svc ALL=(agnostic-user) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/claude
```

And the "is Claude logged in" check (which is `os.ReadFile`) needs to read `/home/agnostic-user/.claude.json` — for that, either:
- Make the file mode `644` (group-readable and world-readable on a single-user VM is fine; the file contains an OAuth account ID and a refresh token — losing it would be bad, but no one else has shell access on this box anyway), or
- Symlink check via a small status sidecar file that agnostic-user writes after each login.

**Bonus: this also fixes the "VM recreation logs you out" gotcha** if we add one more piece — store the resulting `.claude.json` content as a Secret Manager secret after a successful login, and have `base-setup/setup.sh` fetch it on a fresh VM (just like the OAuth config). Then a `terraform taint + apply` doesn't lose your Claude session, and any new VM in a future multi-VM fleet gets it for free. Optional, but clean.

**Want me to implement (A) + the optional Secret-Manager persistence as a follow-up?** That, plus the cache fix (#3 above), would be one focused commit that hits both gotchas you flagged. Let me know.

content.text:
Got the full picture. Two important findings:

**Finding 1 (in scope):** The cache fix is straightforward — `serviceStateFile()`, `loadInstalledServices()`, `saveInstalledServices()`, `markServiceInstalled()`, and `markServiceUninstalled()` (~45 lines) all collapse into a single function that just walks `LookPath`. The handler call sites at line 674 and 705 also drop their `markService*` calls.

**Finding 2 (out of scope but needs flagging):** The dashboard's `Install` / `Uninstall` buttons are **already broken** in the new architecture, separately from this cache issue. They call `handleInstallService` which does `bash script` as `alive-svc`. The install scripts I rewrote require root (they write to `/etc/systemd/system/`). `alive-svc` isn't root, so clicking "Install" would fail with a sudo password prompt. This was *also* working previously only because `alive-server` ran as `condecopedro` (which had NOPASSWD sudo).

I'll **flag this and not fix it in this commit** — the user can install services via SSH for now (which is what we did in verification). Fixing it requires a separate sudoers drop-in for `alive-svc` to invoke specific install scripts as root, which is a meaningful policy decision worth its own pass. Will mention it in the commit message and ask about it after.

Now executing the changes for the in-scope work: cache drop + Claude login as `agnostic-user`.

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Helper that runs 'claude login' with a PTY and navigates the setup wizard.
Communicates with the dashboard via files in /tmp/.
"""

import pty
import os
import select
import time
import sys
import re
import struct
import fcntl
import termios
import traceback

URL_FILE = "/tmp/claude-login-url"
CODE_FILE = "/tmp/claude-login-code"
RESULT_FILE = "/tmp/claude-login-result"
LOG_FILE = "/tmp/claude-login-helper.log"

log_f = open(LOG_FILE, "w")

def log(msg):
    ts = time.strftime("%H:%M:%S")
    line = f"[{ts}] {msg}"
    log_f.write(line + "\n")
    log_f.flush()

def read_all(fd, timeout=5):
    buf = b""
    deadline = time.time() + timeout
    while time.time() < deadline:
        r, _, _ = select.select([fd], [], [], 0.3)
        if r:
            try:
                buf += os.read(fd, 8192)
            except OSError:
                break
        elif buf:
            break
    return buf

def clean(data):
    return re.sub(rb"\x1b\[[0-9;?]*[a-zA-Z]|\x1b[()][AB012]|\x1b[=>]|\x1b\][^\x07]*\x07", b"", data).decode(errors="replace")

def main():
    # Clean up
    for f in [URL_FILE, CODE_FILE, RESULT_FILE]:
        try: os.remove(f)
        except FileNotFoundError: pass

    master_fd, slave_fd = pty.openpty()
    winsize = struct.pack("HHHH", 30, 120, 0, 0)
    fcntl.ioctl(slave_fd, termios.TIOCSWINSZ, winsize)

    env = os.environ.copy()
    env["BROWSER"] = "echo"
    env["TERM"] = "xterm-256color"
    env["COLUMNS"] = "120"
    env["LINES"] = "30"

    pid = os.fork()
    if pid == 0:
        os.close(master_fd)
        os.setsid()
        fcntl.ioctl(slave_fd, termios.TIOCSCTTY, 0)
        os.dup2(slave_fd, 0)
        os.dup2(slave_fd, 1)
        os.dup2(slave_fd, 2)
        if slave_fd > 2: os.close(slave_fd)
        os.execvpe("claude", ["claude", "login"], env)

    # Parent
    os.close(slave_fd)
    log(f"Started claude login (PID {pid})")

    # Continuously read PTY and navigate the wizard
    # Goal: reach the "paste code" screen
    # Along the way, press Enter on theme picker, login method, etc.
    buf = b""
    entered = set()
    url = None
    triggers = ["theme", "text style", "login method", "select login", "subscription", "claude account"]

    log("Navigating wizard to 'paste code' screen...")

    deadline = time.time() + 90
    while time.time() < deadline:
        data = read_all(master_fd, 3)
        if data:
            buf += data
            log(f"  [{len(buf)} bytes total] last read: {len(data)} bytes")

        text = clean(buf).lower()

        # Check if we reached the code input screen
        if "paste code" in text or ("paste" in text and "code" in text):
            log("  REACHED 'paste code' screen!")
            break

        # Press Enter on any wizard screen we recognize
        matched = False
        for trigger in triggers:
            if trigger in text and trigger not in entered:
                log(f"  found '{trigger}' — waiting 2s then pressing Enter")
                time.sleep(2)
                os.write(master_fd, b"\r")
                entered.add(trigger)
                log(f"  Enter pressed for '{trigger}'")
                time.sleep(3)
                # Read new data and APPEND (don't reset)
                new_data = read_all(master_fd, 5)
                buf += new_data
                log(f"  after Enter: +{len(new_data)} bytes, total {len(buf)}")
                matched = True
                break

        if not matched and not data:
            log(f"  no data, waiting...")
            time.sleep(2)

    # Extract URL from accumulated output
    # The URL may be wrapped across lines in the terminal output
    # Strip all ANSI codes and whitespace, then find the URL
    raw = buf.decode(errors="replace")
    # Remove ANSI escape sequences
    stripped = re.sub(r"\x1b\[[0-9;?]*[a-zA-Z]|\x1b[()][AB012]|\x1b[=>]|\x1b\][^\x07]*\x07", "", raw)
    # Remove carriage returns AND newlines (URL wraps across lines at terminal width)
    stripped = stripped.replace("\r", "").replace("\n", "")

    # Find URL. After stripping ANSI + newlines, the URL runs into the next text.
    # Known trailing text is "Pastecodehereifprompted>" or "Browserdidn'topen..."
    # Simply cut at "Paste" or at the first uppercase letter after "state="
    url_match = re.search(r"(https://claude\.com/[^\s]*|https://console\.anthropic\.com/[^\s]*)", stripped)
    if url_match:
        url = url_match.group(1)
        # Cut at "Paste" if it got concatenated
        for cut_word in ["Paste", "Browser", "Press", "Sign"]:
            idx = url.find(cut_word)
            if idx > 0:
                url = url[:idx]
                break
    log(f"Extracted URL ({len(url) if url else 0} chars): ends with ...{url[-30:] if url else ''}")

    if not url:
        log(f"ERROR: No URL found. Clean text: {clean(buf)[-500:]}")
        with open(RESULT_FILE, "w") as f:
            f.write("ERROR: Could not find auth URL")
        os.kill(pid, 9)
        return

    log(f"URL: {url[:80]}...")
    with open(URL_FILE, "w") as f:
        f.write(url)

    # Wait for code
    log("Waiting for code file...")
    code = None
    for i in range(300):
        if os.path.exists(CODE_FILE):
            with open(CODE_FILE) as f:
                code = f.read().strip()
            if code:
                os.remove(CODE_FILE)
                break
        if i % 30 == 0:
            log(f"  waiting... ({i}s)")
            try: os.kill(pid, 0)
            except ProcessLookupError:
                log("ERROR: Process died")
                with open(RESULT_FILE, "w") as f:
                    f.write("ERROR: claude login exited")
                return
        time.sleep(1)

    if not code:
        log("ERROR: Timed out")
        with open(RESULT_FILE, "w") as f:
            f.write("ERROR: Timed out waiting for code")
        os.kill(pid, 9)
        return

    # Type code
    log(f"Typing code ({len(code)} chars)...")
    for ch in code:
        os.write(master_fd, ch.encode())
        time.sleep(0.01)
    time.sleep(0.5)
    os.write(master_fd, b"\r")
    log("Code submitted, handling post-login prompts...")

    # Handle remaining prompts (security notice, trust folder)
    time.sleep(5)
    post_buf = read_all(master_fd, 10)
    post_text = clean(post_buf).lower()
    log(f"Post-login: {len(post_buf)} bytes")

    if "enter" in post_text or "continue" in post_text:
        log("  pressing Enter (security notice)")
        time.sleep(1)
        os.write(master_fd, b"\r")
        time.sleep(5)
        post_buf = read_all(master_fd, 10)
        post_text = clean(post_buf).lower()

    if "trust" in post_text:
        log("  pressing Enter (trust folder)")
        time.sleep(1)
        os.write(master_fd, b"\r")
        time.sleep(3)

    # Exit
    os.write(master_fd, b"\x03")
    time.sleep(2)

    # Clean up
    try: os.kill(pid, 9)
    except ProcessLookupError: pass
    try: os.close(master_fd)
    except OSError: pass
    try: os.waitpid(pid, 0)
    except ChildProcessError: pass

    # Check auth
    import subprocess
    result = subprocess.run(["claude", "auth", "status"], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5)
    log(f"Auth status: {result.stdout.strip()}")

    if '"loggedIn": true' in result.stdout:
        with open(RESULT_FILE, "w") as f:
            f.write("SUCCESS")
        log("AUTH SUCCEEDED!")
    else:
        with open(RESULT_FILE, "w") as f:
            f.write("FAILED: check log")
        log("AUTH FAILED")

try:
    main()
except Exception as e:
    log(f"FATAL: {e}")
    log(traceback.format_exc())
    with open(RESULT_FILE, "w") as f:
        f.write(f"ERROR: {e}")
finally:
    log_f.close()

+        # -H switches HOME to agnostic-user's home so claude writes its

"""
Helper that runs 'claude login' with a PTY and navigates the setup wizard.
Communicates with the dashboard via files in /tmp/.

import pty
import os
import select
import time
import sys
import re
import struct
import fcntl
import termios
import traceback

URL_FILE = "/tmp/claude-login-url"
CODE_FILE = "/tmp/claude-login-code"
RESULT_FILE = "/tmp/claude-login-result"
LOG_FILE = "/tmp/claude-login-helper.log"

    ts = time.strftime("%H:%M:%S")
    line = f"[{ts}] {msg}"
    log_f.write(line + "\n")
    log_f.flush()

def read_all(fd, timeout=5):
    buf = b""

        r, _, _ = select.select([fd], [], [], 0.3)

def main():

    for f in [URL_FILE, CODE_FILE, RESULT_FILE]:
        try: os.remove(f)
        except FileNotFoundError: pass

    master_fd, slave_fd = pty.openpty()

    fcntl.ioctl(slave_fd, termios.TIOCSWINSZ, winsize)

    env = os.environ.copy()
    env["BROWSER"] = "echo"
    env["TERM"] = "xterm-256color"
    env["COLUMNS"] = "120"
    env["LINES"] = "30"

    pid = os.fork()
    if pid == 0:
        os.close(master_fd)
        os.setsid()
        fcntl.ioctl(slave_fd, termios.TIOCSCTTY, 0)
        os.dup2(slave_fd, 0)
        os.dup2(slave_fd, 1)
        os.dup2(slave_fd, 2)
        if slave_fd > 2: os.close(slave_fd)
        # Run `claude login` as agnostic-user, not as alive-svc, so the
        # resulting .claude.json lands in the interactive user's home and
        # `claude` invoked from /terminal sees the same login.
        # Requires /etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-claude to grant alive-svc

        # -H switches HOME to agnostic-user's home so claude writes its
        # config there. -n forbids password prompts (fail-fast on misconfig).
        os.execvpe(
            "sudo",
            ["sudo", "-n", "-u", "agnostic-user", "-H", "claude", "login"],
            env,
        )

    os.close(slave_fd)
    log(f"Started claude login (PID {pid})")

    # Continuously read PTY and navigate the wizard
    # Goal: reach the "paste code" screen
    # Along the way, press Enter on theme picker, login method, etc.
    buf = b""

    url = None

    while time.time() < deadline:

    raw = buf.decode(errors="replace")
    # Remove ANSI escape sequences

        url = url_match.group(1)

    with open(URL_FILE, "w") as f:
        f.write(url)

    log("Waiting for code file...")
    code = None

            log(f"  waiting... ({i}s)")
            try: os.kill(pid, 0)

input.old_string:
# 10. Install alive-server.service
#     - Runs as the alive-svc system user
#     - HOME=${ALIVE_STATE_DIR} so os.UserHomeDir() resolves config/secret paths
#     - ATTLAS_DIR overrides the baked-in $HOME/attlas assumption
#     - CLAUDE_JSON_PATH points at the interactive user's file (read may fail,
#       handled gracefully by the "is Claude logged in" cosmetic check)
echo "Installing alive-server.service..."
cat > /etc/systemd/system/alive-server.service <<UNIT
[Unit]
Description=Attlas VM Dashboard
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=alive-svc
Group=alive-svc
WorkingDirectory=${ATTLAS_DIR}/base-setup/alive-server
ExecStart=${ATTLAS_DIR}/base-setup/alive-server/attlas-server
Restart=always
RestartSec=5
StateDirectory=alive-server
StateDirectoryMode=0700
Environment=HOME=${ALIVE_STATE_DIR}
Environment=ATTLAS_DIR=${ATTLAS_DIR}
Environment=CLAUDE_JSON_PATH=${AGNOSTIC_HOME}/.claude.json

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
UNIT

systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl enable --now alive-server

echo "Installing alive-server.service..."
cat > /etc/systemd/system/alive-server.service <<UNIT
[Unit]
Description=Attlas VM Dashboard
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple

WorkingDirectory=${ATTLAS_DIR}/base-setup/alive-server
ExecStart=${ATTLAS_DIR}/base-setup/alive-server/attlas-server
Restart=always
RestartSec=5
StateDirectory=alive-server
StateDirectoryMode=0700
Environment=HOME=${ALIVE_STATE_DIR}
Environment=ATTLAS_DIR=${ATTLAS_DIR}

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
UNIT

# Sudoers drop-in: allow alive-svc to invoke `claude` as agnostic-user.
# This is what makes the dashboard's "Login to Claude Code" button write
# to /home/agnostic-user/.claude.json instead of alive-svc's home, so
# the same login is visible from the /terminal browser shell.
CLAUDE_BIN="$(command -v claude || true)"
if [[ -n "${CLAUDE_BIN}" ]]; then
  echo "Installing sudoers drop-in for alive-svc → claude (path: ${CLAUDE_BIN})..."
  cat > /etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-claude <<SUDOERS
# Auto-generated by base-setup/setup.sh — do not edit.
# Allows the dashboard (running as alive-svc) to invoke the Claude Code CLI
# as agnostic-user, both for the login flow and for the "is logged in"
# status check. Tightly scoped to the claude binary.
Defaults:alive-svc env_keep += "BROWSER TERM COLUMNS LINES"
alive-svc ALL=(agnostic-user) NOPASSWD: ${CLAUDE_BIN}
SUDOERS
  chmod 440 /etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-claude
  visudo -c -f /etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-claude
else
  echo "WARNING: claude binary not found on PATH, skipping sudoers drop-in"
  echo "         (dashboard Claude login will be unavailable)"
fi

systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl enable --now alive-server

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Base setup — run ONCE after the first boot of a fresh VM.
# Must be invoked as root (e.g. `sudo bash setup.sh`).
#
# This script assumes startup.sh has already:
#   - Created the login user `agnostic-user` with NOPASSWD sudo
#   - Created the system users `alive-svc` and `openclaw-svc`
#   - Cloned attlas to /home/agnostic-user/iapetus/attlas
#
# Responsibilities of this script:
#   1. Install system packages (zsh, tmux, node, caddy, go, hugo, etc.)
#   2. Clone the dotfiels repo into /home/agnostic-user/iapetus/dotfiels
#      and run its install.sh as agnostic-user (wires home symlinks and
#      installs the system-level dotfiles-sync service).
#   3. Build the alive-server Go binary as agnostic-user.
#   4. Provision /var/lib/alive-server/ and write the OAuth config there.
#   5. Install alive-server.service running as `alive-svc` with the state
#      directory, HOME, ATTLAS_DIR and CLAUDE_JSON_PATH set correctly.
#   6. Install Caddy and drop the base Caddyfile.
#   7. Point Cloudflare DNS at the current VM IP.
#   8. Prompt to install browser services (ttyd, code-server, openclaw,
#      diary), all of which run as `agnostic-user` or `openclaw-svc`.
set -euo pipefail

if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: setup.sh must run as root. Use 'sudo bash setup.sh'." >&2
  exit 1
fi

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"

AGNOSTIC_USER="agnostic-user"
AGNOSTIC_HOME="/home/${AGNOSTIC_USER}"
WORKSPACE_DIR="${AGNOSTIC_HOME}/iapetus"
ATTLAS_DIR="${WORKSPACE_DIR}/attlas"
DOTFIELS_DIR="${WORKSPACE_DIR}/dotfiels"
ALIVE_STATE_DIR="/var/lib/alive-server"

echo "=== Base setup starting (running as root) ==="

# Guard: startup.sh must have run.
if ! id "${AGNOSTIC_USER}" &>/dev/null; then
  echo "ERROR: ${AGNOSTIC_USER} does not exist. Did startup.sh run?" >&2
  exit 1
fi
if [[ ! -d "${ATTLAS_DIR}" ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: ${ATTLAS_DIR} does not exist. Did startup.sh run?" >&2
  exit 1
fi

# 1. Base packages
echo "Installing base packages..."
export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
apt-get update -qq
apt-get install -y -qq zsh tmux python3 curl git build-essential jq

# 2. Node.js 24
if ! command -v node &>/dev/null || [[ "$(node -v)" != v24* ]]; then
  echo "Installing Node.js 24..."
  curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_24.x | bash -
  apt-get install -y -qq nodejs
fi
echo "Node.js: $(node -v)"

# 3. Clone dotfiels (as agnostic-user) into the iapetus workspace
if [[ ! -d "${DOTFIELS_DIR}" ]]; then
  echo "Cloning dotfiels..."
  PAT=$(gcloud secrets versions access latest --secret=github-pat --quiet)
  sudo -u "${AGNOSTIC_USER}" git clone \
    "https://${PAT}@github.com/TC-AVNP/dotfiels.git" "${DOTFIELS_DIR}"
fi

# 4. Run dotfiles installer AS agnostic-user (populates ${AGNOSTIC_HOME}
#    symlinks, installs the system-level dotfiles-sync.timer via sudo).
echo "Running dotfiles installer as ${AGNOSTIC_USER}..."
sudo -u "${AGNOSTIC_USER}" bash "${DOTFIELS_DIR}/install.sh"

# 5. Set zsh as default shell for agnostic-user
chsh -s /usr/bin/zsh "${AGNOSTIC_USER}"
echo "Default shell set to zsh for ${AGNOSTIC_USER}"

# 6. Install Claude Code (global npm, available to any user)
if ! command -v claude &>/dev/null; then
  echo "Installing Claude Code..."
  npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
fi

# 7. Install Go
if ! command -v go &>/dev/null; then
  echo "Installing Go..."
  curl -fsSL "https://go.dev/dl/go1.22.5.linux-amd64.tar.gz" | tar -C /usr/local -xzf -
  echo 'export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin' > /etc/profile.d/go.sh
fi
export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin
echo "Go: $(go version)"

# 8. Build alive-server (Go binary) as agnostic-user so the on-disk
#    permissions match the rest of the repo.
echo "Building alive-server..."
sudo -u "${AGNOSTIC_USER}" bash -c "export PATH=\$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin && cd '${ATTLAS_DIR}/base-setup/alive-server' && go build -o attlas-server ."
echo "alive-server built"

# 9. Provision /var/lib/alive-server/ and seed the OAuth config from
#    Secret Manager. The service itself will have StateDirectory= set so
#    systemd also enforces ownership, but we pre-populate here because
#    the config file must exist before the first service start.
echo "Provisioning ${ALIVE_STATE_DIR}..."
install -d -o alive-svc -g alive-svc -m 700 "${ALIVE_STATE_DIR}"
gcloud secrets versions access latest --secret=attlas-server-config --quiet \
  > "${ALIVE_STATE_DIR}/.attlas-server-config.json"
chown alive-svc:alive-svc "${ALIVE_STATE_DIR}/.attlas-server-config.json"
chmod 600 "${ALIVE_STATE_DIR}/.attlas-server-config.json"

# 10. Install alive-server.service
#     - Runs as the alive-svc system user
#     - HOME=${ALIVE_STATE_DIR} so os.UserHomeDir() resolves config/secret paths
#     - ATTLAS_DIR overrides the baked-in $HOME/attlas assumption
#     - CLAUDE_JSON_PATH points at the interactive user's file (read may fail,
#       handled gracefully by the "is Claude logged in" cosmetic check)
echo "Installing alive-server.service..."
cat > /etc/systemd/system/alive-server.service <<UNIT
[Unit]
Description=Attlas VM Dashboard
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=alive-svc
Group=alive-svc
WorkingDirectory=${ATTLAS_DIR}/base-setup/alive-server
ExecStart=${ATTLAS_DIR}/base-setup/alive-server/attlas-server
Restart=always
RestartSec=5
StateDirectory=alive-server
StateDirectoryMode=0700
Environment=HOME=${ALIVE_STATE_DIR}
Environment=ATTLAS_DIR=${ATTLAS_DIR}
Environment=CLAUDE_JSON_PATH=${AGNOSTIC_HOME}/.claude.json

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
UNIT

systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl enable --now alive-server

# 11. Install Caddy
if ! command -v caddy &>/dev/null; then
  echo "Installing Caddy..."
  apt-get install -y -qq debian-keyring debian-archive-keyring apt-transport-https
  curl -1sLf 'https://dl.cloudsmith.io/public/caddy/stable/gpg.key' | gpg --dearmor -o /usr/share/keyrings/caddy-stable-archive-keyring.gpg
  curl -1sLf 'https://dl.cloudsmith.io/public/caddy/stable/debian.deb.txt' | tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/caddy-stable.list
  apt-get update -qq
  apt-get install -y -qq caddy
fi

# 12. Update Cloudflare DNS to point attlas.uk at the current VM IP
EXTERNAL_IP=$(curl -sf -H "Metadata-Flavor: Google" http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/instance/network-interfaces/0/access-configs/0/external-ip)
echo "External IP: ${EXTERNAL_IP}"
CF_TOKEN=$(gcloud secrets versions access latest --secret=cloudflare-dns-token --quiet 2>/dev/null || true)
if [[ -n "$CF_TOKEN" ]]; then
  CF_ZONE="813c7bfa1c9f2b1a02a60c97f3171fa6"
  RECORD_ID=$(curl -sf "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones/${CF_ZONE}/dns_records?type=A&name=attlas.uk" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${CF_TOKEN}" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['result'][0]['id'])")
  curl -sf -X PUT "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones/${CF_ZONE}/dns_records/${RECORD_ID}" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${CF_TOKEN}" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d "{\"type\":\"A\",\"name\":\"attlas.uk\",\"content\":\"${EXTERNAL_IP}\",\"proxied\":false}" > /dev/null
  echo "Cloudflare DNS updated: attlas.uk -> ${EXTERNAL_IP}"
else
  echo "WARNING: cloudflare-dns-token not found in Secret Manager, skipping DNS update"
fi

# 13. Deploy base Caddyfile
CADDY_DOMAIN="attlas.uk"
cp "${SCRIPT_DIR}/Caddyfile" /etc/caddy/Caddyfile
mkdir -p /etc/caddy/conf.d
mkdir -p /etc/systemd/system/caddy.service.d
cat > /etc/systemd/system/caddy.service.d/override.conf <<OVERRIDE
[Service]
Environment=CADDY_DOMAIN=${CADDY_DOMAIN}
OVERRIDE

systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl enable --now caddy
systemctl restart caddy

# 14. Verify dashboard is reachable
echo ""
echo "Verifying dashboard is reachable..."
sleep 5
if curl -sf "http://localhost:3000/api/status" -o /dev/null; then
  echo "OK: alive-server responding on localhost:3000"
else
  echo "FAILED: alive-server not responding"
  echo "Check logs: journalctl -u alive-server --no-pager -n 30"
fi

echo ""
echo "=== Base setup complete ==="
echo ""
echo "NOTE: Run 'sudo -iu ${AGNOSTIC_USER}' then 'claude' to log in to Claude Code."
echo ""

# 15. Offer to install browser services.
if [ -t 0 ]; then
  read -p "Install services now? (y/n) " -n 1 -r
  echo
  if [[ $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
    bash "${SCRIPT_DIR}/../services/install.sh"
  fi
else
  echo "Non-interactive mode — skipping services prompt."
  echo "Run sudo bash ${ATTLAS_DIR}/services/install.sh to install services."
fi

input.command:
set -e
cd /Users/kerima.weller/iapetus/attlas
git add -A
git status --short
echo "---"
git -c user.name="Pedro Condeço" -c user.email="TC-AVNP@users.noreply.github.com" commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Make dashboard Claude login land in agnostic-user's home

The dashboard's "Login to Claude Code" flow used to spawn `claude
login` as the same user alive-server runs as, which after the
agnostic-user split meant the resulting .claude.json was written to
/var/lib/alive-server/ — invisible to the /terminal browser shell
that runs as agnostic-user. The dashboard's "is logged in" indicator
was also reading the wrong file (and lacked perms to read the right
one), so it permanently displayed "Not authenticated".

Both problems collapse into one fix: have alive-server invoke the
claude CLI as agnostic-user via sudo. The credentials land in the
right home, and the same sudo channel is used for the live status
check, so there's no second source of truth.

  - claude-login-helper.py: spawn claude under
    `sudo -n -u agnostic-user -H` instead of directly. The PTY
    scraping logic is unchanged.
  - main.go: rewrite isClaudeLoggedIn() to call
    `sudo -n -u agnostic-user -H claude auth status` and grep for
    `"loggedIn": true`. Drops the CLAUDE_JSON_PATH env var and the
    .claude.json file read entirely.
  - base-setup/setup.sh: install /etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-claude
    granting alive-svc passwordless sudo to the absolute path of the
    claude binary as agnostic-user, with env_keep for the PTY-related
    vars (BROWSER, TERM, COLUMNS, LINES). Validated with `visudo -c`
    before activation.
  - alive-server.service no longer sets CLAUDE_JSON_PATH (unused).

Drop the .attlas-services.json cache from alive-server

Same commit because it touches the same file. The dashboard used to
cache "which services are installed" in /var/lib/alive-server/
.attlas-services.json on first run, then never re-detect. Installing
services out-of-band (via `bash services/install.sh` from SSH) bypasses
that cache, so the dashboard kept reporting "not installed" even
though every systemd unit was active. This bit us during verification.

Fix: drop the cache entirely. loadInstalledServices() is now a live
exec.LookPath walk over the known-services list — microseconds per
call, no on-disk state, always reflects reality. serviceStateFile(),
saveInstalledServices(), markServiceInstalled(), markServiceUninstalled()
are deleted; the install/uninstall HTTP handlers no longer call them.

Known limitation flagged for a follow-up: the dashboard's Install
and Uninstall buttons themselves still don't work in this architecture
because they exec install-*.sh which now require root, and alive-svc
isn't root. Service installation should continue via SSH for now.
Fixing that properly requires its own scoped sudoers drop-in for
each install script, which is a separate decision.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5
echo "---"
git push origin main 2>&1 | tail -5

set -e

echo "=== 1. pull latest attlas as agnostic-user ==="
sudo -u agnostic-user bash -c "cd /home/agnostic-user/iapetus/attlas && git pull --ff-only origin main"

echo ""
echo "=== 2. rebuild alive-server as agnostic-user ==="
sudo -u agnostic-user bash -c "export PATH=\$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin && cd /home/agnostic-user/iapetus/attlas/base-setup/alive-server && go build -o attlas-server ."

echo ""
echo "=== 3. install sudoers drop-in (claude binary path discovery) ==="

sudo tee /etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-claude > /dev/null <<SUDOERS
# Auto-generated by base-setup/setup.sh — do not edit.
Defaults:alive-svc env_keep += "BROWSER TERM COLUMNS LINES"
alive-svc ALL=(agnostic-user) NOPASSWD: ${CLAUDE_BIN}
SUDOERS
sudo chmod 440 /etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-claude
sudo visudo -c -f /etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-claude

echo ""
echo "=== 4. test sudo as alive-svc → agnostic-user → claude (must not prompt) ==="
sudo -u alive-svc sudo -n -u agnostic-user -H claude auth status 2>&1 | head -5

echo ""
echo "=== 5. restart alive-server with new binary ==="
sudo systemctl restart alive-server
sleep 2

echo ""

curl -s http://localhost:3000/api/status | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(\"claude.authenticated:\",d[\"claude\"][\"authenticated\"]); print(\"services:\"); [print(f\"  {s[\\\"id\\\"]}: installed={s[\\\"installed\\\"]} running={s[\\\"running\\\"]}\") for s in d[\"services\"]]"
' 2>&1 | tail -50

content.text:
All clean. Confirmed:
- File timestamp `13:52:25` is *before* the alive-server restart at `14:01:53` — the file was a leftover from the old binary, not recreated by the new one.
- After deletion, hitting `/api/status` did **not** recreate it. The cache is fully removed.

## What's live on the VM right now

| Concern | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| Service install state | Cached `.attlas-services.json`, never invalidated | Live `exec.LookPath` walk on every API call |
| `isClaudeLoggedIn()` | Read `.claude.json` from alive-svc's home (file didn't exist) → always false | `sudo -n -u agnostic-user -H claude auth status` → reflects reality |
| `Login to Claude Code` button | Spawned `claude login` as alive-svc; credentials landed in `/var/lib/alive-server/.claude.json`; `/terminal` couldn't see them | Spawns `claude login` as agnostic-user via sudo; credentials land in `/home/agnostic-user/.claude.json`; `/terminal` reads the same file naturally |
| `CLAUDE_JSON_PATH` env var | Set on the systemd unit (now-unused) | Removed |
| `/etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-claude` | n/a | `alive-svc ALL=(agnostic-user) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/claude` (validated by `visudo -c`) |

## Test it

Open `https://attlas.uk/` and:
1. Refresh — services should now show **installed**, not "not installed".
2. Click **Login to Claude Code**. The dashboard will show you a Claude OAuth URL. Open it in a new tab, complete the OAuth flow, paste the resulting code back into the dashboard.
3. After it succeeds, refresh the dashboard — `claude.authenticated` should flip to true.
4. Open `https://attlas.uk/terminal`, then `cd ~/iapetus && claude` — should drop you straight into a Claude session with **no login prompt**, because the same `.claude.json` you just wrote is in `agnostic-user`'s home.

If anything in that chain doesn't work, the most likely failure modes are:
- The PTY scraping in `claude-login-helper.py` doesn't recognize a wizard screen (the script has hardcoded triggers like "theme", "login method"). Logs are at `/tmp/claude-login-helper.log` on the VM. If the wizard screens have changed since the helper was written, the URL extraction will time out.
- The sudoers entry path might be wrong if `claude` ever moves. We baked in `/usr/bin/claude` (verified at install time on this VM). If npm changes its install location later, the sudoers path needs to update.

## Two known limitations I want to flag (deliberately not fixing in this commit):

**1. The dashboard's `Install` / `Uninstall` buttons are still broken in this architecture.** I noticed this while reading the code but didn't fix it because it needs its own scoped decision. Reason: those handlers do `bash install-*.sh` as `alive-svc`, but the install scripts I rewrote require root (they write to `/etc/systemd/system/`). The fix is another sudoers drop-in like:
```
alive-svc ALL=(root) NOPASSWD: /bin/bash /home/agnostic-user/iapetus/attlas/services/install-*.sh, /bin/bash /home/agnostic-user/iapetus/attlas/services/uninstall-*.sh
```
…but that grants alive-svc passwordless root for an entire glob of scripts, which is *much* broader trust than the targeted `claude` exception. Probably acceptable on a single-purpose VM but worth a deliberate "yes, do that" from you.

**2. VM recreation still logs you out.** `terraform taint + apply` blows away `/home/agnostic-user/.claude.json` along with the rest of the disk. The bonus fix I mentioned earlier (persist `.claude.json` to a Secret Manager secret after a successful login, fetch on fresh-VM bootstrap) would close that gap. Optional, you didn't ask for it, just keeping it on the table.

Let me know if (1) is something you want me to do now, or wait. And confirm the dashboard login flow actually works when you click the button — that's the one part I can't fully test from here without doing the OAuth dance interactively.

content.text:
Two fixes incoming. First the cwd, then the install button sudoers.

**Fix 1 — `/terminal` lands in `/` instead of `~/iapetus`:**
That's because `ttyd.service` doesn't set `WorkingDirectory=`, so systemd defaults to `/`. The shell ttyd spawns inherits that cwd. Fix: add `WorkingDirectory=%h/iapetus` to the unit (`%h` resolves to the `User=agnostic-user`'s home). Same fix for code-server while I'm at it. (As a side note: the `windows98 in /` prompt is the dotfiles `.zshrc`'s rendering of `$HOST` — your VM's hostname is `windows98` for some reason, probably set by hand at some point. Unrelated to this fix.)

**Fix 2 — Install/Uninstall buttons need sudoers:**
The dashboard's `handleInstallService` does `bash script` as `alive-svc`. The scripts now require root. The fix is two-part:
1. Change the handler to invoke `sudo -n bash script` instead of `bash script`.
2. Add a sudoers drop-in granting `alive-svc` passwordless sudo for the install/uninstall script glob *and* `systemctl reload caddy` (which the handler also calls).

I'll use a wildcard `install-*.sh` pattern in sudoers — slightly broader than enumerating each script, but `agnostic-user` already has full NOPASSWD sudo so the marginal blast radius is zero (anyone who can compromise `agnostic-user` already has root via direct sudo).

Editing now.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Cloud terminal (ttyd) — web-based terminal with zsh + dotfiles
#
# Must be invoked as root (typically via services/install.sh from
# base-setup/setup.sh). The ttyd service runs as SERVICE_USER (default
# agnostic-user) so browser shells land in the login user's account.
set -euo pipefail

if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: install-terminal.sh must run as root." >&2
  exit 1
fi

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
SERVICE_USER="${SERVICE_USER:-agnostic-user}"

# Install ttyd
if ! command -v ttyd &>/dev/null; then
  TTYD_VERSION="1.7.7"
  curl -fsSL "https://github.com/tsl0922/ttyd/releases/download/${TTYD_VERSION}/ttyd.x86_64" \
    -o /usr/local/bin/ttyd
  chmod +x /usr/local/bin/ttyd
fi
echo "ttyd: $(ttyd --version 2>&1 | head -1)"

# Create systemd unit
cat > /etc/systemd/system/ttyd.service <<UNIT
[Unit]
Description=ttyd - Web terminal
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=${SERVICE_USER}
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/ttyd --base-path /terminal --port 7681 --writable /usr/bin/zsh
Restart=always
RestartSec=5

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
UNIT

# Deploy Caddy route snippet
cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/terminal.caddy" /etc/caddy/conf.d/

# Start
systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl enable --now ttyd

echo "terminal installed -> /terminal (port 7681), running as ${SERVICE_USER}"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Cloud VS Code (code-server) — VS Code in the browser
#
# Must be invoked as root. code-server runs as SERVICE_USER (default
# agnostic-user). User-specific setup (settings, extensions) is performed
# via `sudo -u ${SERVICE_USER}` so the files land in the right home.
set -euo pipefail

if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: install-code-server.sh must run as root." >&2
  exit 1
fi

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
SERVICE_USER="${SERVICE_USER:-agnostic-user}"
SERVICE_HOME="$(getent passwd "${SERVICE_USER}" | cut -d: -f6)"

if [[ -z "${SERVICE_HOME}" || ! -d "${SERVICE_HOME}" ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: home directory for ${SERVICE_USER} not found." >&2
  exit 1
fi

# Install code-server
if ! command -v code-server &>/dev/null; then
  curl -fsSL https://code-server.dev/install.sh | sh
fi
echo "code-server: $(code-server --version 2>&1 | head -1)"

# Create systemd unit
cat > /etc/systemd/system/code-server.service <<UNIT
[Unit]
Description=code-server - VS Code in browser
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=${SERVICE_USER}
ExecStart=/usr/bin/code-server --bind-addr 127.0.0.1:8080 --auth none --disable-telemetry
Restart=always
RestartSec=5

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
UNIT

# Ship default settings (dark theme, disable welcome) as SERVICE_USER
CS_USER_DIR="${SERVICE_HOME}/.local/share/code-server/User"
sudo -u "${SERVICE_USER}" mkdir -p "${CS_USER_DIR}"
sudo -u "${SERVICE_USER}" tee "${CS_USER_DIR}/settings.json" > /dev/null <<'SETTINGS'
{
  "workbench.colorTheme": "Default Dark Modern",
  "workbench.startupEditor": "none"
}
SETTINGS

# Pre-install extensions as SERVICE_USER
sudo -u "${SERVICE_USER}" code-server --install-extension golang.go 2>/dev/null || true
sudo -u "${SERVICE_USER}" code-server --install-extension dart-code.flutter 2>/dev/null || true

# Deploy Caddy route snippet
cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/code.caddy" /etc/caddy/conf.d/

# Start
systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl enable --now code-server

echo "code-server installed -> /code (port 8080), running as ${SERVICE_USER}"

input.old_string:
# Sudoers drop-in: allow alive-svc to invoke `claude` as agnostic-user.
# This is what makes the dashboard's "Login to Claude Code" button write
# to /home/agnostic-user/.claude.json instead of alive-svc's home, so
# the same login is visible from the /terminal browser shell.
CLAUDE_BIN="$(command -v claude || true)"
if [[ -n "${CLAUDE_BIN}" ]]; then
  echo "Installing sudoers drop-in for alive-svc → claude (path: ${CLAUDE_BIN})..."
  cat > /etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-claude <<SUDOERS
# Auto-generated by base-setup/setup.sh — do not edit.
# Allows the dashboard (running as alive-svc) to invoke the Claude Code CLI
# as agnostic-user, both for the login flow and for the "is logged in"
# status check. Tightly scoped to the claude binary.
Defaults:alive-svc env_keep += "BROWSER TERM COLUMNS LINES"
alive-svc ALL=(agnostic-user) NOPASSWD: ${CLAUDE_BIN}
SUDOERS
  chmod 440 /etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-claude
  visudo -c -f /etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-claude
else
  echo "WARNING: claude binary not found on PATH, skipping sudoers drop-in"
  echo "         (dashboard Claude login will be unavailable)"
fi
input.new_string:
# Sudoers drop-in: allow alive-svc to invoke `claude` as agnostic-user.
# This is what makes the dashboard's "Login to Claude Code" button write
# to /home/agnostic-user/.claude.json instead of alive-svc's home, so
# the same login is visible from the /terminal browser shell.
CLAUDE_BIN="$(command -v claude || true)"
if [[ -n "${CLAUDE_BIN}" ]]; then
  echo "Installing sudoers drop-in for alive-svc → claude (path: ${CLAUDE_BIN})..."
  cat > /etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-claude <<SUDOERS
# Auto-generated by base-setup/setup.sh — do not edit.
# Allows the dashboard (running as alive-svc) to invoke the Claude Code CLI
# as agnostic-user, both for the login flow and for the "is logged in"
# status check. Tightly scoped to the claude binary.
Defaults:alive-svc env_keep += "BROWSER TERM COLUMNS LINES"
alive-svc ALL=(agnostic-user) NOPASSWD: ${CLAUDE_BIN}
SUDOERS
  chmod 440 /etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-claude
  visudo -c -f /etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-claude
else
  echo "WARNING: claude binary not found on PATH, skipping sudoers drop-in"
  echo "         (dashboard Claude login will be unavailable)"
fi

# Sudoers drop-in: allow alive-svc to install / uninstall services and
# reload caddy. Required because the install-*.sh / uninstall-*.sh scripts
# write to /etc/systemd/system/ and /etc/caddy/conf.d/, and need to reload
# caddy after dropping new route snippets. Without this drop-in the
# dashboard's Install / Uninstall buttons fail with a sudo password prompt.
#
# The wildcard scope (install-*.sh, uninstall-*.sh) is intentional: it's
# automatically extended whenever a new install script is added under
# services/, with no sudoers churn. agnostic-user already has full
# NOPASSWD sudo, so this doesn't widen the trust boundary in practice.
echo "Installing sudoers drop-in for alive-svc → service install/uninstall..."
cat > /etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-services <<SUDOERS
# Auto-generated by base-setup/setup.sh — do not edit.
alive-svc ALL=(root) NOPASSWD: /bin/bash ${ATTLAS_DIR}/services/install-*.sh

# Sudoers drop-in: allow alive-svc to install / uninstall services and
# reload caddy. Required because the install-*.sh / uninstall-*.sh scripts
# write to /etc/systemd/system/ and /etc/caddy/conf.d/, and need to reload
# caddy after dropping new route snippets. Without this drop-in the
# dashboard's Install / Uninstall buttons fail with a sudo password prompt.
#
# The wildcard scope (install-*.sh, uninstall-*.sh) is intentional: it's
# automatically extended whenever a new install script is added under
# services/, with no sudoers churn. agnostic-user already has full
# NOPASSWD sudo, so this doesn't widen the trust boundary in practice.
echo "Installing sudoers drop-in for alive-svc → service install/uninstall..."
cat > /etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-services <<SUDOERS
# Auto-generated by base-setup/setup.sh — do not edit.
alive-svc ALL=(root) NOPASSWD: /bin/bash ${ATTLAS_DIR}/services/install-*.sh
alive-svc ALL=(root) NOPASSWD: /bin/bash ${ATTLAS_DIR}/services/uninstall-*.sh
alive-svc ALL=(root) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/systemctl reload caddy, /bin/systemctl reload caddy
SUDOERS
chmod 440 /etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-services
visudo -c -f /etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-services
toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Base setup — run ONCE after the first boot of a fresh VM.
# Must be invoked as root (e.g. `sudo bash setup.sh`).
#
# This script assumes startup.sh has already:
#   - Created the login user `agnostic-user` with NOPASSWD sudo
#   - Created the system users `alive-svc` and `openclaw-svc`
#   - Cloned attlas to /home/agnostic-user/iapetus/attlas
#
# Responsibilities of this script:
#   1. Install system packages (zsh, tmux, node, caddy, go, hugo, etc.)
#   2. Clone the dotfiels repo into /home/agnostic-user/iapetus/dotfiels
#      and run its install.sh as agnostic-user (wires home symlinks and
#      installs the system-level dotfiles-sync service).
#   3. Build the alive-server Go binary as agnostic-user.
#   4. Provision /var/lib/alive-server/ and write the OAuth config there.
#   5. Install alive-server.service running as `alive-svc` with the state
#      directory, HOME, ATTLAS_DIR and CLAUDE_JSON_PATH set correctly.
#   6. Install Caddy and drop the base Caddyfile.
#   7. Point Cloudflare DNS at the current VM IP.
#   8. Prompt to install browser services (ttyd, code-server, openclaw,
#      diary), all of which run as `agnostic-user` or `openclaw-svc`.
set -euo pipefail

if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: setup.sh must run as root. Use 'sudo bash setup.sh'." >&2
  exit 1
fi

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"

AGNOSTIC_USER="agnostic-user"
AGNOSTIC_HOME="/home/${AGNOSTIC_USER}"
WORKSPACE_DIR="${AGNOSTIC_HOME}/iapetus"
ATTLAS_DIR="${WORKSPACE_DIR}/attlas"
DOTFIELS_DIR="${WORKSPACE_DIR}/dotfiels"
ALIVE_STATE_DIR="/var/lib/alive-server"

echo "=== Base setup starting (running as root) ==="

# Guard: startup.sh must have run.
if ! id "${AGNOSTIC_USER}" &>/dev/null; then
  echo "ERROR: ${AGNOSTIC_USER} does not exist. Did startup.sh run?" >&2
  exit 1
fi
if [[ ! -d "${ATTLAS_DIR}" ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: ${ATTLAS_DIR} does not exist. Did startup.sh run?" >&2
  exit 1
fi

# 1. Base packages
echo "Installing base packages..."
export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
apt-get update -qq
apt-get install -y -qq zsh tmux python3 curl git build-essential jq

# 2. Node.js 24
if ! command -v node &>/dev/null || [[ "$(node -v)" != v24* ]]; then
  echo "Installing Node.js 24..."
  curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_24.x | bash -
  apt-get install -y -qq nodejs
fi
echo "Node.js: $(node -v)"

# 3. Clone dotfiels (as agnostic-user) into the iapetus workspace
if [[ ! -d "${DOTFIELS_DIR}" ]]; then
  echo "Cloning dotfiels..."
  PAT=$(gcloud secrets versions access latest --secret=github-pat --quiet)
  sudo -u "${AGNOSTIC_USER}" git clone \
    "https://${PAT}@github.com/TC-AVNP/dotfiels.git" "${DOTFIELS_DIR}"
fi

# 4. Run dotfiles installer AS agnostic-user (populates ${AGNOSTIC_HOME}
#    symlinks, installs the system-level dotfiles-sync.timer via sudo).
echo "Running dotfiles installer as ${AGNOSTIC_USER}..."
sudo -u "${AGNOSTIC_USER}" bash "${DOTFIELS_DIR}/install.sh"

# 5. Set zsh as default shell for agnostic-user
chsh -s /usr/bin/zsh "${AGNOSTIC_USER}"
echo "Default shell set to zsh for ${AGNOSTIC_USER}"

# 6. Install Claude Code (global npm, available to any user)
if ! command -v claude &>/dev/null; then
  echo "Installing Claude Code..."
  npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
fi

# 7. Install Go
if ! command -v go &>/dev/null; then
  echo "Installing Go..."
  curl -fsSL "https://go.dev/dl/go1.22.5.linux-amd64.tar.gz" | tar -C /usr/local -xzf -
  echo 'export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin' > /etc/profile.d/go.sh
fi
export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin
echo "Go: $(go version)"

# 8. Build alive-server (Go binary) as agnostic-user so the on-disk
#    permissions match the rest of the repo.
echo "Building alive-server..."
sudo -u "${AGNOSTIC_USER}" bash -c "export PATH=\$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin && cd '${ATTLAS_DIR}/base-setup/alive-server' && go build -o attlas-server ."
echo "alive-server built"

# 9. Provision /var/lib/alive-server/ and seed the OAuth config from
#    Secret Manager. The service itself will have StateDirectory= set so
#    systemd also enforces ownership, but we pre-populate here because
#    the config file must exist before the first service start.
echo "Provisioning ${ALIVE_STATE_DIR}..."
install -d -o alive-svc -g alive-svc -m 700 "${ALIVE_STATE_DIR}"
gcloud secrets versions access latest --secret=attlas-server-config --quiet \
  > "${ALIVE_STATE_DIR}/.attlas-server-config.json"
chown alive-svc:alive-svc "${ALIVE_STATE_DIR}/.attlas-server-config.json"
chmod 600 "${ALIVE_STATE_DIR}/.attlas-server-config.json"

# 10. Install alive-server.service
#     - Runs as the alive-svc system user
#     - HOME=${ALIVE_STATE_DIR} so os.UserHomeDir() resolves config/secret paths
#     - ATTLAS_DIR overrides the baked-in $HOME/attlas assumption
echo "Installing alive-server.service..."
cat > /etc/systemd/system/alive-server.service <<UNIT
[Unit]
Description=Attlas VM Dashboard
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=alive-svc
Group=alive-svc
WorkingDirectory=${ATTLAS_DIR}/base-setup/alive-server
ExecStart=${ATTLAS_DIR}/base-setup/alive-server/attlas-server
Restart=always
RestartSec=5
StateDirectory=alive-server
StateDirectoryMode=0700
Environment=HOME=${ALIVE_STATE_DIR}
Environment=ATTLAS_DIR=${ATTLAS_DIR}

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
UNIT

# Sudoers drop-in: allow alive-svc to invoke `claude` as agnostic-user.
# This is what makes the dashboard's "Login to Claude Code" button write
# to /home/agnostic-user/.claude.json instead of alive-svc's home, so
# the same login is visible from the /terminal browser shell.
CLAUDE_BIN="$(command -v claude || true)"
if [[ -n "${CLAUDE_BIN}" ]]; then
  echo "Installing sudoers drop-in for alive-svc → claude (path: ${CLAUDE_BIN})..."
  cat > /etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-claude <<SUDOERS
# Auto-generated by base-setup/setup.sh — do not edit.
# Allows the dashboard (running as alive-svc) to invoke the Claude Code CLI
# as agnostic-user, both for the login flow and for the "is logged in"
# status check. Tightly scoped to the claude binary.
Defaults:alive-svc env_keep += "BROWSER TERM COLUMNS LINES"
alive-svc ALL=(agnostic-user) NOPASSWD: ${CLAUDE_BIN}
SUDOERS
  chmod 440 /etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-claude
  visudo -c -f /etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-claude
else
  echo "WARNING: claude binary not found on PATH, skipping sudoers drop-in"
  echo "         (dashboard Claude login will be unavailable)"
fi

systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl enable --now alive-server

# 11. Install Caddy
if ! command -v caddy &>/dev/null; then
  echo "Installing Caddy..."
  apt-get install -y -qq debian-keyring debian-archive-keyring apt-transport-https
  curl -1sLf 'https://dl.cloudsmith.io/public/caddy/stable/gpg.key' | gpg --dearmor -o /usr/share/keyrings/caddy-stable-archive-keyring.gpg
  curl -1sLf 'https://dl.cloudsmith.io/public/caddy/stable/debian.deb.txt' | tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/caddy-stable.list
  apt-get update -qq
  apt-get install -y -qq caddy
fi

# 12. Update Cloudflare DNS to point attlas.uk at the current VM IP
EXTERNAL_IP=$(curl -sf -H "Metadata-Flavor: Google" http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/instance/network-interfaces/0/access-configs/0/external-ip)
echo "External IP: ${EXTERNAL_IP}"
CF_TOKEN=$(gcloud secrets versions access latest --secret=cloudflare-dns-token --quiet 2>/dev/null || true)
if [[ -n "$CF_TOKEN" ]]; then
  CF_ZONE="813c7bfa1c9f2b1a02a60c97f3171fa6"
  RECORD_ID=$(curl -sf "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones/${CF_ZONE}/dns_records?type=A&name=attlas.uk" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${CF_TOKEN}" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['result'][0]['id'])")
  curl -sf -X PUT "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones/${CF_ZONE}/dns_records/${RECORD_ID}" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${CF_TOKEN}" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d "{\"type\":\"A\",\"name\":\"attlas.uk\",\"content\":\"${EXTERNAL_IP}\",\"proxied\":false}" > /dev/null
  echo "Cloudflare DNS updated: attlas.uk -> ${EXTERNAL_IP}"
else
  echo "WARNING: cloudflare-dns-token not found in Secret Manager, skipping DNS update"
fi

# 13. Deploy base Caddyfile
CADDY_DOMAIN="attlas.uk"
cp "${SCRIPT_DIR}/Caddyfile" /etc/caddy/Caddyfile
mkdir -p /etc/caddy/conf.d
mkdir -p /etc/systemd/system/caddy.service.d
cat > /etc/systemd/system/caddy.service.d/override.conf <<OVERRIDE
[Service]
Environment=CADDY_DOMAIN=${CADDY_DOMAIN}
OVERRIDE

systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl enable --now caddy
systemctl restart caddy

# 14. Verify dashboard is reachable
echo ""
echo "Verifying dashboard is reachable..."
sleep 5
if curl -sf "http://localhost:3000/api/status" -o /dev/null; then
  echo "OK: alive-server responding on localhost:3000"
else
  echo "FAILED: alive-server not responding"
  echo "Check logs: journalctl -u alive-server --no-pager -n 30"
fi

echo ""
echo "=== Base setup complete ==="
echo ""
echo "NOTE: Run 'sudo -iu ${AGNOSTIC_USER}' then 'claude' to log in to Claude Code."
echo ""

# 15. Offer to install browser services.
if [ -t 0 ]; then
  read -p "Install services now? (y/n) " -n 1 -r
  echo
  if [[ $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
    bash "${SCRIPT_DIR}/../services/install.sh"
  fi
else
  echo "Non-interactive mode — skipping services prompt."
  echo "Run sudo bash ${ATTLAS_DIR}/services/install.sh to install services."
fi

+# The wildcard scope (install-*.sh, uninstall-*.sh) is intentional: it's

content.text: Quick syntax check, then commit + deploy.

input.command:
bash -n /Users/kerima.weller/iapetus/attlas/services/install-terminal.sh && echo "ttyd ok"
bash -n /Users/kerima.weller/iapetus/attlas/services/install-code-server.sh && echo "code-server ok"
bash -n /Users/kerima.weller/iapetus/attlas/base-setup/setup.sh && echo "setup.sh ok"
echo "---"
cd /Users/kerima.weller/iapetus/attlas
git add -A
git -c user.name="Pedro Condeço" -c user.email="TC-AVNP@users.noreply.github.com" commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Fix terminal cwd + dashboard install buttons

ttyd / code-server cwd:
  Add WorkingDirectory=%h/iapetus to both browser-shell systemd units
  so opening /terminal or /code drops the user into the iapetus
  workspace by default instead of /. %h resolves to the home of
  User=agnostic-user.

Dashboard install/uninstall buttons:
  In the agnostic-user split, alive-svc isn't root and the install-*.sh
  scripts now require root (they write to /etc/systemd/system/), so
  clicking Install in the dashboard would fail with a sudo password
  prompt. Two-part fix:

  - alive-server's install/uninstall handlers invoke `sudo -n bash
    script` instead of `bash script`, and use `sudo -n` for the
    `systemctl reload caddy` follow-up too.
  - base-setup/setup.sh installs /etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-services
    granting alive-svc passwordless sudo to:
      /bin/bash <ATTLAS_DIR>/services/install-*.sh
      /bin/bash <ATTLAS_DIR>/services/uninstall-*.sh
      systemctl reload caddy
    The wildcard scope auto-extends when new install scripts are
    added; agnostic-user already has full NOPASSWD sudo, so this
    doesn't widen the trust boundary in practice.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -3
git push origin main 2>&1 | tail -3

gcloud compute ssh simple-zombie --zone=europe-west1-b --command='
set -e

echo "=== 1. pull latest attlas ==="
sudo -u agnostic-user bash -c "cd /home/agnostic-user/iapetus/attlas && git pull --ff-only origin main"

echo ""
echo "=== 2. rebuild alive-server ==="
sudo -u agnostic-user bash -c "export PATH=\$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin && cd /home/agnostic-user/iapetus/attlas/base-setup/alive-server && go build -o attlas-server ."

echo ""
echo "=== 3. install services sudoers drop-in ==="
ATTLAS_DIR=/home/agnostic-user/iapetus/attlas
sudo tee /etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-services > /dev/null <<SUDOERS
# Auto-generated — do not edit.
alive-svc ALL=(root) NOPASSWD: /bin/bash ${ATTLAS_DIR}/services/install-*.sh
alive-svc ALL=(root) NOPASSWD: /bin/bash ${ATTLAS_DIR}/services/uninstall-*.sh
alive-svc ALL=(root) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/systemctl reload caddy, /bin/systemctl reload caddy
SUDOERS
sudo chmod 440 /etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-services
sudo visudo -c -f /etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-services

echo ""
echo "=== 4. test sudo chain (must not prompt) ==="
sudo -u alive-svc sudo -n /bin/bash ${ATTLAS_DIR}/services/install-terminal.sh --help 2>&1 | head -3 || echo "(script doesnt support --help, but the sudo wouldve printed a prompt error if blocked)"
sudo -u alive-svc sudo -n /bin/systemctl reload caddy 2>&1 && echo "caddy reload OK"

echo ""
echo "=== 5. re-run install-terminal.sh and install-code-server.sh as root ==="
echo "    (regenerates the unit files with WorkingDirectory=%h/iapetus)"
sudo bash ${ATTLAS_DIR}/services/install-terminal.sh
sudo bash ${ATTLAS_DIR}/services/install-code-server.sh

echo ""
echo "=== 6. restart alive-server with new binary ==="
sudo systemctl restart alive-server
sleep 1
sudo systemctl is-active alive-server

echo ""
echo "=== 7. verify WorkingDirectory= is set on both units ==="
systemctl show ttyd -p WorkingDirectory --value
systemctl show code-server -p WorkingDirectory --value

echo ""
echo "=== 8. quick API smoke test ==="
curl -s http://localhost:3000/api/status > /tmp/status.json
python3 -c "
import json
d = json.load(open(\"/tmp/status.json\"))
print(\"claude.authenticated:\", d[\"claude\"][\"authenticated\"])
for s in d[\"services\"]:
    print(\"  {:12s} installed={} running={}\".format(s[\"id\"], s[\"installed\"], s[\"running\"]))
"
' 2>&1 | tail -50

content.text:
Two issues in the verification:

**Issue A**: `WorkingDirectory=%h/iapetus` resolved to `/root/iapetus`, not `/home/agnostic-user/iapetus`. Reading systemd docs more carefully:

> `%h`: This is the home directory of the user running the service manager instance. In case of the system manager this resolves to `/root`.

So `%h` in a *system* unit always resolves to `/root` regardless of `User=`. There's no specifier for "the User= user's home". I need to hardcode the path explicitly. The install script already knows `SERVICE_HOME` via `getent passwd`, so I can interpolate it into the heredoc.

**Issue B**: `sudo: a password is required` when testing the install sudoers chain. The `caddy reload` test passed with the same drop-in, so the issue is specific to the `install-*.sh` glob match. Let me debug it on the VM directly.

content.text:
Issue B was a false alarm from my test command. The sudoers chain actually works fine — calling `install-terminal.sh` with no extra args succeeded. The `--help` I added in my test was the issue (sudoers wildcard + extra args interaction). Real call paths from alive-server invoke the script with no extra args, so that's fine.

Issue A (WorkingDirectory) is real — fixing it now by hardcoding the resolved path via `getent passwd`.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Cloud terminal (ttyd) — web-based terminal with zsh + dotfiles
#
# Must be invoked as root (typically via services/install.sh from
# base-setup/setup.sh). The ttyd service runs as SERVICE_USER (default
# agnostic-user) so browser shells land in the login user's account.
set -euo pipefail

if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: install-terminal.sh must run as root." >&2
  exit 1
fi

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
SERVICE_USER="${SERVICE_USER:-agnostic-user}"

# Install ttyd
if ! command -v ttyd &>/dev/null; then
  TTYD_VERSION="1.7.7"
  curl -fsSL "https://github.com/tsl0922/ttyd/releases/download/${TTYD_VERSION}/ttyd.x86_64" \
    -o /usr/local/bin/ttyd
  chmod +x /usr/local/bin/ttyd
fi
echo "ttyd: $(ttyd --version 2>&1 | head -1)"

# Create systemd unit
# WorkingDirectory=%h/iapetus drops the user into the iapetus workspace by
# default when they open /terminal in the browser. %h resolves to the home
# of the User= above.
cat > /etc/systemd/system/ttyd.service <<UNIT
[Unit]
Description=ttyd - Web terminal
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=${SERVICE_USER}
WorkingDirectory=%h/iapetus
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/ttyd --base-path /terminal --port 7681 --writable /usr/bin/zsh
Restart=always
RestartSec=5

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
UNIT

# Deploy Caddy route snippet
cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/terminal.caddy" /etc/caddy/conf.d/

# Start
systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl enable --now ttyd

echo "terminal installed -> /terminal (port 7681), running as ${SERVICE_USER}"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Cloud VS Code (code-server) — VS Code in the browser
#
# Must be invoked as root. code-server runs as SERVICE_USER (default
# agnostic-user). User-specific setup (settings, extensions) is performed
# via `sudo -u ${SERVICE_USER}` so the files land in the right home.
set -euo pipefail

if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: install-code-server.sh must run as root." >&2
  exit 1
fi

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
SERVICE_USER="${SERVICE_USER:-agnostic-user}"
SERVICE_HOME="$(getent passwd "${SERVICE_USER}" | cut -d: -f6)"

if [[ -z "${SERVICE_HOME}" || ! -d "${SERVICE_HOME}" ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: home directory for ${SERVICE_USER} not found." >&2
  exit 1
fi

# Install code-server
if ! command -v code-server &>/dev/null; then
  curl -fsSL https://code-server.dev/install.sh | sh
fi
echo "code-server: $(code-server --version 2>&1 | head -1)"

# Create systemd unit
# WorkingDirectory=%h/iapetus opens the iapetus workspace as the default
# folder in code-server. %h resolves to the User=`s home.
cat > /etc/systemd/system/code-server.service <<UNIT
[Unit]
Description=code-server - VS Code in browser
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=${SERVICE_USER}
WorkingDirectory=%h/iapetus
ExecStart=/usr/bin/code-server --bind-addr 127.0.0.1:8080 --auth none --disable-telemetry
Restart=always
RestartSec=5

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
UNIT

# Ship default settings (dark theme, disable welcome) as SERVICE_USER
CS_USER_DIR="${SERVICE_HOME}/.local/share/code-server/User"
sudo -u "${SERVICE_USER}" mkdir -p "${CS_USER_DIR}"
sudo -u "${SERVICE_USER}" tee "${CS_USER_DIR}/settings.json" > /dev/null <<'SETTINGS'
{
  "workbench.colorTheme": "Default Dark Modern",
  "workbench.startupEditor": "none"
}
SETTINGS

# Pre-install extensions as SERVICE_USER
sudo -u "${SERVICE_USER}" code-server --install-extension golang.go 2>/dev/null || true
sudo -u "${SERVICE_USER}" code-server --install-extension dart-code.flutter 2>/dev/null || true

# Deploy Caddy route snippet
cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/code.caddy" /etc/caddy/conf.d/

# Start
systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl enable --now code-server

echo "code-server installed -> /code (port 8080), running as ${SERVICE_USER}"

content.text:
`WorkingDirectory` is correct in the unit definition, but the running ttyd process is still using the old cwd. The install script does `systemctl enable --now ttyd`, which is a no-op on a service that's already running — it doesn't restart. The OLD process is still alive with the OLD WorkingDirectory.

Restarting now and also fixing the install scripts to always restart on re-install.

echo "--- code-server cwd ---"
sudo readlink /proc/$(pgrep -u agnostic-user -f "code-server" | head -1)/cwd
' 2>&1 | tail -10

  echo "ERROR: home directory for ${SERVICE_USER} not found." >&2
  exit 1
fi

# Install ttyd
if ! command -v ttyd &>/dev/null; then
  TTYD_VERSION="1.7.7"
  curl -fsSL "https://github.com/tsl0922/ttyd/releases/download/${TTYD_VERSION}/ttyd.x86_64" \
    -o /usr/local/bin/ttyd
  chmod +x /usr/local/bin/ttyd
fi
echo "ttyd: $(ttyd --version 2>&1 | head -1)"

# Create systemd unit
# WorkingDirectory drops the user into the iapetus workspace by default
# when they open /terminal in the browser. The path is hardcoded (not %h)
# because for system units %h resolves to /root regardless of User=.
cat > /etc/systemd/system/ttyd.service <<UNIT
[Unit]
Description=ttyd - Web terminal
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=${SERVICE_USER}
WorkingDirectory=${SERVICE_HOME}/iapetus
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/ttyd --base-path /terminal --port 7681 --writable /usr/bin/zsh
Restart=always
RestartSec=5

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
UNIT

# Deploy Caddy route snippet
cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/terminal.caddy" /etc/caddy/conf.d/

# Start
systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl enable --now ttyd

echo "terminal installed -> /terminal (port 7681), running as ${SERVICE_USER}"
