content.text:
Now I have the real picture, and it shifts the plan meaningfully. Let me lay out what I learned and what I'd change.

## Key finding: openclaw itself can't give us Anthropic dollar costs

The docs explicitly say (under API Usage and Costs):

> *"Anthropic still does not expose a per-message dollar estimate that OpenClaw can show in `/usage full`."*

So even openclaw can't convert Anthropic's responses into dollars — Anthropic just doesn't put that in the streaming response. openclaw only has **token counts** and **provider usage quota percentages** for Anthropic, not dollars.

**What this means for F2**: my original plan to call Anthropic's billing API directly is still correct for the dollar figure. That's the *only* place Anthropic-dollar data lives. openclaw can't help there.

## Key finding: openclaw has a proper status CLI with `--json` output

This is the real win. `openclaw status` has several flags:

- `openclaw status --json` — machine-readable output
- `openclaw status --all --json` — comprehensive report: channels, sessions, token counters, provider usage, runtime model info, service status, update info
- `openclaw status --usage --json` — provider usage windows as "X% left"

So instead of reading openclaw's sqlite file directly, I can shell out to `openclaw status --all --json` and parse it. This is a **documented, stable, machine-readable interface** — exactly what I should've been looking for instead of the HTTP endpoint probing I did earlier.

## Key finding: there *are* three HTTP APIs on the gateway, but they're for calling openclaw (asking it to do work), not querying its state

Found in the docs index:
- `openai-http-api.md` — OpenAI Chat Completions compatibility layer
- `openresponses-http-api.md` — Response management
- `tools-invoke-http-api.md` — Tool invocation

These let you *use* openclaw to do agent work over HTTP. None of them return state/usage/metrics. So the WebSocket gateway + the status CLI really are the two ways in, and for "read my state" the CLI is the right tool.

## Revised F2 plan

**Replace** "open `runs.sqlite` directly with `modernc.org/sqlite`" **with** "shell out to `openclaw status --all --json` via sudo and parse the JSON".

Concrete changes:

1. **Backend (`/api/services/openclaw`)**:
   - Call `sudo -n -u openclaw-svc -H /usr/bin/openclaw status --all --json` from alive-server, parse the JSON, extract whatever openclaw reports (tasks, sessions, token counters, usage windows, channel status, runtime info).
   - Separately, call Anthropic's billing API with the admin key for the dollar figure. Cached 30 seconds.
   - Fetch `systemctl show openclaw-gateway` for uptime/memory (unchanged).
   - Walk `/var/lib/openclaw/.openclaw/` for storage size (unchanged).
   - **Drop `modernc.org/sqlite` from `go.mod`** — no longer needed for F2. It may still be needed for F5 (listing/introspecting sqlite databases on the detail page), or we can drop it entirely and let sqlite-web do all the inspection. **Decision point — see below.**

2. **Sudoers additions**:
   - `alive-svc ALL=(openclaw-svc) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/openclaw` (scoped to the binary, identical pattern to the existing `alive-svc → agnostic-user: /usr/bin/claude` rule).
   - Same `env_keep` for any vars openclaw needs (if any — its CLI seems self-contained).

3. **The `sqlite-readers` group ACL I was planning for F2 goes away**. alive-svc no longer needs read access to `/var/lib/openclaw/.openclaw/tasks/runs.sqlite` at all — it's now going through openclaw's own CLI, which runs as openclaw-svc and already has access.

4. **The F2 detail page content stays the same** from the user's perspective — same cards, same metrics. The only thing that changed is the backend's data source.

## What I'd change about F5 (sqlite service) as a knock-on

F5 was partly motivated by "you can browse openclaw's database". Now that alive-server doesn't need to touch openclaw's sqlite at all, the "shared group ACL" piece of F5 is also unnecessary for F2's sake — but you might still want it for F5 so that sqlite-web (running as `sqlite-svc`) can browse `/var/lib/openclaw/.openclaw/tasks/runs.sqlite`. That's a separate decision: do you still want sqlite-web to be able to open openclaw's sqlite file, or only sqlite files it can read without ACL gymnastics?

- **If yes**: keep the `sqlite-readers` group and the ACL on openclaw's file, but only `sqlite-svc` is in it (not `alive-svc`).
- **If no**: sqlite-web only browses databases it can read with default perms (which on our system means its own state dir, plus `/var/lib/alive-server/` if we add it to the group). openclaw's sqlite stays unreadable to sqlite-web. Simpler.

## A third benefit I didn't mention

The **`openclaw status --all --json` output almost certainly includes more than what I'd get from the sqlite file** — session info, channel status, token counters, provider usage percentages, update info, runtime model labels. My original plan was going to display a subset of what the sqlite schema has. The CLI version likely gives me *more* useful data than the sqlite version would have. The openclaw detail page will be richer and more useful as a result.

---

## Decisions I need from you before I update the plan

1. **Pivot F2 to `openclaw status --all --json` via sudo?** (I strongly recommend yes.)
2. **F5's access to openclaw's sqlite**: should sqlite-web be able to browse openclaw's `runs.sqlite` (keep the sqlite-readers group ACL), or only sqlite databases it can access without ACL gymnastics (simpler, drop the ACL)?

Once you answer, I'll update the unified plan document with the revised F2 and push it for final approval.

content.text:
F5 is gone. Here's the updated unified plan.

---

# Final unified plan (v2)

## Locked features

| # | Feature | Scope |
|---|---|---|
| F0 | Tab title + favicon consistency | Hand-drawn Atlas-shrugging SVG, title "attlas" on every route where controllable, openclaw accepted as-is |
| F1 | Dotfiles sync panel on dashboard | Timestamp + commit hash + `[sync now]` button |
| F2 | openclaw detail page (simplified) | **See revised version below.** CLI-via-sudo + Anthropic billing API. Minimal metrics only. |
| F3 | Domain expiry banner | `whois`-backed, color-coded |
| F6 | Dashboard redesign | Hero-driven main dashboard + card-based detail pages, diary palette |

**F5 (sqlite service) is dropped entirely.**

---

## F2 — revised, minimal openclaw detail page

**Backend (`/api/services/openclaw`)**, a single Go handler in `main.go`:

```go
// Pseudocode of the handler structure
func handleOpenclawDetail(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    // 1. Runtime status — already have this from existing systemctl helpers
    uptime, running := getServiceUptime("openclaw-gateway")

    // 2. openclaw's own status (JSON), called as openclaw-svc via sudo
    //    Output is parsed into a typed Go struct; only a few fields survive.
    status := runOpenclawStatusJSON()  // shells out, 2s timeout, 30s cache

    // 3. Anthropic billing API — dollars and tokens for the last 7 days
    billing := fetchAnthropicCost(adminKey)  // 30s cache

    sendJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{
        "running":              running,
        "uptime":               uptime,
        "anthropic_spend_usd":  billing.CostUSD,
        "anthropic_tokens_in":  billing.TokensIn,
        "anthropic_tokens_out": billing.TokensOut,
        "provider_quota":       status.ProviderQuota,  // e.g. "Anthropic: 73% left"
        "active_tasks":         status.ActiveTasks,
    })
}
```

**What the handler does NOT try to do:**

- No reading of openclaw's sqlite file directly (no `modernc.org/sqlite` dependency, no group ACL, no file permission gymnastics).
- No session listings, no per-task history, no transcripts, no update-check status, no channel breakdown, no runtime model labels, no secret diagnostics — all the comprehensive stuff `openclaw status --all` can emit. We only cherry-pick what's meaningful.
- No Brave data of any kind.

**New sudoers drop-in** (added to `base-setup/setup.sh`, right next to the existing `alive-svc-claude` one):

```
# /etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-openclaw
alive-svc ALL=(openclaw-svc) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/openclaw
```

Validated with `visudo -c`. Tightly scoped to the openclaw binary, same pattern as the existing Claude login rule.

**Frontend detail page** (`pages/detail/Openclaw.jsx`) — minimal card layout:

```
┌─── back to dashboard ──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                                                        │
│  openclaw                                                              │
│  AI agent daemon                                                       │
│                                                                        │
│  ┌────────────────────────────┐  ┌────────────────────────────┐      │
│  │  ANTHROPIC SPEND (7 DAYS)  │  │  STATUS                    │      │
│  │                            │  │                            │      │
│  │  $12.43                    │  │  ● running                 │      │
│  │  ──────                    │  │  uptime  3d 14h            │      │
│  │  1.2M tokens in            │  │                            │      │
│  │  340K tokens out           │  │  active tasks  2           │      │
│  └────────────────────────────┘  └────────────────────────────┘      │
│                                                                        │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐      │
│  │  PROVIDER QUOTA                                             │      │
│  │                                                             │      │
│  │  anthropic  ████████████████░░░░░░  73% left               │      │
│  │                                                             │      │
│  │  (quota windows reported by `openclaw status --usage`)      │      │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘      │
│                                                                        │
│                              open openclaw ↗                           │
│                                                                        │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

Three cards, one action link. That's it.

**One open flag in F2**: the exact shape of `openclaw status --all --json` output I don't know yet — I'll inspect it on the VM as the *first* step of F2 implementation and adjust the Go struct to match. If openclaw doesn't actually expose "active tasks" in its JSON, that card item silently disappears and we show just uptime. I'd rather find that out by running the command once than speculate.

---

## Cleanup from earlier inspection

`sqlite3` CLI is currently installed on the VM from when I ran `sudo apt-get install sqlite3` during openclaw schema discovery. Since F5 is dropped and nothing else needs it, I'll **`sudo apt remove --purge sqlite3`** on the VM as part of the first commit, to leave the box clean.

---

## F0, F1, F3, F6 (unchanged from v1)

Unchanged from the previous plan. Full details as written before — I won't repaste them. Key points:

- **F0** — hand-drawn SVG favicon, title "attlas", Caddy global handler for cross-route favicon, per-upstream title flags where available.
- **F1** — dotfiles sync panel with `[sync now]` button, backed by a new `/api/dotfiles/sync` endpoint + a small sudoers rule for `systemctl start dotfiles-sync.service`.
- **F3** — `whois`-backed domain banner, installed via `apt install whois` in base-setup, cached 6 hours, color-coded by days remaining.
- **F6** — hero-driven main dashboard + card-based detail pages, using the diary palette (`#0c0c0c` bg, `#00ff00` links, `#ff8700` brand, `#ffff00` headers), Inter for UI text + JetBrains Mono for technical values.

---

## Cross-cutting work (unchanged)

- Install `react-router-dom`, split `App.jsx` into `App.jsx` + `pages/Dashboard.jsx` + `pages/detail/Openclaw.jsx`.
- Add CSS variables for the diary palette, load Inter + JetBrains Mono from Google Fonts.
- `main.go` gains status helpers (`getDotfilesStatus`, `getDomainExpiry`, `handleOpenclawDetail`) and new endpoints (`/api/dotfiles/sync`, `/api/services/openclaw`).
- `OAuthConfig` struct extended with `AnthropicAdminKey string` field to read the admin key from the merged secret.
- **`modernc.org/sqlite` is NOT added to `go.mod`** — dropped along with F5.
- New sudoers drop-ins: `alive-svc-openclaw`, `alive-svc-dotfiles-sync`.

---

## Order of implementation

1. **Pre-work**: frontend refactor (react-router, split pages, CSS variables, fonts). ~30 min.
2. **F0**: favicon SVG + index.html title + Caddy global handler + per-upstream titles. ~30 min.
3. **F6**: dashboard + detail page shells (empty at first, just the layout and chrome). ~1 hour.
4. **F1**: dotfiles panel. ~30 min.
5. **F3**: domain expiry banner. ~30 min.
6. **F2**: first inspect `openclaw status --all --json` on the VM to see what it actually returns, then build the backend handler + frontend detail page. ~1.5 hours (including the inspection step).
7. **Cleanup**: `apt remove --purge sqlite3` on the VM, confirm nothing was depending on it.

Total ~4.5 hours. Commit after each feature, push after each clean build.

---

**Ready to execute?** One last thumbs-up and I start from step 1 without further questions. If anything still looks off, tell me now.

message.content: before we start, can we fursther refine what we will show on the openclaw details dashbaord ?

content.text:
Good instinct. Let me pin down exactly what goes on the page so we don't ship a random collection of cards.

**Current proposal (3 cards):**

1. **Anthropic spend, 7 days** — `$12.43`, `1.2M tokens in`, `340K tokens out`
2. **Status** — `● running`, `uptime 3d 14h`, `active tasks 2`
3. **Provider quota** — `anthropic 73% left`

Plus an `open openclaw ↗` action link.

---

Before we refine, I want to understand what you *actually* open this page to find out. That shapes what's meaningful. When you click into openclaw from the dashboard, which of these is the main thing on your mind?

- **(a) "Am I bleeding money?"** — cost awareness. Primary metric is $ spent, secondary is rate of spend.
- **(b) "Is it alive and doing things?"** — activity. Primary is "yes it's running and tasks are flowing through", secondary is spend.
- **(c) "Something's broken, what happened?"** — debugging. Primary is recent failures, logs, errors, secondary is status.
- **(d) Mix of (a) and (b)** — I want cost *and* "is it working" at a glance, but never use this page to debug.

Tell me which, and I'll revise the card layout to match. If it's (d) my current 3-card layout is about right and we just tune the specific fields. If it's (a), I'd drop cards 2 and 3 and go all-in on spend — maybe add a 30-day total, a daily sparkline, breakdown by Claude model. If it's (c), I'd drop the spend stuff and show recent task history, error counts, last failure, log tail.

content.text:
Got it. (a) + (b) + **"how many Telegram sessions I've had with the agent"** as the headline usage metric. Here's the refined proposal:

```
┌────────────────────────────┐  ┌────────────────────────────┐
│  SPEND · LAST 7 DAYS       │  │  USAGE · LAST 7 DAYS       │
│                            │  │                            │
│  $12.43                    │  │  14 telegram sessions      │
│                            │  │  47 tasks run              │
│  1.2M tokens in            │  │                            │
│  340K tokens out           │  │                            │
└────────────────────────────┘  └────────────────────────────┘

┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  STATUS                                                     │
│                                                             │
│  ● running · uptime 3d 14h · 2 active tasks                 │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

                         open openclaw ↗
```

**Three cards, three focus areas — matched to your three concerns:**

- **Spend card** = (a) cost awareness. Dollars is the primary number (big, orange, prominent), tokens are the detail line for when you want to eyeball the shape of usage.
- **Usage card** = your Telegram-sessions question + rough activity. Telegram sessions as the primary number since you said that's the main one you care about; total tasks run as a secondary line.
- **Status card** = (b) is-it-alive. Slim horizontal strip at the bottom, since "yes it's running" is usually a 1-second glance.

Plus `open openclaw ↗` as the single action — no restart buttons, no log viewer, no per-task table. If you want to dig in you click through to openclaw's own UI.

**Dropped from my previous proposal:**
- ~~Provider quota (% left)~~ — interesting but not in your (a) / (b) / telegram-sessions concerns. Skip.
- ~~Active tasks as its own field~~ — folded into the status line (`2 active tasks`).

---

**Two things I want to be honest about before we lock this:**

1. **"14 telegram sessions last 7 days" is the metric I'm committing to show, but I don't yet know for certain whether `openclaw status --all --json` exposes that count.** The docs mention "channels & sessions — per-agent session stores" in the comprehensive report, so I'm cautiously optimistic, but I won't know until I run the command on the VM and see the JSON. **My commitment**: if openclaw's CLI doesn't expose the number cleanly, I'll either (a) find an alternate source (e.g., count rows in `runs.sqlite` filtered by telegram origin — we'd still need the sqlite read for this specific case), or (b) come back to you and say "openclaw doesn't track this, here are the alternatives". I won't silently ship a broken card.

2. **The "spend last 7 days" window is a choice**. Anthropic's billing API supports arbitrary ranges. 7 days is my default because it's "recent" without being so short that a quiet day makes it look alarmingly low or high. If you'd rather see 30 days (more stable), "this month" (billing cycle), or both 7d and 30d side-by-side, say so.

**Lock this layout?** Or any final tweaks?

content.text:
Spend card becomes the focal point. Here's the revised layout:

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  SPEND                                                           │
│                                                                  │
│  $42.18                                                          │
│  april                                                           │
│                                                                  │
│      ┃                                                           │
│      ┃              ▆                                            │
│      ┃       ▃     ██       ▂                                    │
│      ┃      ██     ██   ▅  ██                                    │
│      ┃  ▄  ███  ▁ ███  ██ ███                                    │
│      ┃ ██ ████ ██ ████ ███████                                   │
│      ┣─────────────────────────                                  │
│        thu fri sat sun mon tue wed    ← last 7 days              │
│                                                                  │
│  $2.87 avg / day · 1.2M tokens in · 340K tokens out             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

┌─────────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│  USAGE · 7 DAYS     │  │  STATUS                             │
│                     │  │                                     │
│  14 telegram        │  │  ● running                          │
│     sessions        │  │  uptime  3d 14h                     │
│  47 tasks run       │  │  active  2 tasks                    │
└─────────────────────┘  └─────────────────────────────────────┘

                         open openclaw ↗
```

**What's on the spend card:**
- **Headline**: `$42.18` in big orange text + `april` subtitle = month-to-date total. This is the "am I bleeding money this month" number.
- **Chart**: 7 daily bars (SVG, hand-rolled — no chart library dependency, ~30 lines of React). Hovering a bar shows the exact `$X.XX` for that day in a tooltip. Bars colored in terminal green with orange for the highest bar (or the current day, whichever reads better — I'll pick on implementation).
- **Footer line**: `$2.87 avg / day` + token totals. Average is over the last 7 days (gives a more stable number than month-to-date, which can look tiny on the 1st of a month). Token counts are also 7 days.

**What's below the spend card:**
- **Usage** card (narrower, ~40% width): Telegram sessions headlines it, tasks count as secondary line.
- **Status** card (wider, ~60% width): running state, uptime, active tasks as one horizontal row.

**Data fetching** (one Anthropic API call, not two):
- Single `GET /v1/organizations/cost_report?starting_at=<first-of-month>&bucket_width=1d&group_by[]=api_key_id` call.
- Server computes: month-to-date total = `sum(all buckets)`, 7-day bars = `buckets[-7:]`, average/day = `sum(last 7) / 7`.
- Cached 30 seconds server-side.

**Two small questions before I lock this:**

1. **The chart: bars or line?** I defaulted to bars because they read better at the small size inside a card, and daily cost is discrete ("how much did I spend on Wednesday"). Line graph would work too, slightly more "trendy" looking. Your call — I'll go with bars if you don't care.

2. **"Avg/day" denominator**: I'm defaulting to "average over the last 7 days" because it's stable and matches what the chart shows. If you'd rather see "average for this month so far" (which would be $42.18 ÷ N days into the month), say so. The 7-day average is more informative IMO — it tells you today's rate — but the month average is what you'd divide to forecast the full month's bill.

Lock it or tweak?

input.content:
# Attlas Dashboard Feature Plan Consolidated execution plan for 5 dashboard features. Every decision in this document was locked through interactive planning — no open questions. When executing, follow the order in the "Order of implementation" section. ## Locked decisions | # | Feature | Scope | |---|---|---| | F0 | Tab title + favicon consistency | Hand-drawn Atlas-shrugging SVG favicon, `attlas` on every route where controllable, openclaw accepted as-is | | F1 | Dotfiles sync panel | Backend reads systemctl + git, frontend shows timestamp + commit hash + `[sync now]` button | | F2 | Openclaw detail page (simplified) | 3 cards: spend (month total + 7-day bar chart with hover), usage (telegram sessions + tasks), status (running + uptime + active tasks). Data via `openclaw status --all --json` subprocess + Anthropic billing API. No Brave. No sqlite file reads. | | F3 | Domain expiry banner | `whois attlas.uk`, parsed, cached 6h, color-coded by days remaining | | F6 | Dashboard redesign | Main dashboard = hero-driven minimal, detail pages = modern card-based, both in diary color palette | **F5 (sqlite as a service) was dropped entirely. Do not implement.** ## Color palette (diary-derived) Add these as CSS variables in `frontend/src/index.css`: ```css :root { --bg: #0c0c0c; /* page background */ --fg: #c0c0c0; /* default body text */ --border: #333; /* dividers, section rules */ --accent: #00ff00; /* terminal green — all links, ok indicators */ --brand: #ff8700; /* orange — "attlas" brand, headline numbers */ --header: #ffff00; /* yellow — section labels like "── STATUS ──" */ --muted: #8a8a8a; /* timestamps, secondary info */ --surface: #1a1a1a; /* card backgrounds, input backgrounds */ --danger: #ff5555; /* error states */ --warning: #ffcc00; /* warning states */ } ``` ## Typography Load both from Google Fonts in `frontend/index.html`: - **Inter** — primary UI font (sans-serif, modern, reads well at all sizes) - **JetBrains Mono** — technical values only: IPs, commit hashes, paths, timestamps, code ## Cross-cutting work (do this before any feature) ### Frontend refactor 1. `cd frontend && npm install react-router-dom` 2. Split `frontend/src/App.jsx` into: - `App.jsx` — router + global layout (header, domain banner, logout, footer, `` for page content). - `pages/Dashboard.jsx` — home view. - `pages/detail/Openclaw.jsx` — openclaw detail view. 3. Extract shared UI primitives into `frontend/src/components/`: - `StatusDot.jsx` — colored dot (green/red/yellow/grey) - `Card.jsx` — `
` with `--surface` background, rounded, bordered - `Button.jsx` — primary / ghost / danger variants - `Banner.jsx` — domain expiry banner component 4. Add the CSS variables and font imports to `frontend/src/index.css` and `frontend/index.html`. 5. Route table: - `/` → `` - `/services/details/openclaw` → `` - All other routes handled server-side by alive-server (the router only owns the SPA routes). ### Backend refactor (alive-server `main.go`) 1. Add `getDotfilesStatus()`, `getDomainExpiry()`, `handleOpenclawDetail()` helper functions. 2. Add fields to `/api/status` response: - `dotfiles` — `{last_sync, head_commit, remote_commit, behind, status}` - `domain_expiry` — `{expires_at, days_remaining, severity}` 3. New endpoints: - `POST /api/dotfiles/sync` — triggers `systemctl start dotfiles-sync.service` - `GET /api/services/openclaw` — returns openclaw detail data 4. Extend `OAuthConfig` struct: ```go type OAuthConfig struct { ClientID string `json:"google_oauth_client_id"` ClientSecret string `json:"google_oauth_client_secret"` AllowedEmails []string `json:"allowed_emails"` AnthropicAdminKey string `json:"anthropic_admin_key"` // NEW } ``` 5. Add `whois` parser helper — shells out to `whois attlas.uk`, greps for `Expiry date:`, parses to ISO8601. Cache for 6 hours. ### New sudoers drop-ins Add these to `base-setup/setup.sh`. Each must be validated with `visudo -c -f ` after writing. **`/etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-openclaw`** (for F2): ``` Defaults:alive-svc env_keep += "HOME" alive-svc ALL=(openclaw-svc) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/openclaw ``` **`/etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-dotfiles-sync`** (for F1 `[sync now]` button): ``` alive-svc ALL=(root) NOPASSWD: /bin/systemctl start dotfiles-sync.service ``` ### Caddyfile changes (F0) Add a global favicon handler in `base-setup/Caddyfile` so every subpath falls through to the same SVG icon. Placement: before the per-service imports, so it takes priority over upstream `/favicon.ico` paths. ```caddy {$CADDY_DOMAIN} { # Global favicon override — every */favicon.{ico,svg,png} request # returns the attlas SVG regardless of subpath. @favicon path_regexp ^/.*favicon\.(ico|svg|png)$ handle @favicon { header Content-Type "image/svg+xml" header Cache-Control "public, max-age=86400" rewrite * /favicon.svg reverse_proxy localhost:3000 } # ... existing forward_auth and imports ... } ``` Note: the rewrite + reverse_proxy lets alive-server serve the SVG from its frontend `public/` directory. Alternative is a `respond` with the SVG inlined into the Caddyfile — pick whichever is more maintainable. ### Package additions (add to `base-setup/setup.sh` apt-get line) - `whois` — for F3 ### Cleanup from earlier discovery The `sqlite3` apt package was installed on the VM during discovery when inspecting openclaw's database. F5 is dropped and nothing else uses it: ```bash sudo apt remove --purge -y sqlite3 ``` Run this as part of the first commit's deployment, so the box is clean. ## Feature specs ### F0 — Tab title + favicon **Favicon**: hand-drawn SVG of Atlas with the celestial sphere floating above his visibly-not-holding-it hands. Palette: `--brand` (#ff8700) for the figure, `--accent` (#00ff00) highlights for the sphere. Save as `frontend/public/favicon.svg`. **Dashboard ``**: edit `frontend/index.html`: ```html <title>attlas ``` **Per-upstream tab titles** (4-of-5 consistency — openclaw accepted as-is): - **Dashboard** → `attlas` in `frontend/index.html` - **ttyd** → add `--title-format=attlas` to `ExecStart` in `services/install-terminal.sh` - **code-server** → add `--app-name=attlas` to `ExecStart` in `services/install-code-server.sh` - **Hugo diary** → edit `diary/layouts/_default/baseof.html` `` block: ```html <title>{{ if .IsHome }}attlas{{ else }}attlas — {{ .Title }}{{ end }} ``` - **openclaw** → accepted as-is (its UI hardcodes the title in bundled JS) **Cross-route favicon**: via the Caddy global handler in the "Caddyfile changes" section above. Every `/*/favicon.*` request at any subpath returns the same SVG. ### F1 — Dotfiles sync panel **Backend helpers** in `main.go`: ```go type DotfilesStatus struct { LastSync string `json:"last_sync"` // ISO timestamp LastExitStatus int `json:"last_exit_status"` // 0 = ok HeadCommit string `json:"head_commit"` // short hash HeadCommittedAt string `json:"head_committed_at"` // ISO RemoteCommit string `json:"remote_commit"` // short hash Behind int `json:"behind"` // commit count Status string `json:"status"` // "up-to-date" | "behind" | "error" } func getDotfilesStatus() DotfilesStatus { // systemctl show dotfiles-sync.service -p ExecMainExitTimestamp -p ExecMainStatus // git -C /home/agnostic-user/iapetus/dotfiels rev-parse HEAD // git -C ... rev-parse origin/master // git -C ... log -1 --format=%cI HEAD // git -C ... rev-list --count HEAD..origin/master } ``` Wired into `/api/status` as a new `dotfiles` field. **New endpoint**: `POST /api/dotfiles/sync` ```go func handleDotfilesSync(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) { cmd := exec.Command("sudo", "-n", "systemctl", "start", "dotfiles-sync.service") if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil { sendJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{"error": err.Error()}) return } sendJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{"success": true}) } ``` **Frontend**: new row inside the STATUS section of `pages/Dashboard.jsx`: ``` dotfiles ● up to date · 8c3ea15 · synced 2 min ago sync now ``` - Green dot if status=up-to-date and last_exit=0 - Yellow dot if behind - Red dot if last_exit != 0 - `[sync now]` is a text link (uses `--accent` color) that calls `POST /api/dotfiles/sync` and refreshes status on success. ### F2 — Openclaw detail page **Route**: `/services/details/openclaw` **Backend endpoint**: `GET /api/services/openclaw` ```go type OpenclawDetail struct { Running bool `json:"running"` Uptime string `json:"uptime"` // human-readable ActiveTasks int `json:"active_tasks"` TelegramSessions int `json:"telegram_sessions"` // last 7 days TasksRun int `json:"tasks_run"` // last 7 days SpendThisMonth float64 `json:"spend_this_month"` // USD SpendDaily []DayCost `json:"spend_daily"` // last 7 days } type DayCost struct { Date string `json:"date"` // "2026-04-10" USD float64 `json:"usd"` } func handleOpenclawDetail(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) { // 1. systemctl show openclaw-gateway — uptime, running state // 2. sudo -n -u openclaw-svc -H /usr/bin/openclaw status --all --json // Parse the JSON (shape TBD — inspect on the VM first). // Extract: active tasks, telegram session count, tasks run count. // 3. Anthropic cost_report API call: // GET https://api.anthropic.com/v1/organizations/cost_report // ?starting_at= // &bucket_width=1d // Parse buckets into spend_this_month and spend_daily (last 7). // Cache 30s. } ``` **Frontend** (`pages/detail/Openclaw.jsx`): ``` back to dashboard ← openclaw AI agent daemon ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ SPEND │ │ │ │ $42.18 │ │ april │ │ │ │ [7-day SVG bar chart — see below] │ └──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ┌───────────────────┐ ┌──────────────────────────┐ │ USAGE · 7 DAYS │ │ STATUS │ │ │ │ │ │ 14 telegram │ │ ● running │ │ sessions │ │ uptime 3d 14h │ │ 47 tasks run │ │ active 2 tasks │ └───────────────────┘ └──────────────────────────┘ open openclaw ↗ ``` **Bar chart implementation** (inline SVG, no library): - 7 bars representing the last 7 days - Each bar is a `` with height proportional to USD - `` child on each `<rect>` = native browser tooltip showing `$X.XX on <date>` - Day labels below the bars using `<text>` elements - Bars colored `--accent` (green), tallest bar or "today" colored `--brand` (orange) — pick on implementation - ~30 lines of React **Visual spec for the detail page chrome**: - Modern card layout: cards have `background: var(--surface)`, `border: 1px solid var(--border)`, `border-radius: 8px`, generous padding - Big headline number in `--brand` orange, large font - Labels in `--muted` grey, small caps - `● running` uses `--accent` green for the dot - Header has `← back to dashboard` link (uses `--accent` color, hover underline) and the same logout link as the main dashboard **Known unknown**: the exact JSON shape of `openclaw status --all --json` is not confirmed. Before writing the Go struct, run this on the VM: ```bash sudo -u openclaw-svc -H /usr/bin/openclaw status --all --json | python3 -m json.tool | head -100 ``` and map the keys to the `OpenclawDetail` fields. If openclaw doesn't expose `telegram_sessions` or `active_tasks` directly, fall back to: - Telegram sessions: grep for any telegram-related session keys in the JSON, or count rows in `runs.sqlite` with a telegram label. If neither works, remove the field from the usage card and ship with only `tasks_run`. **Do not silently ship a broken card — either find a source or remove the field.** - Active tasks: if not in status JSON, count task_runs with `status='running'` in runs.sqlite. Requires the sqlite dependency back — only add it if actually needed. ### F3 — Domain expiry banner **Backend helper** in `main.go`: ```go type DomainExpiry struct { ExpiresAt string `json:"expires_at"` // ISO date DaysRemaining int `json:"days_remaining"` Severity string `json:"severity"` // ok | warn | danger } func getDomainExpiry() DomainExpiry { // shell out: whois attlas.uk // grep for "Expiry date:" // parse "09-Apr-2027" → ISO → days from now // severity: "ok" if >60d, "warn" if 30-60d, "danger" if <30d // cache 6 hours } ``` Wired into `/api/status` as `domain_expiry`. **Frontend banner component** in `App.jsx` (shows on every route): ```jsx function DomainBanner({ expiry }) { if (!expiry || expiry.severity === "ok") return null; // hide when far // yellow border/accent for warn // red for danger return ( <div className={`banner banner-${expiry.severity}`}> ⚠ domain attlas.uk expires in {expiry.days_remaining} days ({expiry.expires_at}) <a href="https://dash.cloudflare.com">renew</a> </div> ); } ``` **Package**: add `whois` to the apt-get install line in `base-setup/setup.sh`. ### F6 — Dashboard redesign **Main dashboard** (`pages/Dashboard.jsx`) — hero-driven minimal: ``` ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ attlas you@email logout │ │ │ │ │ │ ⬤ all systems healthy │ │ all 5 services running, dotfiles up to date │ │ │ │ │ │ ──── STATUS ─────────────────────────────────────────────── │ │ │ │ vm simple-zombie · europe-west1-b │ │ ip 34.62.185.156 │ │ domain attlas.uk → renews in 365 days │ │ claude ● authenticated logout │ │ dotfiles ● up to date · 2 min ago sync now │ │ │ │ │ │ ──── SERVICES ───────────────────────────────────────────── │ │ │ │ ⬤ cloud terminal /terminal open details × │ │ ⬤ cloud vs code /code open details × │ │ ⬤ openclaw /openclaw open details × │ │ ⬤ project diary /diary/ open details × │ │ │ └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` **Key UX moves:** - **Hero status** at top: big colored dot + one-line phrase. Green "all systems healthy" when nothing is wrong; yellow/red with count and brief description when anything is off. - **Single status section** folds VM info + domain + claude + dotfiles into aligned rows. No more three separate sections. - **Service rows** get `open` and `details` text links — biggest current omission. `open` goes to the service's URL, `details` goes to `/services/details/<id>`. Uninstall is a small `×` at the row end. - **`[sync now]` on dotfiles row** triggers F1's new endpoint. - **Only the openclaw row has a working `details` link in the first cut**. Other services' details links can be disabled (greyed out) or point to a "not yet implemented" page. **Layout**: - Single column, `max-width: 720px`, centered, generous vertical padding - Inter for text, JetBrains Mono for IPs/hashes/timestamps/paths - Section labels (`STATUS`, `SERVICES`) in `--header` yellow, with a thin `--border` rule **Detail pages** (`pages/detail/Openclaw.jsx`) — modern card-based: - Header: `← back to dashboard` link + page title + subtitle - Body: grid of cards (2-column on desktop, 1-column on mobile) - Cards: `--surface` background, rounded, `--border` stroke, generous padding - Headline metric in `--brand` orange, large - Action row at the bottom: primary action as a proper `<button>` (green bg, black text) not a text link - Footer: same domain banner + logout as the main dashboard **Shared chrome (both dashboard and detail pages)**: - Domain banner above the header when `severity !== "ok"` (F3) - Toast notifications bottom-right: green success, red error, yellow warning, auto-dismiss 4s - Loading state: pulsing `--accent` dot, not a spinner - Focus states: bright `--accent` outline for keyboard navigation - Claude login modal stays functional, restyled to match ## Files that will change **Frontend** (`base-setup/alive-server/frontend/`): - `index.html` — title, favicon link, font imports - `src/index.css` — CSS variables, new components styles - `src/App.jsx` — refactored to router + layout - `src/pages/Dashboard.jsx` — new (extracted from old App.jsx, redesigned) - `src/pages/detail/Openclaw.jsx` — new - `src/components/StatusDot.jsx` — new - `src/components/Card.jsx` — new - `src/components/Button.jsx` — new - `src/components/Banner.jsx` — new - `public/favicon.svg` — new (hand-drawn) - `package.json` — add `react-router-dom` **Backend** (`base-setup/alive-server/`): - `main.go` — `getDotfilesStatus()`, `getDomainExpiry()`, `handleOpenclawDetail()`, `handleDotfilesSync()`, extended OAuthConfig struct, new routes - `go.mod` — no new dependencies (stretch: may add `modernc.org/sqlite` only if F2 fallback requires it) **Setup scripts**: - `base-setup/setup.sh` — add whois to apt list, add new sudoers drop-ins, everything else unchanged - `base-setup/Caddyfile` — global favicon handler - `services/install-terminal.sh` — `--title-format=attlas` - `services/install-code-server.sh` — `--app-name=attlas` **Diary template**: - `diary/layouts/_default/baseof.html` — `<title>` block update ## Order of implementation Execute sequentially. Commit after each step. Push after each clean build. 1. **Pre-work** (frontend refactor): install `react-router-dom`, split `App.jsx` into router + pages, add CSS variables + fonts, create shared components. Build must pass before moving on. 2. **F0**: favicon SVG + index.html title + Caddy global handler + per-upstream title flags. Verify on the VM: open `/terminal`, `/code`, `/diary` and confirm the tab icon is the attlas SVG on all three. 3. **F6 (shells)**: redesigned main dashboard + detail page scaffolding with the new card/hero chrome, using placeholder data. This lets the other features slot into a finished-looking layout. 4. **F1**: dotfiles panel + sync button + backend helpers + sudoers drop-in. 5. **F3**: domain expiry banner + whois install + cached helper. 6. **F2**: openclaw detail page. **First step: run `openclaw status --all --json` on the VM and document the actual JSON shape** before writing the Go struct. Then backend endpoint, then frontend card rendering with the SVG bar chart. 7. **Cleanup**: `apt remove --purge sqlite3` on the VM. Estimated total: ~4.5 hours of focused work. ## Known risks to resolve during implementation - **F2 openclaw status JSON shape is unverified**. Step 1 of F2 is to run the command and inspect output. If `telegram_sessions` isn't exposed, use the fallback plan documented in the F2 section. - **Caddy favicon rewrite** may conflict with existing path handlers. Test `/favicon.ico`, `/terminal/favicon.ico`, `/code/favicon.ico` after deploying — all three should return the attlas SVG. If not, adjust the matcher precedence. - **`openclaw status` subprocess may hang** if the gateway is unresponsive. Wrap the call in a 2-second timeout (`exec.CommandContext` with `context.WithTimeout`). On timeout, return last-known-good cached data or a "temporarily unavailable" state — never block the dashboard. - **Anthropic cost_report API** may return 401 if the admin key is revoked or the billing endpoint is disabled for the org. Handle 4xx responses gracefully and show "billing data unavailable" in the spend card instead of crashing. - **`whois` package** may not be installed on a fresh VM before the first run of base-setup. The domain banner backend call must tolerate `whois: command not found` and return "unknown" severity. ## Deployment After every feature commit on the local laptop: 1. Build the frontend: `cd frontend && npm run build` 2. Commit both the source AND the `dist/` changes to the attlas repo. 3. Push to origin/main. 4. SSH to the VM, pull the attlas repo as `agnostic-user`, rebuild alive-server as `agnostic-user`, restart the service: ```bash sudo -u agnostic-user bash -c "cd /home/agnostic-user/iapetus/attlas && git pull" sudo -u agnostic-user bash -c "cd /home/agnostic-user/iapetus/attlas/base-setup/alive-server && PATH=\$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin go build -o attlas-server ." sudo systemctl restart alive-server ``` 5. For changes that touch Caddy or sudoers: additionally run `sudo systemctl reload caddy` and validate sudoers with `sudo visudo -c`. 6. Verify via the dashboard URL that the change is live. ## Out of scope (explicitly, do not add) - Brave search metrics (no API, no on-disk data). - Sqlite as a service (F5 was dropped). - Token counts on the openclaw spend card (dropped by user decision). - Average-cost-per-day footer on the spend card (dropped by user decision). - Provider quota card on the openclaw detail page (dropped in favor of the simpler 3-card layout). - Detail pages for services other than openclaw in this pass. Other services' `details` links can be disabled or point to a stub. - Reverse-proxy refactor to route all services through alive-server. - Service install/uninstall sudoers scope widening. - Multi-VM Terraform module refactor.

content.content: File created successfully at: /Users/kerima.weller/iapetus/attlas/details-plan.md

Every `/*/favicon.*` request at any subpath returns the same SVG. ### F1 — Dotfiles sync panel **Backend helpers** in `main.go`: ```go type DotfilesStatus struct { LastSync string `json:"last_sync"` // ISO timestamp LastExitStatus int `json:"last_exit_status"` // 0 = ok HeadCommit string `json:"head_commit"` // short hash HeadCommittedAt string `json:"head_committed_at"` // ISO RemoteCommit string `json:"remote_commit"` // short hash Behind int `json:"behind"` // commit count Status string `json:"status"` // "up-to-date" | "behind" | "error" } func getDotfilesStatus() DotfilesStatus { // systemctl show dotfiles-sync.service -p ExecMainExitTimestamp -p ExecMainStatus // git -C /home/agnostic-user/iapetus/dotfiels rev-parse HEAD // git -C ... rev-parse origin/master // git -C ... log -1 --format=%cI HEAD // git -C ... rev-list --count HEAD..origin/master } ``` Wired into `/api/status` as a new `dotfiles` field. **New endpoint**: `POST /api/dotfiles/sync` ```go func handleDotfilesSync(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) { cmd := exec.Command("sudo", "-n", "systemctl", "start", "dotfiles-sync.service") if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil { sendJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{"error": err.Error()}) return } sendJSON(w, map[string]interface{}{"success": true}) } ``` **Frontend**: new row inside the STATUS section of `pages/Dashboard.jsx`: ``` dotfiles ● up to date · 8c3ea15 · synced 2 min ago sync now ``` - Green dot if status=up-to-date and last_exit=0 - Yellow dot if behind - Red dot if last_exit != 0 - `[sync now]` is a text link (uses `--accent` color) that calls `POST /api/dotfiles/sync` and refreshes status on success. ### F2 — Openclaw detail page **Route**: `/services/details/openclaw` **Backend endpoint**: `GET /api/services/openclaw` ```go type OpenclawDetail struct { Running bool `json:"running"` Uptime string `json:"uptime"` // human-readable ActiveTasks int `json:"active_tasks"` TelegramSessions int `json:"telegram_sessions"` // last 7 days TasksRun int `json:"tasks_run"` // last 7 days SpendThisMonth float64 `json:"spend_this_month"` // USD SpendDaily []DayCost `json:"spend_daily"` // last 7 days } type DayCost struct { Date string `json:"date"` // "2026-04-10" USD float64 `json:"usd"` } func handleOpenclawDetail(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) { // 1. systemctl show openclaw-gateway — uptime, running state // 2. sudo -n -u openclaw-svc -H /usr/bin/openclaw status --all --json // Parse the JSON (shape TBD — inspect on the VM first). // Extract: active tasks, telegram session count, tasks run count. // 3. Anthropic cost_report API call: // GET https://api.anthropic.com/v1/organizations/cost_report // ?starting_at= // &bucket_width=1d // Parse buckets into spend_this_month and spend_daily (last 7). // Cache 30s. } ``` **Frontend** (`pages/detail/Openclaw.jsx`): ``` back to dashboard ← openclaw AI agent daemon ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ SPEND │ │ │ │ $42.18 │ │ april │ │ │ │ [7-day SVG bar chart — see below] │ └──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ┌───────────────────┐ ┌──────────────────────────┐ │ USAGE · 7 DAYS │ │ STATUS │ │ │ │ │ │ 14 telegram │ │ ● running │ │ sessions │ │ uptime 3d 14h │ │ 47 tasks run │ │ active 2 tasks │ └───────────────────┘ └──────────────────────────┘ open openclaw ↗ ``` **Bar chart implementation** (inline SVG, no library): - 7 bars representing the last 7 days - Each bar is a `` with height proportional to USD - `` child on each `<rect>` = native browser tooltip showing `$X.XX on <date>` - Day labels below the bars using `<text>` elements - Bars colored `--accent` (green), tallest bar or "today" colored `--brand` (orange) — pick on implementation - ~30 lines of React **Visual spec for the detail page chrome**: - Modern card layout: cards have `background: var(--surface)`, `border: 1px solid var(--border)`, `border-radius: 8px`, generous padding - Big headline number in `--brand` orange, large font - Labels in `--muted` grey, small caps - `● running` uses `--accent` green for the dot - Header has `← back to dashboard` link (uses `--accent` color, hover underline) and the same logout link as the main dashboard **Known unknown**: the exact JSON shape of `openclaw status --all --json` is not confirmed. Before writing the Go struct, run this on the VM: ```bash sudo -u openclaw-svc -H /usr/bin/openclaw status --all --json | python3 -m json.tool | head -100 ``` and map the keys to the `OpenclawDetail` fields. If openclaw doesn't expose `telegram_sessions` or `active_tasks` directly, fall back to: - Telegram sessions: grep for any telegram-related session keys in the JSON, or count rows in `runs.sqlite` with a telegram label. If neither works, remove the field from the usage card and ship with only `tasks_run`. **Do not silently ship a broken card — either find a source or remove the field.** - Active tasks: if not in status JSON, count task_runs with `status='running'` in runs.sqlite. Requires the sqlite dependency back — only add it if actually needed. ### F3 — Domain expiry banner **Backend helper** in `main.go`: ```go type DomainExpiry struct { ExpiresAt string `json:"expires_at"` // ISO date DaysRemaining int `json:"days_remaining"` Severity string `json:"severity"` // ok | warn | danger } func getDomainExpiry() DomainExpiry { // shell out: whois attlas.uk // grep for "Expiry date:" // parse "09-Apr-2027" → ISO → days from now // severity: "ok" if >60d, "warn" if 30-60d, "danger" if <30d // cache 6 hours } ``` Wired into `/api/status` as `domain_expiry`. **Frontend banner component** in `App.jsx` (shows on every route): ```jsx function DomainBanner({ expiry }) { if (!expiry || expiry.severity === "ok") return null; // hide when far // yellow border/accent for warn // red for danger return ( <div className={`banner banner-${expiry.severity}`}> ⚠ domain attlas.uk expires in {expiry.days_remaining} days ({expiry.expires_at}) <a href="https://dash.cloudflare.com">renew</a> </div> ); } ``` **Package**: add `whois` to the apt-get install line in `base-setup/setup.sh`. ### F6 — Dashboard redesign **Main dashboard** (`pages/Dashboard.jsx`) — hero-driven minimal: ``` ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ attlas you@email logout │ │ │ │ │ │ ⬤ all systems healthy │ │ all 5 services running, dotfiles up to date │ │ │ │ │ │ ──── STATUS ─────────────────────────────────────────────── │ │ │ │ vm simple-zombie · europe-west1-b │ │ ip 34.62.185.156 │ │ domain attlas.uk → renews in 365 days │ │ claude ● authenticated logout │ │ dotfiles ● up to date · 2 min ago sync now │ │ │ │ │ │ ──── SERVICES ───────────────────────────────────────────── │ │ │ │ ⬤ cloud terminal /terminal open details × │ │ ⬤ cloud vs code /code open details × │ │ ⬤ openclaw /openclaw open details × │ │ ⬤ project diary /diary/ open details × │ │ │ └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` **Key UX moves:** - **Hero status** at top: big colored dot + one-line phrase. Green "all systems healthy" when nothing is wrong; yellow/red with count and brief description when anything is off. - **Single status section** folds VM info + domain + claude + dotfiles into aligned rows. No more three separate sections. - **Service rows** get `open` and `details` text links — biggest current omission. `open` goes to the service's URL, `details` goes to `/services/details/<id>`. Uninstall is a small `×` at the row end. - **`[sync now]` on dotfiles row** triggers F1's new endpoint. - **Only the openclaw row has a working `details` link in the first cut**. Other services' details links can be disabled (greyed out) or point to a "not yet implemented" page. **Layout**: - Single column, `max-width: 720px`, centered, generous vertical padding - Inter for text, JetBrains Mono for IPs/hashes/timestamps/paths - Section labels (`STATUS`, `SERVICES`) in `--header` yellow, with a thin `--border` rule **Detail pages** (`pages/detail/Openclaw.jsx`) — modern card-based: - Header: `← back to dashboard` link + page title + subtitle - Body: grid of cards (2-column on desktop, 1-column on mobile) - Cards: `--surface` background, rounded, `--border` stroke, generous padding - Headline metric in `--brand` orange, large - Action row at the bottom: primary action as a proper `<button>` (green bg, black text) not a text link - Footer: same domain banner + logout as the main dashboard **Shared chrome (both dashboard and detail pages)**: - Domain banner above the header when `severity !== "ok"` (F3) - Toast notifications bottom-right: green success, red error, yellow warning, auto-dismiss 4s - Loading state: pulsing `--accent` dot, not a spinner - Focus states: bright `--accent` outline for keyboard navigation - Claude login modal stays functional, restyled to match ## Files that will change **Frontend** (`base-setup/alive-server/frontend/`): - `index.html` — title, favicon link, font imports - `src/index.css` — CSS variables, new components styles - `src/App.jsx` — refactored to router + layout - `src/pages/Dashboard.jsx` — new (extracted from old App.jsx, redesigned) - `src/pages/detail/Openclaw.jsx` — new - `src/components/StatusDot.jsx` — new - `src/components/Card.jsx` — new - `src/components/Button.jsx` — new - `src/components/Banner.jsx` — new - `public/favicon.svg` — new (hand-drawn) - `package.json` — add `react-router-dom` **Backend** (`base-setup/alive-server/`): - `main.go` — `getDotfilesStatus()`, `getDomainExpiry()`, `handleOpenclawDetail()`, `handleDotfilesSync()`, extended OAuthConfig struct, new routes - `go.mod` — no new dependencies (stretch: may add `modernc.org/sqlite` only if F2 fallback requires it) **Setup scripts**: - `base-setup/setup.sh` — add whois to apt list, add new sudoers drop-ins, everything else unchanged - `base-setup/Caddyfile` — global favicon handler - `services/install-terminal.sh` — `--title-format=attlas` - `services/install-code-server.sh` — `--app-name=attlas` **Diary template**: - `diary/layouts/_default/baseof.html` — `<title>` block update ## Order of implementation Execute sequentially. Commit after each step. Push after each clean build. 1. **Pre-work** (frontend refactor): install `react-router-dom`, split `App.jsx` into router + pages, add CSS variables + fonts, create shared components. Build must pass before moving on. 2. **F0**: favicon SVG + index.html title + Caddy global handler + per-upstream title flags. Verify on the VM: open `/terminal`, `/code`, `/diary` and confirm the tab icon is the attlas SVG on all three. 3. **F6 (shells)**: redesigned main dashboard + detail page scaffolding with the new card/hero chrome, using placeholder data. This lets the other features slot into a finished-looking layout. 4. **F1**: dotfiles panel + sync button + backend helpers + sudoers drop-in. 5. **F3**: domain expiry banner + whois install + cached helper. 6. **F2**: openclaw detail page. **First step: run `openclaw status --all --json` on the VM and document the actual JSON shape** before writing the Go struct. Then backend endpoint, then frontend card rendering with the SVG bar chart. 7. **Cleanup**: `apt remove --purge sqlite3` on the VM. Estimated total: ~4.5 hours of focused work. ## Known risks to resolve during implementation - **F2 openclaw status JSON shape is unverified**. Step 1 of F2 is to run the command and inspect output. If `telegram_sessions` isn't exposed, use the fallback plan documented in the F2 section. - **Caddy favicon rewrite** may conflict with existing path handlers. Test `/favicon.ico`, `/terminal/favicon.ico`, `/code/favicon.ico` after deploying — all three should return the attlas SVG. If not, adjust the matcher precedence. - **`openclaw status` subprocess may hang** if the gateway is unresponsive. Wrap the call in a 2-second timeout (`exec.CommandContext` with `context.WithTimeout`). On timeout, return last-known-good cached data or a "temporarily unavailable" state — never block the dashboard. - **Anthropic cost_report API** may return 401 if the admin key is revoked or the billing endpoint is disabled for the org. Handle 4xx responses gracefully and show "billing data unavailable" in the spend card instead of crashing. - **`whois` package** may not be installed on a fresh VM before the first run of base-setup. The domain banner backend call must tolerate `whois: command not found` and return "unknown" severity. ## Deployment After every feature commit on the local laptop: 1. Build the frontend: `cd frontend && npm run build` 2. Commit both the source AND the `dist/` changes to the attlas repo. 3. Push to origin/main. 4. SSH to the VM, pull the attlas repo as `agnostic-user`, rebuild alive-server as `agnostic-user`, restart the service: ```bash sudo -u agnostic-user bash -c "cd /home/agnostic-user/iapetus/attlas && git pull" sudo -u agnostic-user bash -c "cd /home/agnostic-user/iapetus/attlas/base-setup/alive-server && PATH=\$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin go build -o attlas-server ." sudo systemctl restart alive-server ``` 5. For changes that touch Caddy or sudoers: additionally run `sudo systemctl reload caddy` and validate sudoers with `sudo visudo -c`. 6. Verify via the dashboard URL that the change is live. ## Out of scope (explicitly, do not add) - Brave search metrics (no API, no on-disk data). - Sqlite as a service (F5 was dropped). - Token counts on the openclaw spend card (dropped by user decision). - Average-cost-per-day footer on the spend card (dropped by user decision). - Provider quota card on the openclaw detail page (dropped in favor of the simpler 3-card layout). - Detail pages for services other than openclaw in this pass. Other services' `details` links can be disabled or point to a stub. - Reverse-proxy refactor to route all services through alive-server. - Service install/uninstall sudoers scope widening. - Multi-VM Terraform module refactor.

content.text:
Plan written to `/Users/kerima.weller/iapetus/attlas/details-plan.md` (~400 lines of markdown, everything we agreed on).

The file is standalone — if you `cat` it next week with no conversation context, it should still give you (or another agent) a complete picture of what to build, in what order, with exact code snippets where they're stable (CSS variables, sudoers rules, systemd snippets).

**Sections in the file:**
- Locked decisions table
- Color palette + typography
- Cross-cutting work (frontend refactor, backend refactor, sudoers, Caddy, cleanup)
- Per-feature specs: F0, F1, F2, F3, F6
- Complete file-change list
- Order of implementation (7 numbered steps)
- Known risks to resolve during implementation
- Deployment steps
- Explicit "out of scope" section (so future me/you can't accidentally scope-creep it back in)

**Notable**: `F5 is dropped — do not implement` is called out twice, once in the decisions table and once in out-of-scope. Same with the footer/tokens/quota items we dropped during refinement.

The file is in the attlas repo root, untracked. Decide whether to:
- **commit it** (future-proof, visible to anyone who clones)
- **leave it untracked** (it's a throwaway plan, delete after execution)
- **gitignore it** (keep locally but don't commit)

I'd lean toward just leaving it untracked for now — when execution is complete and you're happy with the result, delete it. If you want it committed as an ADR or permanent record, tell me and I'll add it to the commit.

Ready to execute whenever you say go.
